> Even races other than humans have two genders, male and female, exactly the same as humans. Also consider the differences in appearance between men and women, as well as the differences in the division of roles in society, to be equivalent to those of humans.

### 《 **Name** 》

- It's not convenient to enjoy the fictional world of Forcelia if they're always called by the player's name or just _hey.... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00372))_
- Naturally, a character must have a name. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00372))_
- Therefore, a character should be given a name that is appropriate for Forcelia. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00373))_
- Therefore, a character should be given a name that is appropriate for Forcelia. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00373))_
- Purely Japanese or Chinese names are not suitable. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00374))_
- When deciding on a name for a character, it is usually best to stick with a basic western-style name. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00374))_
- After all, the names of most people in _Alecrast_ , the setting of _Sword World RPG_ , are like that. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00374))_
- After all, the names of most people in _Alecrast_ , the setting of _Sword World RPG_ , are like that. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00374))_
- Alternatively, it's acceptable to simply decide on a first name and leave the last unspecified, or to use a nickname or alias, as is often used by thieves. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00375))_
- In _Alecrast_ , first name, then last name are usually written in that order. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00375))_
- In rare cases, a middle name may also be added between the first and last names. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00375))_
- In _Alecrast_ , first name, then last name are usually written in that order. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00375))_

### **Example:**

- Since they're a human, their age will be around 15~30 years old. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00377))_
- This should be understood as _purchasing skills with experience points_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00378))_
- character comes from the _mercenary_ background, so being too young is not an option. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00379))_
- Note, however, that experience points are spent at each level attained. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00380))_
- It is also possible to increase the level of skills that have already been acquired. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00380))_
- You may even acquire 2 or more levels at a time, so long as there are enough experience points to spend. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00380))_
- Although there are many women who fight in Forcelia, mercenaries will probably still have a higher percentage of men. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00381))_
- Either gender is acceptable. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00381))_
- In this section, we'll use _Ducard Sample_ `Ⅱ` (the second), though it's not a very sensible name. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00382))_
- You may follow your own sensibilities, or you may borrow the name of a real westerner. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00382))_
- Only adventurer skills can be acquired or increased using experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00384))_
- General skills cannot be acquired or increased with experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00384))_

> For example, if you do not have the ranger skill and you want to acquire 2 levels in ranger at once, you must spend 500 + 1000 for a total of 1500 experience points.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00382))_

> As for the name, it’s up to the player to decide on a name. You may follow your own sensibilities, or you may borrow the name of a real westerner. In this section, we'll use _Ducard Sample_ `Ⅱ` (the second), though it's not a very sensible name.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00383))_

> If you have enough experience points, you can acquire multiple skills.

### **1.4.5**

### **Character Growth via**

- In addition, starting experience points do not have to be used up. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00387))_
- Leaving them in place will give you an advantage in the growth of your character after the game. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00387))_
- Leaving them in place will give you an advantage in the growth of your character after the game. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00387))_

### **Starting Experience Points**

- In addition to this, starting experience points can also be used to acquire new skills or increase a character's skills. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00389))_
- A character has already acquired certain skills (starting skills) according to Table 1-5: Backgrounds or Table 1-6: Starting Experience by Race. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00389))_

### **Example:**

- In addition, we can even acquire 2 levels in thief at once (2500-1000-1500= _0_ remaining). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00391))_
- Ducard II has 2500 experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00391))_
- With these, for example, we could acquire 1 level in sorcerer (25002000= _500_ remaining) or increase fighter to 2 levels (2500-1500= _1000_ remaining). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00391))_
- With these, for example, we could acquire 1 level in sorcerer (25002000= _500_ remaining) or increase fighter to 2 levels (2500-1500= _1000_ remaining). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00391))_
- However, the skills that can be acquired or increased are limited to those of adventurer skills, and general skills _cannot_ be acquired or increased. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00392))_
- However, the skills that can be acquired or increased are limited to those of adventurer skills, and general skills _cannot_ be acquired or increased. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00392))_
- This leaves us 500 points, which we’ll save for future use. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00394))_
- For example, if you want to acquire 1 level in ranger, you can do so by subtracting 500 from your starting experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00395))_
- For example, if you want to acquire 1 level in ranger, you can do so by subtracting 500 from your starting experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00395))_
- When you have the sorcerer skill, there's a few things to be careful of. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00399))_
- You may acquire sorcerer only, but it's better to avoid this, as it will put you at a disadvantage during the game. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00399))_
- This skill is closely related to the sage skill and requires fewer experience points when acquiring both. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00399))_
- You may acquire sorcerer only, but it's better to avoid this, as it will put you at a disadvantage during the game. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00399))_
- The sage skill is useful enough on its own. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00400))_
- Of course, having the sage skill does not mean that you're required to have the sorcerer skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00400))_
- It is important to note that _it is against the rules for a character who has neither the sorcerer nor sage skills to acquire only the sorcerer skill for 1500 points._ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00401))_
- It is important to note that _it is against the rules for a character who has neither the sorcerer nor sage skills to acquire only the sorcerer skill for 1500 points._ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00401))_
- Table 1-7: Experience Points by Skill (at Character Creation) is almost identical to a segment of Table 8-1: Experience Points by Skill, which is used for character growth as explained in chapter 8. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00402))_
- As this shows, the use of starting experience points is exactly the same as the use of experience points during character growth, so it might be a good idea to read the explanation in chapter 8 first. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00403))_

### **Required Strength**

- Even swords of the same shape are sold in a variety of sizes to match the physical strength of the person using it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00412))_
- For an adventurer, the first step to survival is to choose a weapon most suited to their strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00413))_
- Basically, the most suitable weapon is one whose _required strength equals the character's strength_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00414))_
- As long as you choose that, you are guaranteed the same attack capability, even if you use a different type of weapon. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00414))_
- Think of a sword, ax, or spear with a required strength of 14 as the same in terms of game value. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00414))_
- Therefore, it’s a good idea to choose one based on the image of your character. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00414))_
- Therefore, it’s a good idea to choose one based on the image of your character. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00414))_
- However, most weapons have a limited range of required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00415))_
- However, most weapons have a limited range of required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00415))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00409, source-range-870a16bc-00414))_

> Basically, the most suitable weapon is one whose _required strength equals the character's strength_ . As long as you choose that, you are guaranteed the same attack capability, even if you use a different type of weapon. ### **One-Handed and TwoHanded Weapons**

- A two-handed weapon will deal more damage to an opponent than a onehanded weapon of the same required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00417))_
- To determine whether a weapon is one-handed or two-handed, see the usage column on Table 1-8: Weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00418))_
- 1~2H means the weapon can be wielded with one _or_ both hands. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00418))_
- 1~2H means the weapon can be wielded with one _or_ both hands. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00418))_
- _Throwable_ weapons are basically used in melee, although they can be thrown when the need arises. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00421))_
- Note that these cannot be used in melee. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00421))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00414))_

> Basically, the most suitable weapon is one whose _required strength equals the character's strength_ . As long as you choose that, you are guaranteed the same attack capability, even if you use a different type of weapon. Think of a sword, ax, or spear with a required strength of 14 as the same in terms of game value. Therefore, it’s a good idea to choose one based on the image of your character.

> In the case of shields, there are only two types: **small shields** (required strength 1) and **large shields** (required strength 13). If a character's strength is 13 or greater, a large shield can be chosen. However, in terms of increasing a character's defensive ability, the effects of small and large shields are basically the same in this game. Therefore, it is safe to choose the less expensive small shield (Table 1-10: Shields).

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00426))_

### **Price Calculation**

- There you will find expressions like **x20+40** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00430))_
- Weapons and armor have different prices for the same shape, depending on the required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00430))_
- The required strength is applied to this formula to find the actual price. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00430))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00430))_

> Weapons and armor have different prices for the same shape, depending on the required strength. For the prices of weapons and armor, please see the **price calculation** column of the weapons table. There you will find expressions like **x20+40** . The required strength is applied to this formula to find the actual price. For example, for a broadsword with a required strength of 14, the price would be:

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00431))_

> 14 (required strength) x 20 + 40 = **320.** Prices are in gamels ( **G** ).

### **1.4.7**

### **Restrictions on Weapons and Armor Based on Skill**

- The general rule is to _choose one whose required strength equals the character's strength_ , but a character may be substantially limited by the skills they have. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00434))_
- For example, if one with the shaman skill wears metal armor, they cannot use spirit magic, which is the primary ability of the shaman skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00434))_
- In this case, it wouldn’t make sense to have the shaman skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00434))_
- For example, if one with the shaman skill wears metal armor, they cannot use spirit magic, which is the primary ability of the shaman skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00434))_
- The general rule is to _choose one whose required strength equals the character's strength_ , but a character may be substantially limited by the skills they have. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00434))_
- The following are guidelines for purchasing weapons and armor based on which skills a character has. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00435))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00435))_

> The following are guidelines for purchasing weapons and armor based on which skills a character has. For characters with multiple skills, look at **Table 1-9: Armor**

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00436))_

| **Table 1-9: Armor** |
| --- |
| Armor Name |

> For weapons, there are no restrictions. You should buy a weapon whose required strength is the same as your own strength. For armor, _metal armor other than ring mail_ may _not_ be used, regardless of the character's strength or required strength. Choose non-metal armor or ring mail.

### **[ Fighter** ］

- Weapons and armor that have a required strength less than your own strength will make your character's attack and defense incomplete, and weapons and armor with a required strength greater than your own strength cannot be handled. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00444))_
- The basic idea is to choose weapons and armor whose required strength is the same as your own strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00444))_

### **[ Thief ]**

- For armor, _metal armor other than ring mail_ may _not_ be used, regardless of the character's strength or required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00447))_
- Specifically, weapons and armor that require up to _half your strength (rounded up)_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00447))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00444))_

> There are no restrictions. The basic idea is to choose weapons and armor whose required strength is the same as your own strength. Weapons and armor that have a required strength less than your own strength will make your character's attack and defense incomplete, and weapons and armor with a required strength greater than your own strength cannot be handled.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00447))_

> If you want to take advantage of your thief skill, your weapons _and_ armor must be _light_ .

### **[ Sorcerer ]**

- Either of these should be the one with the greatest required strength within the character's strength range. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00450))_
- Also, you should not buy a shield. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00450))_
- Either of these should be the one with the greatest required strength within the character's strength range. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00450))_
- This is essential when using **ancient magic** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00451))_
- For weapons, be sure to purchase a **mage’s staff** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00451))_

> If you want to take advantage of your thief skill, your weapons _and_ armor must be _light_ . Specifically, weapons and armor that require up to _half your strength (rounded up)_ . For armor, _metal armor other than ring mail_ may _not_ be used, regardless of the character's strength or required strength. Choose non-metal armor or ring mail.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00451))_

> If the character's strength is 10 or less, it is appropriate to buy one with the same required strength as the character's strength; if the character's strength is 10 or greater, it is appropriate to buy one with a required strength of 10.

### **[ Shaman ]**

- Also, you should not buy a shield. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00453))_
- Of these, the one with the greatest required strength within the character's strength range should be chosen. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00453))_
- If you want to make use of your shaman skill, your armor is limited to _cloth_ , _soft leather_ , or _hard leather_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00453))_
- If you want to make use of your shaman skill, your armor is limited to _cloth_ , _soft leather_ , or _hard leather_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00453))_
- Of these, the one with the greatest required strength within the character's strength range should be chosen. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00453))_
- It is fundamental to match your strength to the required strength of the weapon. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00454))_

### **[ Sage, Bard, Priest ]**

- These skills have no weapon or armor restrictions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00456))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00454, source-range-870a16bc-00458))_

> When choosing a weapon, since you’re not buying a shield, it's a good idea to choose a _two-handed_ weapon if at all possible. It is fundamental to match your strength to the required strength of the weapon. Ducard II has 2 levels in the fighter skill and 1 level in the ranger skill. There are no restrictions on either skill when choosing a weapon. Ranger has an advantage in projectiles, but this is consequently irrelevant because of the higher level of the _can use any weapon_ fighter.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00456))_

> If a character has acquired only these skills, the basic idea is to choose weapons and armor whose required strength is the same as their own strength.

### **Example:**

- Ranger has an advantage in projectiles, but this is consequently irrelevant because of the higher level of the _can use any weapon_ fighter. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00458))_
- Ducard II has 2 levels in the fighter skill and 1 level in the ranger skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00458))_
- There are no restrictions on either skill when choosing a weapon. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00458))_
- Ranger has an advantage in projectiles, but this is consequently irrelevant because of the higher level of the _can use any weapon_ fighter. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00458))_
- Here we have chosen the most typical weapon, a **broadsword** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00459))_
- The required strength of a broadsword is 8~16, so a broadsword with a required strength of 14 certainly exists. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00459))_
- Our character's strength is 14, so we can buy armor with a required strength of 7. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00461))_
- If we have a large shield, the ranger skill will be limited, so our choice here is limited to a **small shield** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00464))_
- Since a broadsword is a _one-handed_ weapon, this character can _also_ hold a shield. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00464))_
- If we have a large shield, the ranger skill will be limited, so our choice here is limited to a **small shield** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00464))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00458))_

> Ducard II has 2 levels in the fighter skill and 1 level in the ranger skill. There are no restrictions on either skill when choosing a weapon. Ranger has an advantage in projectiles, but this is consequently irrelevant because of the higher level of the _can use any weapon_ fighter.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00459))_

> So, when choosing a weapon, we'll choose one that has a required strength equal to the character's strength (14).

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00459))_

> So, when choosing a weapon, we'll choose one that has a required strength equal to the character's strength (14). Here we have chosen the most typical weapon, a **broadsword** . The required strength of a broadsword is 8~16, so a broadsword with a required strength of 14 certainly exists.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00460))_

### **1.4.8**

### **Purchasing Other Equipment**

- If you only have weapons and armor, you will not be able to go on an adventure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00468))_
- It won’t be an adventure if you forget your ropes and torches. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00468))_
- If you only have weapons and armor, you will not be able to go on an adventure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00468))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00469))_

> For equipment other than weapons and armor, see Table 1-11: Equipment.

### **[ Weight Limits ]**

- Many games set some kind of _weight_ on weapons, armor and equipment, limiting the amount of goods a character can carry. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00471))_
- Instead, please use your _common sense_ to make decisions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00471))_
- However, _Sword World RPG_ does not do this. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00471))_
- Many games set some kind of _weight_ on weapons, armor and equipment, limiting the amount of goods a character can carry. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00471))_
- This would be a matter of course even if the rules do not stipulate it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00472))_
- A character cannot have an infinite number of things. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00472))_
- It is impossible to hold 1000 torches or 1 kilometer of rope. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00472))_

### **Example:**

- Ducard II has 580 gamels left. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00474))_
- Without it, it'll be difficult to carry luggage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00475))_
- Since we’re going on an adventure, a water bag (20G) is a must. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00475))_
- We should also have a belt pouch (15G) for small items and money. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00475))_
- First, we'll need a rucksack (50G). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00475))_
- If he has friends, though, it would be nice if we could pool our money together to buy it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00476))_
- If we're planning to sleep outdoors, we'll need a mantle (40G) and a blanket (50G). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00476))_
- Tableware (12G) and cooking utensils (50G) will also be needed for camping. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00476))_
- A tent is too expensive and we don't need something large enough for five people. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00476))_
- We’ll also need torches (6 for 5 G). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00477))_
- Without that, a fire cannot be started. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00477))_
- For a lighting tool, we’ll first need a tinderbox (20G). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00477))_
- A lantern would be brighter, but money is tight and we can't afford that luxury. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00477))_
- It may be useful at some point. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00479))_
- Ducard II's ranger skill includes the _map creation_ ability. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00479))_
- The thieves' tools are for a thief and the musical instruments are for a bard, so they're not necessary for Ducard II. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00479))_
- So far, 277 gamels have been spent on purchases, with 303 gamels remaining. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00480))_
- We won’t buy preserved food yet. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00481))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00483))_

> **Table 1-11: Equipment**

### **1.4.9**

### **Filling Out the Character Sheet**

- The **Character Sheet** that accompanies this book is designed to record and list various character details. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00489))_
- This will help keep the game running smoothly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00489))_

### **[** ① **Player Name ]**

- The player should write their own name in the player name field. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00493))_

### **[** ② **Race ]**

- The first decision in character creation is race. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00495))_
- Once you've decided, please write it here. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00495))_

### **[** ③ **Sub-Ability Scores]**

- Once race is chosen, sub-ability scores can be determined. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00497))_

### **[** ④ **Ability Scores ]**

- Once sub-ability scores are determined, ability scores are calculated by addition. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00499))_

### **[** ⑤ **Ability Bonuses ]**

- A bonus is an ability score divided by 6 (rounded down). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00501))_
- Once ability scores have been calculated, the next step is the ability bonuses. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00501))_

### **[** ⑥ **Origin ]**

- If the character was not raised in a human society, there is no need to write anything in this field. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00503))_
- Once it's been decided, write it in the _origin_ field. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00503))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00503))_

> If the character is a human or a halfelf raised by a human, Table 1-5: Backgrounds must be used to determine their origin.

### **[** ⑦ **Starting Experience Points ]**

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00503))_

> If the character is a human or a halfelf raised by a human, Table 1-5: Backgrounds must be used to determine their origin. Once it's been decided, write it in the _origin_ field. If the character was not raised in a human society, there is no need to write anything in this field.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00505))_

> Please write down starting experience points as determined by Table 1-5: Backgrounds or Table 1-6: Starting Experience by Race in the _experience points_ field.

### **[** ⑧ **Starting Skills ]**

### **[** ⑨ **Money ]**

- Table 1-5: Backgrounds or Table 1-6: Starting Experience by Race determine the amount of money (2D x _modifier_ ) that you have. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00509))_

### **[** ⑩ **Gender ]**

- Once the process has been completed up to this point, you should have developed an image of the character. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00511))_
- It should now be easier to decide on gender and age, plus the character's name. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00511))_

### **[** ⑪ **Age ]**

### **[** ⑫ **Character Name ]**

### **[** ⑬ **Increase Skills ]**

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00518))_

> Starting experience points can be used to acquire new adventurer skills or to increase any skills already acquired (Table 1-7: Experience Points by Skill).

### **[** ⑭ **Adventurer Level ]**

- Once you’ve acquired your final skills (starting skill and growth via starting experience points), you can determine your adventurer level. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00521))_
- Adventurer level is equal to the highest adventurer skill level. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00522))_

### **[** ⑮ **Purchase Equipment]**

- Write down the items you purchased then rewrite your money, reducing by the amount of money you spent (Table 1-11: Equipment). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00524))_
- Write down the items you purchased then rewrite your money, reducing by the amount of money you spent (Table 1-11: Equipment). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00524))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00524))_

> Use your money to purchase equipment. Write down the items you purchased then rewrite your money, reducing by the amount of money you spent (Table 1-11: Equipment).

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00525))_

> When you purchase weapons, shields, and armor, write them in the appropriate fields (Table 1-8: Weapons, Table 1-9: Armor, and Table 1-10: Shields).

### **[** ⑯ **Other Sections ]**

- However, to fill in anything other than what’s explained here, you must understand the rules a bit more in detail. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00527))_
- So for now, this is the end of the instructions for filling out the sheet. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00527))_
- The character sheet is not completely filled out yet. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00527))_
- The other sections will be explained as necessary in the coming rules. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00528))_

## Chapter 2: Success Rolls

## 2.3 Checks

### **Baseline Scores**

### **2.1**

- _skill level_ , and their dexterity bonus is used for _ability bonus_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00540))_
- A **baseline score** is a number that indicates how competent or skilled a character is at a given activity. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00541))_
- Also, if the character is trained or experienced as a warrior, they’ll be even more likely to hit their target. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00542))_
- For example, if you want to hit an enemy with your sword, it is important to have dexterity to wield it accurately. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00542))_

### **Target Scores**

### **2.2**

- The difficulty of hitting a target with the same weapon is much greater when your opponent is a seasoned warrior, than when he is an amateur with no fighting experience. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00553))_
- However, the success of an action is not only related to the ability of the person attempting it, but also to the difficulty of the action itself. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00553))_
- The baseline score is a number that represents a character's ability. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00553))_
- However, the success of an action is not only related to the ability of the person attempting it, but also to the difficulty of the action itself. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00553))_
- This is represented by a **target score** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00554))_
- This is because it is the game master who sets the target scores, not the players. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00555))_

### **Checks**

### **2.3**

- Once the baseline score and target score are determined, a success roll **check** can finally be made. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00558))_

### **Example:**

- His baseline score for attacks is _4_ and the target score for hitting a goblin is _10_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00560))_
- In other words, we must roll two dice, add 4 to the result, and if the total is _10 or more_ , we’ll hit the target. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00560))_
- The result is called the **final score** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00561))_
- This final score should be _equal to or greater than_ the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00561))_
- The result is called the **final score** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00561))_
- It also lists the ability scores associated with those actions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00564))_
- Skills Other Than Combat and Magic to see which skills can be used for the baseline score of any given action. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00564))_
- If a character does not have any skills related to an action, the baseline score is **0** regardless of their ability scores. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00565))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00560))_

## 2.5 Difficulty Checks

- On a success roll, if a player rolls a **12** , i.e., both dice roll 6 ( **double sixes** ), the action is a _success_ , no matter how low the baseline score nor how high the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00577))_
- Basically, automatic success and automatic failure are _not_ definitive success or definitive failure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00578))_
- It simply means that you succeeded or failed, _not_ that you succeeded very _well_ or failed very _badly_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00578))_
- It simply means that you succeeded or failed, _not_ that you succeeded very _well_ or failed very _badly_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00578))_

### **Bonuses and Penalties**

### **2.4**

- This is to replicate any favorable or unfavorable circumstances when a character is about to perform an action. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00582))_
- A success roll may be subject to modifiers. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00582))_
- In such cases, the game master applies a **bonus** or **penalty** to the success roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00583))_
- When such bonuses or penalties are applied, the baseline score will go up or down. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00584))_
- A player must also calculate these before making a success roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00584))_
- A player must also calculate these before making a success roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00584))_
- For example, if the person you’re fighting is falling, their movements will be restricted and your attacks will be easier to land. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00585))_
- For example, if the person you’re fighting is falling, their movements will be restricted and your attacks will be easier to land. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00585))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00584))_

> When such bonuses or penalties are applied, the baseline score will go up or down. A player must also calculate these before making a success roll.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00585))_

> On the other hand, if you’re fighting in the dark, then unless your character has darkvision, you cannot see your opponent, so it is not so easy to hit him.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00585))_

> For example, if the person you’re fighting is falling, their movements will be restricted and your attacks will be easier to land. On the other hand, if you’re fighting in the dark, then unless your character has darkvision, you cannot see your opponent, so it is not so easy to hit him.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00586))_

> final score {baseline score + 2D ± (bonus, penalty)} ≥ target score → **action is successful**

### **2.5.1 Difficulty**

- **Difficulty** is a score determined by the game master based on the difficulty of the actions the character attempts. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00610))_
- What makes this different from a target score is that this is _not_ the direct number the player is aiming for. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00610))_

### **2.5.2**

### **How to Make a Difficulty Check**

- This roll is then added to the _difficulty_ and used as the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00613))_
- To make a **difficulty check** , the game master also rolls the dice (2D). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00613))_
- The game master does not reveal their roll nor the target score that results from their calculation, to the players. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00613))_
- Whether the base difficulty is revealed or not depends on the situation. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00613))_
- This roll is then added to the _difficulty_ and used as the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00613))_
- The players' dice rolls are otherwise the same. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00614))_
- They will be told the baseline score, roll 2D, and modify it with any bonuses or penalties to find a final score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00614))_
- If there is a tie or if the final score is greater, the action is a _success_ ; if it is less, the action is a _failure_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00615))_
- The game master then takes the final score and compares it to the target score (difficulty + 2D) that they have secretly set. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00615))_
- The game master then takes the final score and compares it to the target score (difficulty + 2D) that they have secretly set. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00615))_

- They must only answer as much as the character can tell from their successes and failures. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00617))_

### **2.5.3**

### **Analogy of Success and Failure**

- That is precisely the reason why we use difficulty checks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00623))_
- If a success roll is made for a difficulty check, the success or failure of the action is strictly unknown to the player. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00623))_
- However, a player is given only _one_ criteria to determine whether the success roll they made for a difficulty check is a success or a failure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00624))_
- However, a player is given only _one_ criteria to determine whether the success roll they made for a difficulty check is a success or a failure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00624))_
- That is: the dice that the players themselves have rolled. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00625))_
- Therefore, if after examining the door, you do not find a trap, the risk is quite low even if you assume that there really are no traps. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00626))_
- Therefore, if after examining the door, you do not find a trap, the risk is quite low even if you assume that there really are no traps. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00626))_
- So, even if you examine the door and do not find any traps, you should be skeptical of the results. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00627))_
- In particular, when a player rolls either of the two extremes, _double sixes_ or _double ones_ , it means _automatic success_ or _automatic failure_ , so that the player can clearly see whether the success roll is successful or not. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00628))_
- In particular, when a player rolls either of the two extremes, _double sixes_ or _double ones_ , it means _automatic success_ or _automatic failure_ , so that the player can clearly see whether the success roll is successful or not. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00628))_
- A player's dice roll should be interpreted as an expression of _confidence_ in the character's actions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00629))_
- A high roll indicates a high level of confidence, while a low roll indicates a lack of confidence in what they've done. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00629))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00624))_

> However, a player is given only _one_ criteria to determine whether the success roll they made for a difficulty check is a success or a failure.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00626))_

> If the roll is as high as 10 or 11, the success roll is considered to have a very high chance of success.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00626))_

> If the roll is as high as 10 or 11, the success roll is considered to have a very high chance of success. Therefore, if after examining the door, you do not find a trap, the risk is quite low even if you assume that there really are no traps.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00627))_

> If the roll is low, such as 3 or 4, then there's a high chance that the success roll has failed.

### **Caution**

- It has been noted before that _double sixes_ and _double ones_ are _automatic_ successes or failures and do not imply _definitive_ successes or failures. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00631))_
- It has been noted before that _double sixes_ and _double ones_ are _automatic_ successes or failures and do not imply _definitive_ successes or failures. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00631))_
- Which means I can say for sure “There are definitely no traps on this door”. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00632))_
- It is especially bad to misinterpret an automatic failure as a _definitive_ failure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00632))_
- For example, if a player is trying to find a trap on a door and has rolled double ones, the game master will of course tell the player _you didn't find any traps_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00632))_
- When the player hears this, if they reply like the following, it's a prime example of this misinterpretation: _Oh, I screwed up big time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00632))_
- Which means I can say for sure “There are definitely no traps on this door”. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00632))_
- For example, if a player is trying to find a trap on a door and has rolled double ones, the game master will of course tell the player _you didn't find any traps_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00632))_

## 2.7 Resistance Rolls

### **2.6**

### **Success Rolls Using Adventurer Level**

- However, in some cases, _adventurer level_ , rather than skill level, is used to calculate the baseline score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00639))_
- For a success roll, the baseline score is usually calculated as _relevant skill level + ability bonus_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00639))_
- For actions that _anyone_ would be able to perform if they became proficient in adventuring, the baseline score for a success roll is _adventurer level + ability bonus_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00640))_
- Actions that fit into this category are explained in _6.6: Common Adventurer Abilities_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00641))_
- In addition, the following _2.7: Resistance Rolls_ are also a check that uses adventurer level in its baseline score calculation. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00641))_
- When a character with adventurer level 0 (a character with no adventurer skills) makes these checks, the baseline score is _0_ regardless of their ability scores. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00642))_

### **2.7**

### **Resistance Rolls**

- A success roll made using _adventurer level + life force bonus_ as the baseline score is specifically referred to as a **life force resistance roll** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00645))_
- Similarly, a success roll made using _adventurer level + mental power bonus_ as the baseline score is specifically called a **mental power resistance roll** , and the two are collectively called simply **resistance rolls** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00645))_
- The reason for the name change and the distinction between the two in particular, is that they are frequently performed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00646))_
- Therefore, these baseline scores are also defined by the terms **life force resistance** and **mental power resistance** , among others. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00647))_
- Therefore, these baseline scores are also defined by the terms **life force resistance** and **mental power resistance** , among others. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00647))_
- Life force resistance rolls are made to avoid or reduce the effects of physical dangers such as poison, while mental power resistance rolls are made to avoid or reduce the effects of magic or curses. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00648))_
- Life force resistance rolls are made to avoid or reduce the effects of physical dangers such as poison, while mental power resistance rolls are made to avoid or reduce the effects of magic or curses. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00648))_
- Checks against special attacks by monsters that are magical in nature are also made with mental power resistance rolls. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00649))_
- The _toxicity score_ of ghoul poison is 10, which is the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00650))_
- Our 2D roll is 5, so with 5+5= _10_ , he escapes paralysis, if barely. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00650))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00648))_

> Life force resistance rolls are made to avoid or reduce the effects of physical dangers such as poison, while mental power resistance rolls are made to avoid or reduce the effects of magic or curses.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00649))_

> In particular, all checks against magic, even if it causes physical damage, are made with mental power resistance rolls.

### **Example 2:**

- Since Ducard II has an adventurer level of 2 and a +2 mental power bonus, the baseline score of his mental power resistance roll (mental power resistance power) is _4_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00652))_
- While touring an underground cave on an adventure, he wanders into a mysterious room where all four walls are painted in extreme colors. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00652))_
- The richly colored patterns in the room begin to move strangely, and Ducard II is struck by a strong sense of drowsiness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00653))_
- In fact, there is a magic trap set in this room that will put anyone who enters it into a deep sleep. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00653))_
- Normally, this would never succeed, but our 2D roll is _12_ ! _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00653))_
- Write the baseline scores of each resistance roll here . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00655))_
- The two resistance rolls are frequently performed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00655))_
- Therefore, it’s advisable to make a note of the baseline score ( **resistance** ) so that it is readily available. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00655))_
- The Character Sheet provides a field for this purpose. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00655))_
- Therefore, it’s advisable to make a note of the baseline score ( **resistance** ) so that it is readily available. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00655))_

## Chapter 3: Combat Rounds

## 3.1 Action Announcements

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00668))_

> same group (e.g., a1, a2, a3 within group A) may be determined freely and can also be changed from round to round.

### **3.1.1**

### **Order of Action Announcements**

- The groups with the higher intelligence can decide on their actions later than the others. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00671))_
- If there are three or more groups instead of two, the order of action announcements is determined in the same way. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00671))_
- Each round begins with an announcement of what your character intends to do. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00672))_
- In combat, there are basically two opposing groups. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00673))_
- **Action announcements** are made by each group. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00673))_
- If there's an enemy in front of you that’s attacking you, attacking another enemy several meters away is not possible, just by common sense. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00674))_
- Casting a spell while running at full speed is prohibited by the rules. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00674))_
- These impossible actions cannot be declared, as a matter of course. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00674))_
- Suppose there are three characters, a1, a2, and a3 in **group A** and two characters, b1 and b2 in **group B** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00675))_
- In this case, the order of action announcements is **A → B** or **B → A** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00675))_
- If _a2_ is the one with the highest intelligence between groups A and B as mentioned, then the announcements are made in the order of _B → A_ , regardless of the intelligence of the other characters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00676))_
- Those who announce their actions later- the ones with highest representative intelligence --will have a significant advantage because they can announce their own actions _after_ hearing their opponents' announcements. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00676))_
- The group with the _highest_ intelligence among all the characters (including monsters, etc.) participating in combat will announce their action _later_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00676))_
- Which of the two will make their action announcements first is determined based on _intelligence_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00676))_
- If _a2_ is the one with the highest intelligence between groups A and B as mentioned, then the announcements are made in the order of _B → A_ , regardless of the intelligence of the other characters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00676))_
- Those who announce their actions later- the ones with highest representative intelligence --will have a significant advantage because they can announce their own actions _after_ hearing their opponents' announcements. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00676))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00676))_

> Which of the two will make their action announcements first is determined based on _intelligence_ . The group with the _highest_ intelligence among all the characters (including monsters, etc.) participating in combat will announce their action _later_ . If _a2_ is the one with the highest intelligence between groups A and B as mentioned, then the announcements are made in the order of _B → A_ , regardless of the intelligence of the other characters. Those who announce their actions later- the o

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00674))_

> In an action announcement, you cannot declare something impossible.

### **3.1.2**

### **Player Action Announcements**

- Instead of _attack with my weapon_ , they should say _attack the rightmost goblin with my sword_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00680))_
- If the game master asks a question, be sure to answer it clearly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00682))_
- You must be specific in this way. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00682))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00679))_

> A player must be specific when announcing their action.

### **3.1.3**

### **NPC and Monster Action Announcements**

- If those are _also_ the same, then compare the _third_ highest. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00685))_
- If the two groups are _equal_ in intelligence (i.e., the highest intelligence in each group is the same), compare the _second_ highest in each group. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00685))_
- If those are _also_ the same, then compare the _third_ highest. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00685))_
- Specific action announcements may not be made at this time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00686))_
- However, the players should only act from their _characters’_ point of view. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00690))_
- This is the difference in position between the players and the game master. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00690))_
- The game master, as the person who manages the game, must know the actions of all the characters and monsters that appear. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00690))_
- When you see it for the first time, you won't know how a monster you don't recognize will act, and when an enemy tries to cast a spell you don't recognize, you won't know what that spell does. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00690))_
- So, if a _character_ cannot predict something, the _player_ cannot know it either. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00690))_
- However, the players should only act from their _characters’_ point of view. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00690))_
- Of course, if a character has a wealth of knowledge, or if they’re an advanced spellcaster, they _might_ know such things. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00691))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00686))_

> When NPCs, monsters, etc. announce their actions, it’s done by the game master who manages them. Specific action announcements may not be made at this time.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00687))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00690))_

> You may only declare, _I'm preparing to cast a spell_ , and not give out the specific name of the spell.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00690))_

## 3.3 Action Canceling

### **Action Order**

### **3.2**

- Therefore, the goblin will act before Ducard II. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00696))_
- Therefore, the goblin will act before Ducard II. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00696))_
- Once action announcements are complete, it is time to resolve both friends’ and foes’ actions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00697))_
- Combat will then actually take place. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00697))_
- Combat will then actually take place. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00697))_
- Ducard II cannot carry out his intended attack on the goblin. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00698))_
- Each of their action announcements are _attack the opponent (with a weapon)_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00698))_
- Therefore, it is possible to have a mixture of friends and foes, as in _a1 → b1 → a2_ ....... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00699))_
- This is done in order of _agility_ , friend and foe alike. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00699))_
- Therefore, it is possible to have a mixture of friends and foes, as in _a1 → b1 → a2_ ....... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00699))_
- If, as a result of the action of someone with _higher_ agility, someone with _lower_ agility is _unable to perform their intended action_ , it means that their action is _no longer possible_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00700))_
- If, as a result of the action of someone with _higher_ agility, someone with _lower_ agility is _unable to perform their intended action_ , it means that their action is _no longer possible_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00700))_
- If two have exactly the _same_ agility, their actions are considered to be **simultaneous** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00702))_
- Suppose that, as in the first example, IIDash is still hit badly enough to fall down as a result of the goblin's attack check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00704))_
- However, IIDash’s action order = agility is the _same_ as the goblin's, so he would've been slashing at the goblin _at the same time_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00704))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00701))_

> Suppose that Ducard II has an agility of 13, which is equal to that of a goblin (let's call him _IIDash_ from now on, since he is a bit faster). In this case, both attacks are considered to be carried out simultaneously. During the course of the game, each attack check is made in the order of _goblin → IIDash_ (or vice versa). Otherwise, it would be difficult to progress the game.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00703))_

> For convenience, they are resolved in order, but even if you’re attacked by an enemy of the _same_ agility and fall down, you can _still_ perform your intended actions.

- Ducard II is fighting a goblin. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00706))_

### **Action Canceling**

### **3.3**

- you intended to cast a spell but were _blocked_ from chanting halfway through. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00709))_
- If you fall down yourself early in the order, you will not be able to act. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00710))_
- For example, if an enemy that you intended to attack falls down due to the attack of a fellow player, no further attack is necessary. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00710))_
- A character may **cancel** and _not_ perform their announced action. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00710))_
- For example, if an enemy that you intended to attack falls down due to the attack of a fellow player, no further attack is necessary. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00710))_
- The choice is basically this: either continue with the announced action or cancel it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00711))_

### **3.3.1**

- Or, in the middle of a round, your companion may ask you to help out by giving money or goods. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00713))_

### **When It’s Possible to Change Attack Targets**

- In such a case, a character may cancel their intended action. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00715))_
- However, there is an exception to this principle. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00716))_
- As mentioned earlier, a character has only two choices: carry out their action as is or cancel it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00716))_
- As mentioned earlier, a character has only two choices: carry out their action as is or cancel it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00716))_
- However, characters cannot _switch_ their actions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00717))_
- You also cannot switch from _magic_ to a _weapon_ attack because It's when you're engaged with several enemies and some of them are trying to **withdraw (flee)** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00717))_
- You also cannot switch from _magic_ to a _weapon_ attack because It's when you're engaged with several enemies and some of them are trying to **withdraw (flee)** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00717))_
- IIDash _can_ make an attack check against the goblin. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00718))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00717))_

> Just because a companion has knocked down an enemy in front of you does _not_ mean that you can attack a _different_ enemy.

### **3.2.1**

### **Delaying an Action**

- Characters (including NPCs and monsters) do not have to act based on their agility and can instead delay their actions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00721))_
- In other words, they will act only at the very _end_ of the round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00723))_
- In such a case, a character may lower their **action order** to **0** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00723))_
- In other words, they will act only at the very _end_ of the round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00723))_
- In this case, an intended attack on an enemy that is about to withdraw can be changed to another target that you’re engaged with. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00725))_
- If the enemy that's trying to withdraw has a higher agility than you, you _must_ switch to a different target, because according to your action order, the enemy will have escaped. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00726))_
- If the enemy that's trying to withdraw has a higher agility than you, you _must_ switch to a different target, because according to your action order, the enemy will have escaped. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00726))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00722))_

> For example, suppose you know that a fellow wizard can use magic to increase the attack power of your sword. But what if your agility is higher than theirs? The support magic will be applied after you swing your sword, and the magic will be wasted.

## 3.4 Movement and Actions

- You cannot wield weapons or cast spells while running at full speed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00733))_
- However, when **movement** is made, actions are restricted. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00733))_
- Therefore, a character can also move during this time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00733))_
- Therefore, a character can also move during this time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00733))_
- Naturally, you can advance farther, but the restrictions on your actions will also be greater. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00734))_
- At this time, attacks from the enemy are also much more likely to hit. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00734))_
- For monsters, their **movement speed** is set separately from agility, so they can move up to **movement speed x 3** meters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00735))_
- The distance you can go with full movement is **character's agility x 3** meters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00735))_
- Characters have three options for movement: **full movement** , **normal movement** , or **stand still** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00736))_
- Each has different distances that can be traveled and different restrictions on actions that can be taken. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00736))_
- Below are some guidelines on how movement works and which actions can be taken. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00738))_
- For any other actions, please use _common sense_ based on the time frame of 1 round = 10 seconds. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00738))_
- Final decisions are to be made by the game master. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00738))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00730))_

> engaged with attempt to withdraw (flee), even if they have high agility, you can still attack, and you will have a higher chance of hitting than usual.

- If you chose _stand still_ , then _attack with that weapon_ can be read as _attack with that projectile_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00790))_

## 4.3 Projectile Restrictions

### **Weapon and Armor Restrictions**

### **4.1**

- A very frail man, all skin and bones, dressed in heavy plate armor and wielding a long greatsword... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00801))_
- In other words, a character with 14 strength can wield a weapon with a required strength of 14, wield a shield with a required strength of 14, and wear armor with a required strength of 14. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00802))_
- A character's arms are largely divided into weapons, shields, and armor. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00802))_
- All weapons and armor have a required strength to use them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00803))_
- All joking aside, however, if we want to be realistic, weapons and armor used should be limited by a character's strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00803))_
- All joking aside, however, if we want to be realistic, weapons and armor used should be limited by a character's strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00803))_

### **Weapon Combat Skills**

### **4.2**

### 【 **Thief Skill** 】

- A thief also has the ability to fight, but only with _light_ equipment. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00809))_
- Metal armor other than ring mail may _not_ be used, even if the required strength (half of strength) is met. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00809))_
- A thief also has the ability to fight, but only with _light_ equipment. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00809))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00809))_

> Specifically, you can only fight if the weapon or armor you're using requires up to _half (rounded up)_ of your character's strength.

### 【 **Fighter Skill** 】

- The fighter skill is the most basic skill for weapon combat. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00812))_
- weapon + shield + armor or two-handed weapon + armor. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00813))_
- Of course, one-handed weapon + armor, two-handed weapon without armor, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00813))_
- However, you may not hold two weapons in both hands or hold a shield in each hand-- it is fine to do so, but a shield in your dominant hand or a weapon in your non-dominant hand is of no use. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00813))_

### 【 **Ranger Skill** 】

- There is no ability to attack with melee weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00815))_
- 21) - can they fight as well as a fighter when attacking. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00815))_
- The ranger skill cannot be used for defense. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00816))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00813))_

> weapon + shield + armor or two-handed weapon + armor. Of course, one-handed weapon + armor, two-handed weapon without armor, etc. are also possible, and it is acceptable to carry a spare weapon on your waist or back, as long as it is within the bounds of common sense. However, you may not hold two weapons in both hands or hold a shield in each hand-- it is fine to do so, but a shield in your dominant hand or a weapon in your non-dominant hand is of no use.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00815))_

> Weapons that can be used for both melee _and_ throwing, as indicated by _throwable_ on the Weapons table, are useful _only_ when used for throwing, _not_ for melee.

## 4.4 Scores Used in Weapon Combat

- This is represented by **bonus damage** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00828))_
- using the same sized weapon. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00828))_
- **Attack power** , **strike power** , **bonus damage** , **critical target** , **evasion speed** , **defense power** , and **damage reduction** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-00831))_
