## 4.9 Unconscious and Death Checks

### **Applying Damage**

### **4.8**

- If you suffer damage and your life force (points) is reduced, then suffer more damage, your life force (points) will be further reduced from there. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01033))_
- If you suffer damage and your life force (points) is reduced, then suffer more damage, your life force (points) will be further reduced from there. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01033))_
- This reduction is temporary and can be recovered naturally or by magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01034))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01035))_

> After damage is applied, a character (monster) can still act if they have at least _1_ life force (point) remaining.

### **Unconscious and Death Checks**

### **4.9**

- If, as a result of damage (accumulated damage), a character's (monster's) life force (points) becomes _0 or negative_ , that character (monster) will fall **unconscious** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01038))_
- Also, that character (monster) is now in danger of _dying_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01038))_
- It is no longer possible for them to act. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01038))_

### **Example:**

- His life force resistance is _5_ (adventurer level 2, life force bonus +3). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01040))_
- Since Ducard II has a life force of _-4_ , that will also be the penalty. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01040))_
- His result after rolling 2D is _7_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01040))_
- His result after rolling 2D is _7_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01040))_
- If the result of this death check had been 6 or lower, Ducard II would have met an _untimely end_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01041))_
- 5 (life force resistance) + 7 (roll) - 4 (penalty) = **8** , which is greater or equal than 7. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01041))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01041))_

> 5 (life force resistance) + 7 (roll) - 4 (penalty) = **8** , which is greater or equal than 7. Ducard II survives. If the result of this death check had been 6 or lower, Ducard II would have met an _untimely end_ .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01040))_

> Ducard II, with a life force of _-4_ , must make a **death check** .

### **Example:**

- Ducard II, with 2 points of life force left, was hit by Bucky's ax again in the next round, suffering 6 points of damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01043))_
- As a result, Ducard II now has a life force of **-4** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01043))_

### **4.9.2 Death Checks Every Hour**

### **4.9.1**

### **Adventurer Death Checks**

- and have their life force increased by up to at least 1 (positive) point, they will regain consciousness, but if they’re left unconscious, they may die. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01047))_
- Characters with 0 or negative life force remain unconscious, even if they successfully survived a death check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01047))_
- Any adventurer who falls to _0 or negative_ life force is now at risk of _dying_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01048))_
- If the character's life force is _negative_ , the negative amount will be used as a _penalty_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01049))_
- The baseline score is _life force resistance (adventurer level + life force bonus)_ and the target score is _7_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01049))_
- This check is made using a _life force resistance roll_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01049))_
- The target score is still _7_ , and if their life force is still _negative_ , it’s still a _penalty_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01050))_
- At this time, their life force becomes _1_ point. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01052))_
- If the result is _double sixes_ (automatic success), the character will regain consciousness on their own. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01052))_
- life force resistance + 2D - (damage applied beyond life force) < 7 → **death** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01053))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01048))_

> You must roll the dice to determine if your character survives.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01049, source-range-870a16bc-01052))_

> This check is made using a _life force resistance roll_ . The baseline score is _life force resistance (adventurer level + life force bonus)_ and the target score is _7_ . If the character's life force is _negative_ , the negative amount will be used as a _penalty_ . If the result is _double sixes_ (automatic success), the character will regain consciousness on their own. At this time, their life force becomes _1_ point.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01050))_

> Characters with 0 or negative life force must make a death check every _hour_ .

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01049, source-range-870a16bc-01052))_

> This check is made using a _life force resistance roll_ . The baseline score is _life force resistance (adventurer level + life force bonus)_ and the target score is _7_ . If the character's life force is _negative_ , the negative amount will be used as a _penalty_ . If the result is _double sixes_ (automatic success), the character will regain consciousness on their own. At this time, their life force becomes _1_ point.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01051))_

> life force resistance + 2D - (damage applied beyond life force) ≥ 7 → **survival**

### **Example:**

- Ducard II has 18 life force. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01055))_
- Ducard II is hit by Bucky's attack and suffers 16 points of damage, reducing his life force to 2 points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01055))_
- If the result is failure, the character will die. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01057))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01057))_

> If the result is failure, the character will die.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01056))_

> In _one hour_ , they must make another death check.

### **4.9.3**

### **Monster Death Check**

- Subtract the amount of negative life points, if any, from life point resistance, and if the result is _7 or greater_ , the monster is _alive_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01060))_
- A score called the monster’s **life point resistance** is used instead. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01060))_
- If the result is _6 or lower_ , the monster is _dead_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01060))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01060))_

> Monsters with 0 or negative life points must also make a check to see if they live or die.

### **Example:**

- 10-6= **4** , which is _less than 7_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01062))_
- A goblin's life point resistance score is _10_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01062))_
- A goblin, Scarnose, was hit by an adventurer's sword and fell down with _-6_ life points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01062))_
- If it's alive after a death check, it recovers 1 life point every _hour_ , and will regain consciousness when it reaches (positive) _1_ life point. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01063))_
- If it's alive after a death check, it recovers 1 life point every _hour_ , and will regain consciousness when it reaches (positive) _1_ life point. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01063))_
- If the result is any success _other_ than double sixes, the character is alive _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01064))_

## 4.10 Excellent Weapons and Armor

### **4.9.4 Mercy**

- On Table 1-8: Weapons, _maces_ , _clubs_ , and _staves_ are classified as dull weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01068))_
- You must declare mercy _before_ you make a hit check for your attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01068))_
- It _cannot_ be done _after_ a hit or after damage check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01068))_
- However, to do so, their weapon _must_ be a _dull weapon_ (a bludgeoning weapon). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01068))_
- You can also declare mercy when using a _barehanded_ attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01068))_
- However, even if an item is classified as one of these, if you _throw_ it, you _cannot_ declare mercy. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01068))_
- It _cannot_ be done _after_ a hit or after damage check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01068))_
- On any other roll, the character remains _alive_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01069))_
- A character who falls down with 0 or negative life force due to an attack that was declared to be _mercy_ , will _only_ fail their _death check_ if _double ones_ are rolled. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01069))_
- If they fail, then unless they roll double ones, they will continue to remain unconscious. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01070))_
- If successful, then even if it’s not double sixes, they will regain consciousness with 1 point of life force. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01070))_
- If they fail, then unless they roll double ones, they will continue to remain unconscious. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01070))_
- If successful, then even if it’s not double sixes, they will regain consciousness with 1 point of life force. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01070))_
- If left alone, it will _always_ regain consciousness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01071))_
- Whenever an adventurer declares that their attack on a monster is a mercy, that monster will _always_ remain alive. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01071))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01068))_

> If a character does not want to kill their opponent, they can declare **mercy** .

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01069))_

> A character who falls down with 0 or negative life force due to an attack that was declared to be _mercy_ , will _only_ fail their _death check_ if _double ones_ are rolled. On any other roll, the character remains _alive_ .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01070))_

> If left untreated, they must make another death check after _1 hour_ , and again, will _only_ die on double ones.

### **4.10**

### **Excellent Weapons and Armor**

- Some are of high quality made by master craftsmen, some have magical powers, and others are made of unusual materials such as silver or mithril silver. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01074))_
- A variety of weapons and armor can be found in Forcelia. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01074))_
- Some are of high quality made by master craftsmen, some have magical powers, and others are made of unusual materials such as silver or mithril silver. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01074))_
- When a character obtains and uses these weapons and armor, their various scores are modified as follows. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01075))_

### **4.10.1**

### **High-Quality Weapons and Armor**

- These **highquality** weapons and armor are available to adventurers, if they seek them out. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01078))_
- There are people in the world who are called master craftsmen, and they sometimes create weapons and armor that are better than normal. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01078))_
- For example, a broadsword that originally had a required strength 16 could be made with required strength _15_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01079))_
- As a result, this broadsword can be wielded by someone with 15 strength, but _still_ has a strike power of 16. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01079))_
- High-quality weapons and armor are easier to handle, and are made with lower required strength than usual. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01079))_
- For example, a broadsword that originally had a required strength 16 could be made with required strength _15_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01079))_
- For example, if you want a high-quality broadsword, its required strength (size) is naturally limited to _8-16_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01080))_
- It is important to note that such weapons and armor cannot exceed the limits of the weapon or armor itself. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01080))_
- Since there is no such broadsword with a natural required strength of 17, that means there is _no such thing_ as a broadsword with _required strength 16_ and _strike power 17_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01080))_
- Since there is no such broadsword with a natural required strength of 17, that means there is _no such thing_ as a broadsword with _required strength 16_ and _strike power 17_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01080))_
- For example, if you want a high-quality broadsword, its required strength (size) is naturally limited to _8-16_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01080))_
- It is important to note that such weapons and armor cannot exceed the limits of the weapon or armor itself. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01080))_
- It’ll also have no effect on monsters that can only be wounded by magic or magical weapons (such as spirits, beastmen, etc.). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01081))_
- In addition, no matter how high quality it is, _nothing_ can affect its attack power or evasion speed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01081))_
- It’ll also have no effect on monsters that can only be wounded by magic or magical weapons (such as spirits, beastmen, etc.). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01081))_
- You will _not_ see any with a required strength that’s _6 lower than usual_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01082))_
- For reference, a guideline is printed here (Table 4-5: Guideline for Obtaining High-Quality Weapons and Armor). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01083))_
- So long as required strength _is_ up to 5 lower than usual, a character _can_ purchase one at a store. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01083))_
- These high-quality weapons and armor can be expensive. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01084))_
- Their price increases by _20%_ for every _1_ required strength lower than usual. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01084))_
- _2_ lower will be _40%_ more expensive, _3_ lower will be _60%_ more expensive, _4_ lower will be _80%_ more expensive, and _5_ lower will be _twice_ the original price. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01084))_
- 5 less Required Strength Royal city level in a large country 4 less Required Strength Large city in a large country, royal city level in a small country 3 less Required Strength Reasonable city 2 less Required Strength Town of about 1000 people 1 less Required Strength All but the most remote village _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01086))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01081))_

> In addition, no matter how high quality it is, _nothing_ can affect its attack power or evasion speed. It’ll also have no effect on monsters that can only be wounded by magic or magical weapons (such as spirits, beastmen, etc.).

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01082))_

> Also, even if you search all over Alecrast, you will usually only find _one_ with a required strength up to _5 lower than usual_ .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01085))_

> **Table 4-5: Guideline for Obtaining High-Quality Weapons and Armor**

### **4.10.2 Silver Weapons and Armor**

- **Silver** is a special metal, imbued with magical powers. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01089))_
- It is also one of only two types of metal that’s known to exist in the _spirit world_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01089))_
- It is also one of only two types of metal that’s known to exist in the _spirit world_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01089))_
- Even silver weapons and armor have no differences in their strike power, defense power, attack power, or evasion speed at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01090))_
- However, **silver weapons** , due to their magic power, can deal damage to monsters that can _only_ be wounded by magic or magical weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01091))_
- Also, shamans are not normally able to wear metal armor (as metal armor prevents them from using spirit magic), but they _can_ use spirit magic while wearing it, if it’s **silver armor** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01092))_
- For example, the price of a silver item with a required strength _2 times lower_ than the original would be 5 x 2 = **10 times** the price of the original. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01093))_
- To buy silver weapons and armor in a store, you must be in a town about the size of the _royal city level in a small country._ Silver weapons and armor will also cost _5 times_ the original price. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01093))_
- You can also ask for a _high-quality_ item of silver, but the price will be even _higher_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01093))_
- For example, the price of a silver item with a required strength _2 times lower_ than the original would be 5 x 2 = **10 times** the price of the original. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01093))_

### **4.10.3**

### **Magical Weapons and Armor**

- These magical weapons and armor are seldom found, and are usually rarely traded. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01096))_
- In Forcelia, there are magical weapons and armor that were once created during the age of the _magical kingdom_ (Kastuul). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01096))_
- Characters may obtain these magical weapons and armor through their adventures. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01097))_
- The power of magical weapons and armor is represented by a +1 or +2 score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01097))_
- There are only a scant few weapons and armor in the world with _+3_ power, and any with _+4 or more_ have yet to be found. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01098))_
- In Forcelia, the limit for this power is _+3_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01098))_
- In Forcelia, the limit for this power is _+3_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01098))_
- There are only a scant few weapons and armor in the world with _+3_ power, and any with _+4 or more_ have yet to be found. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01098))_

### 【 **Magical Weapons** 】

- If +2, those bonuses are +2, and if +3, they're +3. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01100))_
- If you use a +1 magical weapon, you receive a _+1 bonus_ to attack power and bonus damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01100))_
- **Magical weapons** have exceptional _attack power_ and _bonus damage_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01100))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01100))_

> **Magical weapons** have exceptional _attack power_ and _bonus damage_ . If you use a +1 magical weapon, you receive a _+1 bonus_ to attack power and bonus damage. If +2, those bonuses are +2, and if +3, they're +3.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01101))_

> Magical weapons can deal damage to monsters that normal weapons cannot harm.

### 【 **Magical Shields** 】

- A +1 magic shield grants _+1_ evasion speed, +2 if +2. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01103))_
- **Magical shields** increase evasion speed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01103))_

### 【 **Magical Armor** 】

- It does _not_ reduce damage caused by magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01105))_
- It does _not_ reduce damage caused by magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01105))_
- Magical weapons and armor are not usually traded, but if you are very lucky you _may_ be able to buy them if they’re +1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01107))_
- Also, they offer basically _no_ bonus to strike power or defense power, same as required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01107))_
- _High-quality_ ones (made with lower required strength) seem to exist only _rarely_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01108))_
- _High-quality_ ones (made with lower required strength) seem to exist only _rarely_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01108))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01105))_

> If you wear +1 magical armor, you gain _+1_ to evasion speed and damage reduction.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01106))_

> Even if it is magical, if it is _metal_ armor, a shaman cannot wear it.

### **4.10.4 Mithril Silver Weapons and Armor**

- However, the art of forging mithril silver was lost with the fall of the _magical kingdom_ , so it has not been handed down to the present day. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01110))_
- **Mithril silver** , also known as **true silver** , is produced in very few places in the world. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01110))_
- If a character ever sees them, mithril silver weapons and armor are definitely a _legacy_ from the era of the magical kingdom. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01111))_
- No one has yet discovered anything with a power of +4 or higher. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01112))_
- This power affects attack power, evasion speed, bonus damage, and damage reduction. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01112))_
- Mithril silver weapons and armor are always _magical_ items and, like magical weapons and armor, have a power of +1 or +2. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01112))_
- For this reason, strike power and defense power are calculated by adding _+5_ to required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01113))_
- Mithril silver is also a very strong metal. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01113))_
- What makes them different from high-quality weapons and armor is that they do not _lower required strength_ but rather _increase strike power and defense power_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01114))_
- Therefore, you can find a broadsword clad in mithril silver that has _required strength 16_ and _strike power 21_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01114))_
- Therefore, you can find a broadsword clad in mithril silver that has _required strength 16_ and _strike power 21_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01114))_
- Mithril silver _and_ high-quality-- such as a broadsword made of mithril silver, originally with required strength _16_ but made with _5 less_ , and _also_ with _+5_ strike power because it’s mithril silver, resulting in required strength _11_ and strike power _21_ --is almost _always_ rare. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01115))_
- Mithril silver _and_ high-quality-- such as a broadsword made of mithril silver, originally with required strength _16_ but made with _5 less_ , and _also_ with _+5_ strike power because it’s mithril silver, resulting in required strength _11_ and strike power _21_ --is almost _always_ rare. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01115))_
- Mithril silver, like regular silver, is a metal that exists in the spirit world. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01116))_
- Therefore, a shaman can wear mithril silver armor and _still_ use spirit magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01116))_
- Therefore, a shaman can wear mithril silver armor and _still_ use spirit magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01116))_
- Mithril silver chain mail does _not_ interfere with the thief or ranger skills. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01117))_
- Chain mail made of mithril silver does _not_ inhibit bodily movement as much as that made of steel (but splint 52 armor, lamellar armor, and plate armor still _do_ ). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01117))_
- Mithril silver weapons and armor are _never_ , if ever, placed in shops or traded for money. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01118))_
- It should be noted that, although this does not affect the numeric aspects of the game, mithril silver can be made to emit a variety of colors depending on how it’s processed, and these colors will _never_ fade. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01119))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01110))_

> Mithril silver is itself a mineral imbued with magic, and when processed, it gains power.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01116))_

> Mithril silver, like regular silver, is a metal that exists in the spirit world. Therefore, a shaman can wear mithril silver armor and _still_ use spirit magic.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01115))_

> Mithril silver weapons can deal damage to monsters that normal weapons cannot harm.

### **Forger of Colors**

- The time of the ancient kingdom was a time of magical civilization far more advanced than today. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01121))_
- Dwarves in particular excelled in their craft, and powerful and beautiful mithril silver items made by dwarves can still be found in Alecrast even today. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01121))_
- The following is an excerpt from a travelog entitled _The Natural History of Alecrast_ , compiled by Laverna, a witch of Orphan, who toured the continent of Alecrast, and tells the episode of Somska, a dwarven craftsman said to be the best in the ancient kingdom. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01122))_
- Since I was told that showing off such a thing would only make me a target for thieves, I have rarely used this pen and have put it away, but when I occasionally take it out, I can see that it has not lost any of the beautiful contrast between red and yellow._ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01123))_
- The "Crimson" breastplate is said to glow even redder than the blood of enemies, and the "White Sword" given to the commander of the kingsguard of Ramliearth does not lose its whiteness, no matter how much blood is spilled. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01123))_
- I myself was given a beautifully crafted pen in the shape of a feather by the king at the start of my journey. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01123))_
- _The many mithril silver objects dating back to the ancient kingdom period are a marvel to us with their variety of colors and brilliancy. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01123))_
- Since I was told that showing off such a thing would only make me a target for thieves, I have rarely used this pen and have put it away, but when I occasionally take it out, I can see that it has not lost any of the beautiful contrast between red and yellow._ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01123))_
- _However, even today, on rare occasions, mithril silver ore is found, but it is black and has no sparkle whatsoever. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01124))_
- Depending on the skill of the craftsman, it seems that different colors could be shown in a single item, as in my pen._ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01124))_
- Mithril silver is said to acquire different colors and brilliance only_ **==> picture [345 x 226] intentionally omitted <==** _when one draws out its power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01124))_
- _your skill, you can make all the colors of the world appear in one crown," he said. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01125))_
- Somska silently accepted the lump of mithril silver and resigned his seat._ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01125))_
- _It's been said that no one could surpass the dwarves when it comes to the processing of mithril silver. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01126))_
- The quality and quantity of Somska's mithril work was said to be outstanding, and countless legends have been passed down. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01126))_
- Among the dwarves, the one who is said to have been the most skilled in the processing of mithril silver in the ancient kingdom, was Somska, an enchanter called the "Forger of Colors". _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01126))_
- Finally, the last one, the crown of Somska, was presented. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01127))_
- The crowns were all wonderfully made. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01127))_
- _A month passed, and the enchanters came before the great lord, each carrying a crown. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01127))_
- The great lord sighed loudly and contentedly at each of the crowns. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01127))_
- The great lord expected a crown that would be greater than any he had ever received before._ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01127))_
- _A month passed, and the enchanters came before the great lord, each carrying a crown. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01127))_
- The great lord expected a crown that would be greater than any he had ever received before._ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01127))_
- The great lord gathered the famous enchanters and gave them mithril silver. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01128))_
- _It is said that there were two great lords, both very vain. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01128))_
- Whoever makes the most beautiful crown will receive any reward they desire."_ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01128))_
- One day, one of these great lords heard that the other had ordered a crown made, adorned with a hundred precious stones, and decided to have a crown made for himself as well. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01128))_
- The two were strongly conscious of one another, and when one of them found a beautiful wife, the other sought out a beautiful girl, and when one threw a grand feast, the other would compete by throwing one twice as grand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01128))_
- The great lord ordered, "Make a crown with all your skill and magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01128))_
- The great lord was surprised, angry, and exclaimed, "Somska, what is this?! _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01129))_
- _But what Somska offered was a black, gleamless crown with no trace of processing. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01129))_
- But like Somska, the dwarves will not reveal that smile behind their beards._ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01130))_
- And, in fact, I also felt that the dwarves might have an exceptional sense of humor. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01130))_
- _Somska was also summoned and given mithril silver. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01131))_
- Somska was also the one that the great lord relied on the most. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01131))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01126))_

> _It's been said that no one could surpass the dwarves when it comes to the processing of mithril silver. Among the dwarves, the one who is said to have been the most skilled in the processing of mithril silver in the ancient kingdom, was Somska, an enchanter called the "Forger of Colors". The quality and quantity of Somska's mithril work was said to be outstanding, and countless legends have been passed down. The following anecdote is one of them._

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01129))_

> Was this crown not to display all the colors of the world?!" It is said that Somska answered without changing the expression beneath his beard at all, "If you mix all colors together, you will only get black."_

## 4.11 Damage Recovery

- Damage dealt to a character's life force can be recovered in a variety of ways. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01135))_

### **4.11.1**

### **Magical Recovery**

- Life force that a character has lost due to damage is relatively easy to recover through the use of magic or items with magical powers. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01138))_
- The degree of this recovery can be found in the descriptions of each spell or magic item. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01139))_

### **4.11.2**

### **First-Aid**

- A character with 0 or negative life force will recover up to 1 (positive) point if given treatment by someone with the ranger skill, healer skill, or similar. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01142))_
- It can also instantly recover life force to 1 point, no matter how great the negative, but it _cannot_ recover life force to 2 or more points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01143))_

### **4.11.3**

### **Natural Recovery**

- If a character is conscious and at _rest_ (full rest in a safe place), that character recovers 1 point of life force per day. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01146))_
- If a character is _not_ at rest (e.g., traveling, fighting monsters in a labyrinth, not getting much sleep at night due to keeping watch), that character cannot recover any life force at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01148))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01147))_

> If someone with the **healer skill** is nursing them, that character can recover even more life force each day.

## Chapter 5: Magic

## 5.1.4 How to Use Magic

### **Basic Rules of Magic**

### **5.1**

### **5.1 Basic Rules of Magic**

### **5.1.1**

### **Types of Magic**

- In _Sword World_ , there is a power called **magic** that can produce various mysterious effects by chanting words called **runes** and performing certain procedures at the same time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01161))_
- However, this also has some limitations. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01162))_
- Some rune masters are fluent in multiple runes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01162))_
- However, this also has some limitations. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01162))_

### **Example:**

- There are five types of runes in the world of _Sword World_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01164))_
- This difference in runes is at the same time a difference in the type of magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01164))_
- Each has a different source of magical power and a completely different procedure (although _holy prayer_ and _daemon scream_ are essentially the same power, the only difference being the nature of the magic). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01164))_
- The five runes are **high ancient** , **silent spirit** , **holy prayer** , **daemon scream** , and **dragon roar** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01164))_
- Each has a different source of magical power and a completely different procedure (although _holy prayer_ and _daemon scream_ are essentially the same power, the only difference being the nature of the magic). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01164))_
- This adventurer can use 1st level holy magic and 1st-2nd level spirit magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01165))_
- Suppose we have an adventurer with priest skill level 1 and shaman skill level 2. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01165))_
- These types of magic are obtained through belief in _gods_ , _wicked gods_ , and _dragons_ , respectively. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01166))_
- A character cannot hold more than _one_ of these beliefs at the same time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01166))_
- 13) The other, is that you _cannot_ have more than _one each_ of holy, dark, and dragon roar magic at the same time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01166))_
- One, is that some races may not be able to learn some or all of the runes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01166))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01165))_

> He cannot use any ancient magic, nor any holy magic of 2nd level or higher, nor any spirit magic of 3rd level or higher.

### **5.1.4**

### **How to Use Magic**

- They are called **common runes** and **spellsongs** , respectively. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01169))_
- In addition to these runes, there are two others that, although weak, can still manifest magical power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01169))_

### **5.1.4.1 Magic Power**

- First, adventurers who use magic are given a score called **magic power** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01172))_

### **5.1.3**

### **Learning Magic**

### **5.1.2**

### **Rune Masters and Rune Master Skills**

- Namely, Sorcerer uses **ancient magic** Shaman uses **spirit magic** Priest uses **holy magic** Dark priest uses **dark magic** and dragon priest uses **dragon roar magic** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01177))_
- Rune masters are divided into five types according to the type of magic (runes) they use. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01177))_
- In _Sword World_ , all those who use magic utilizing runes are called **rune masters** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01177))_
- Those with the _sorcerer skill_ can use _ancient magic_ and are called _sorcerers_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01178))_
- Each corresponds to the skill for which it is named. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01178))_
- Magic is not omnipotent. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01179))_
- 1st level spells are very rudimentary, while 10th level spells are very powerful. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01179))_
- It is only possible to achieve a defined and limited set of effects by weaving certain words (in runes), called **spells** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01179))_
- Depending on the strength of their effects, spells are divided into _levels_ from _1-10_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01179))_
- There are several types of spells, each with different effects. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01179))_
- It is only possible to achieve a defined and limited set of effects by weaving certain words (in runes), called **spells** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01179))_
- A rune master can use _all_ magic up to _their_ skill level, but _cannot_ use magic of a _higher_ level than their current skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01180))_
- However, working with higher level (i.e., more advanced) spells requires a high rune master skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01180))_
- For those who have acquired multiple rune master skills, consider the level of spells you can use for each type of magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01181))_
- If you’ve acquired _multiple_ magic skills, consider the magic power of each _separately_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01183))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01181))_

> For those who have acquired multiple rune master skills, consider the level of spells you can use for each type of magic.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01182))_

> **magic power** = magic skill level + intelligence bonus

### **Example:**

- An adventurer with priest skill level 1, shaman skill level 2, and 13 intelligence (+2 bonus) would have a magic power of _3_ in holy magic and a magic power of _4_ in spirit magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01185))_

### **Filling out the Character Sheet**

- Characters with rune master skills must write their _runes_ , _skill level_ , and _magic power_ in this field. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01187))_
- On the Character Sheet, there is a _magic_ field. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01187))_
- **==> picture [348 x 407] intentionally omitted <==** _spirit_ in the _runes_ column and _1_ and _3_ (holy prayer), and _2_ and 4 (silent spirit) in the _level_ and _magic power_ columns to the right of them, respectively. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01190))_

### **Procedure for Casting 5.1.4.3 Non-resistible Magic**

- The game procedures for using resistible and non-resistible magic are quite different. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01192))_

### **Resisting**

### **5.1.4.2**

- _Non-resistible_ magic can be effective regardless of what the target does. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01196))_
- There are two kinds of magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01196))_
- Declare which magic you’re using, then roll 2D. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01197))_
- Declare which magic you’re using, then roll 2D. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01197))_
- If it _is_ double ones, then for some reason (the spell was inaccurate, etc.) the magic takes _no_ effect at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01198))_
- If it _is_ double ones, then for some reason (the spell was inaccurate, etc.) the magic takes _no_ effect at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01198))_
- This distinction will be specified for each spell in the spell lists that follow. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01199))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01193, source-range-870a16bc-01198))_

> The procedure for an adventurer to use _non-resistible_ magic is as follows: That's all. If the result is anything other than double ones, the magic takes effect. If it _is_ double ones, then for some reason (the spell was inaccurate, etc.) the magic takes _no_ effect at all.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01196))_

> Magic that is _resistible_ can be rendered ineffective or less effective if the target chooses to **resist** .

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01197))_

> Declare which magic you’re using, then roll 2D.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01198))_

> If the result is anything other than double ones, the magic takes effect.

### **Procedure When an 5.1.4.4 Adventurer Casts Resistible Magic on a Monster**

- If the 2D roll comes up _double ones_ , that means there was some mistake and the magic did _not_ work. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01201))_
- You must make a success roll using _caster’s magic power_ as the baseline score and the monster’s **mental point resistance** as the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01201))_
- If the resistance score is _not_ met, the magic will _not_ work or will only have a _weak_ effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01201))_
- Even if the magic doesn’t normally lose its effect when resisted, in this case it is completely nullified. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01201))_
- If _2D roll + magic power_ is greater than or equal to the monster’s resistance score, the magic will be cast with its full effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01201))_
- If the 2D roll comes up _double ones_ , that means there was some mistake and the magic did _not_ work. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01201))_
- If the resistance score is _not_ met, the magic will _not_ work or will only have a _weak_ effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01201))_
- In this case, there is no need to roll the dice, and the magic does not take effect). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01202))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01199))_

> This distinction will be specified for each spell in the spell lists that follow.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01201))_

> When attempting to cast _resistible_ magic on a monster, the caster must determine whether their opponent can resist it.

### **Example:**

- In other words, if the 2D roll is _5 or mor_ e, _Fire Bolt_ will take its full effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01206))_
- The baseline score is _4_ (magic power) and the target score is _9_ (goblin's mental point resistance). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01206))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01204))_

> Consider an adventurer with shaman skill level 2 and a +2 intelligence bonus (magic power 4) who casts the _Fire Bolt_ spell, which shoots a flaming bullet at the opponent, on a goblin (mental point resistance 9).

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01205))_

> _Fire Bolt_ is _resistible_ magic, so if it’s _resisted_ by the opponent, its effect will be reduced.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01204))_

> Consider an adventurer with shaman skill level 2 and a +2 intelligence bonus (magic power 4) who casts the _Fire Bolt_ spell, which shoots a flaming bullet at the opponent, on a goblin (mental point resistance 9).

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01206))_

> In this case, the adventurer (the player controlling them) must make a success roll.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01209))_

> Roll of 5 or greater: _Fire Bolt_ takes its full effect.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01210))_

> Roll of 3-4: _Fire Bolt_ takes an incomplete effect.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01211))_

> Roll of 2 (double ones): _Fire Bolt_ does not work at all.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01212))_

> magic power + 2D ≥ monster's mental point resistance → **magic takes full effect** magic power + 2D < monster's mental point resistance → **magic takes an incomplete effect (some take no effect at all)** When the 2D roll is _double sixes_ , the magic will _automatically_ take its _full_ effect.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01211))_

> Roll of 2 (double ones): _Fire Bolt_ does not work at all.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01212))_

> magic power + 2D ≥ monster's mental point resistance → **magic takes full effect** magic power + 2D < monster's mental point resistance → **magic takes an incomplete effect (some take no effect at all)** When the 2D roll is _double sixes_ , the magic will _automatically_ take its _full_ effect.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01212))_

> magic power + 2D ≥ monster's mental point resistance → **magic takes full effect** magic power + 2D < monster's mental point resistance → **magic takes an incomplete effect (some take no effect at all)** When the 2D roll is _double sixes_ , the magic will _automatically_ take its _full_ effect.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01213))_

> When the 2D roll is _double ones_ , the magic _unconditionally_ takes _no_ effect at all.

### **Procedure When a 5.1.4.5 Monster Casts Resistible Magic on an Adventurer**

- When a monster casts magic, the procedure is different from when an adventurer casts magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01215))_
- In the case of _resistible_ magic, the issue is resolved from the perspective of whether the adventurer is able to resist the magic cast by the monster. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01216))_
- There is no need to roll the dice, as the magic will _definitely_ take its effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01216))_
- This is determined by a success roll using the character's _mental power resistance_ , that is, a _mental power resistance roll_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01216))_
- First, in the case of _non-resistible_ magic, a monster can _never_ fail. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01216))_
- The target score is a score called the monster’s **magic intensity** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01216))_
- If the adventurer's final score is _less_ than the monster's magic intensity, they will receive the _full_ effect of the magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01217))_
- Also, since this is considered a success roll, it’ll be an _automatic failure_ if the 2D roll is _double ones_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01218))_
- Conversely, _double sixes_ is an _automatic success_ , so in this case, regardless of the adventurer's mental power resistance or the monster's magic intensity, that’s a _successful resist_ * and the magic will only take an incomplete effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01219))_
- **==> picture [344 x 429] intentionally omitted <==** resistance or how low the monster's magic intensity, the magic will take its _full_ effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01219))_
- Conversely, _double sixes_ is an _automatic success_ , so in this case, regardless of the adventurer's mental power resistance or the monster's magic intensity, that’s a _successful resist_ * and the magic will only take an incomplete effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01219))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01216))_

> First, in the case of _non-resistible_ magic, a monster can _never_ fail. There is no need to roll the dice, as the magic will _definitely_ take its effect. In the case of _resistible_ magic, the issue is resolved from the perspective of whether the adventurer is able to resist the magic cast by the monster. This is determined by a success roll using the character's _mental power resistance_ , that is, a _mental power resistance roll_ . The target score is a score called the monster’s **magic in

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01217))_

> If the adventurer's final score ( _mental power resistance + 2D_ ) is equal to or greater than the monster's _magic intensity_ , the monster's magic will only take an incomplete effect (some magic will take no effect at all).

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01218))_

> Also, since this is considered a success roll, it’ll be an _automatic failure_ if the 2D roll is _double ones_ . No matter how high the adventurer's mental power

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01220))_

> Ducard II, Ducard II must make a mental power resistance roll (baseline score 4, target score 13).

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01222))_

> Roll is 8 or less: failed resist. _Fire Bolt_ takes its full effect.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01223))_

> mental power resistance + 2D ≥ monster's magic intensity → **magic takes an incomplete effect (some take no effect at all)** *In this way, when making a _resistible_ magic check, you can also call it a _successful resist_ or _failed resist_ from the perspective of the one being affected by the magic.

> mental power resistance + 2D ≥ monster's magic intensity → **magic takes an incomplete effect (some take no effect at all)** *In this way, when making a _resistible_ magic check, you can also call it a _successful resist_ or _failed resist_ from the perspective of the one being affected by the magic.

### **Example:**

- Ducard II (mental power resistance 4) is attacked by a dark elf during an adventure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01227))_
- If the dark elf casts a _Fire Bolt_ on When the 2D roll is _double ones_ , the resist _automatically fails_ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01227))_

### **Procedure When an 5.1.4.6 Adventurer Casts Resistible Magic on an Adventurer**

- Just like player characters, even those whose data was created for a character may appear as an enemy. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01230))_
- In other words, you compare the _magic power + 2D_ score of the one casting the magic with the _mental power resistance + 2D_ score of the one on whom the magic is being cast. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01231))_
- The resolution of _double ones_ and _double sixes_ is also the same as weapon combat. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01232))_
- In other words, if the _resisting side_ rolls double sixes, the resist is an _unconditional success_ , otherwise, if _the side casting the magic_ rolls double sixes, the resist is a _failure_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01232))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01230))_

> Just like player characters, even those whose data was created for a character may appear as an enemy. If such an enemy casts magic on an adventurer, or if an adventurer casts magic on such an enemy, both sides must roll dice to resolve the situation, just as in weapon combat between adventurers.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01231))_

> If the score of the one casting the magic is greater, the magic will take _full_ effect, but if there is a tie or if the score of the one on whom the magic is being cast is greater, the magic is _resisted_ and will only take an _incomplete_ effect.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01230))_

> Just like player characters, even those whose data was created for a character may appear as an enemy. If such an enemy casts magic on an adventurer, or if an adventurer casts magic on such an enemy, both sides must roll dice to resolve the situation, just as in weapon combat between adventurers.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01233))_

> When the one casting the magic rolls _double ones_ , the magic takes _no_ effect at all.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01234))_

> When the resisting side rolls _double ones_ , the resist _fails_ , unless the side casting the magic _also_ rolls double ones.

### **Handling Resistible Magic 5.1.4.7 and Multiple Targets**

- This kind of magic can have multiple targets, and each target must make their own check to resist. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01237))_

### **When an Adventurer Casts**

### **Magic on Monsters**

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01240))_

### **Example:**

- _Lightning_ is a spell that shoots a bolt of lightning, dealing damage to everything in its path. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01244))_
- Suppose there are three goblins (each with a mental point resistance of 9) and one goblin lord (mental point resistance 12) in this space. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01248))_
- If the rolls against the three goblins were _6_ , _3_ , and _2_ (double ones!), and the roll against the goblin lord was _6_ , then the result would look like this: _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01250))_
- If the rolls against the three goblins were _6_ , _3_ , and _2_ (double ones!), and the roll against the goblin lord was _6_ , then the result would look like this: _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01250))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01240))_

> The player must roll the dice and make a check for each monster.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01243))_

> 1st goblin: 5 (magic power) + 6 (roll) = 11 ≥ 9 (mental point resistance), full effect. 3rd goblin: The result is double ones, so no effect

If there are multiple targets in the path of the lightning bolt, you must make a check for each one’s attempt to resist. 3rd goblin: The result is double ones, so no effect

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01245))_

> 2nd goblin: 5 (magic power) + 3 (roll) = 8 < 9 (mental point resistance), incomplete effect.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01245))_
