> When rolling the dice, you must clearly declare which target the dice are meant for before you actually roll.

## 5.1.6 Recovering Mental Power

### **When a Monster Casts Magic on Adventurers**

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01254))_

> Each player must make a resistance roll using _monster's magic intensity_ as the target score.

### **Example:**

- This centaur has the ability to use ancient magic and unleashes _Lightning_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01256))_
- The game master declares that its magic intensity is _13_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01256))_
- Each of the three adventurers (the players in control) roll 2D to check if they resist, using _their mental power resistance_ as the baseline score, and _13 (magic intensity)_ as the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01257))_

### **When an Adventurer Casts Magic on Adventurers**

- The following procedure is used to make a check when data created for a character casts magic on adventurers, and vice versa. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01259))_
- First, the side casting the magic rolls 2D. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01260))_
- The final score (magic power + 2D) must then be determined this one time only. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01260))_
- The final score (magic power + 2D) must then be determined this one time only. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01260))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01259))_

> The following procedure is used to make a check when data created for a character casts magic on adventurers, and vice versa.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01260))_

> Each resisting side must then make a mental power resistance roll, using that determined final score as the target score.

### **Example:**

- If their rolls are each _2_ (double ones!), _6_ , and _9_ , then only the _last_ one is able to resist the _Lightning_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01262))_
- If we assume his 2D roll is _8_ , each of the three adventurers must make a mental power resistance roll, with a target score of 5 (magic power) + 8 (roll) = _13_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01262))_
- Suppose that the baseline score of all three of their mental power resistance rolls is _4_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01262))_
- First, Gorman rolls 2D and adds his own magic power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01262))_
- If their rolls are each _2_ (double ones!), _6_ , and _9_ , then only the _last_ one is able to resist the _Lightning_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01262))_
- If the side casting the magic rolls double ones, the magic has _no_ effect on _any_ targets. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01263))_
- Handling double ones and double sixes is no different from when there’s only one target. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01263))_
- Handling double ones and double sixes is no different from when there’s only one target. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01263))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01262))_

> The evil sorcerer Gorman (magic power 5) unleashes _Lightning_ on the three adventurers. First, Gorman rolls 2D and adds his own magic power. If we assume his 2D roll is _8_ , each of the three adventurers must make a mental power resistance roll, with a target score of 5 (magic power) + 8 (roll) = _13_ . Suppose that the baseline score of all three of their mental power resistance rolls is _4_ . If their rolls are each _2_ (double ones!), _6_ , and _9_ , then only the _last_ one is able to resi

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01263))_

> If the side casting the magic rolls double sixes, _no_ _one_ can resist, except for those who _also_ roll double sixes.

### **When an Adventurer Casts Magic on a Mix of**

### **Adventurers and Monsters**

- Sometimes enemies written in the same data format as a character may also be accompanied by monsters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01266))_
- Casting magic on such a group is handled the same way as when an adventurer casts magic on (a group of) adventurers, as described above. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01266))_
- At this time, the monsters must _also_ roll the dice to make resist checks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01266))_

### **If the Target Does 5.1.4.8 Not Resist**

- For example, the spirit magic _Fanaticism_ increases attack power, but conversely _decreases_ evasion speed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01268))_
- Some magic can also have secondary effects. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01268))_
- With such magic, the one on whom it is cast can _choose_ whether or not to resist. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01268))_
- For example, the spirit magic _Fanaticism_ increases attack power, but conversely _decreases_ evasion speed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01268))_
- You’re also allowed to choose to _not resist_ magic like _Fire Bolt_ , which will definitely result in a loss if it's cast on you....... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01269))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01268))_

> Some magic can also have secondary effects. For example, the spirit magic _Fanaticism_ increases attack power, but conversely _decreases_ evasion speed. With such magic, the one on whom it is cast can _choose_ whether or not to resist.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01269))_

> If the one on whom the magic is being cast chooses _not_ to resist the magic, it will _definitely_ take effect so long as the one casting the magic does _not_ roll double ones.

### **5.1.5**

### **Consuming Mental Power**

- When using magic, an adventurer must temporarily _reduce_ their mental power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01272))_
- Each magic has a score called **base mental power cost** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01273))_
- This base mental power cost, divided by the corresponding rune master skill level (rounded up), equals the actual mental power consumed by the character. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01273))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01269))_

> If the one on whom the magic is being cast chooses _not_ to resist the magic, it will _definitely_ take effect so long as the one casting the magic does _not_ roll double ones. You’re also allowed to choose to _not resist_ magic like _Fire Bolt_ , which will definitely result in a loss if it's cast on you....... but that probably won't benefit you in any way.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01272))_

> Adventurers cannot use magic endlessly.

### **Example:**

- A character with shaman skill level 2 uses the _Fire Bolt_ spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01275))_
- The base mental power cost for _Fire Bolt_ is _5_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01275))_
- The character must consume 5 ÷ 2 = 2.5, rounding up to _3_ points of mental power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01275))_
- A character can reduce their mental power to 0, but they _cannot_ make it negative. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01276))_
- For example, a character with 5 points of mental power remaining _can_ use magic that costs 5 points of mental power, but _cannot_ use magic that costs _6 or more_ points of mental power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01276))_
- For example, a character with 5 points of mental power remaining _can_ use magic that costs 5 points of mental power, but _cannot_ use magic that costs _6 or more_ points of mental power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01276))_
- If a character’s mental power reaches _0_ , they will _lose consciousness_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01277))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01276))_

> A character can reduce their mental power to 0, but they _cannot_ make it negative. For example, a character with 5 points of mental power remaining _can_ use magic that costs 5 points of mental power, but _cannot_ use magic that costs _6 or more_ points of mental power.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01277))_

> The character cannot regain consciousness unless they recover their mental power to _1 point or more_ in some way.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01276))_

> A character can reduce their mental power to 0, but they _cannot_ make it negative. For example, a character with 5 points of mental power remaining _can_ use magic that costs 5 points of mental power, but _cannot_ use magic that costs _6 or more_ points of mental power.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01278))_

> Even if magic _fails_ , such as by rolling double ones or being resisted by the opponent, a character must _still_ consume their mental power, same as if they had actually used the magic.

### **5.1.6**

### **Recovering Mental Power**

- Mental power reduced due to using magic, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01281))_

### 《 **Magical Recovery** 》

- The amount recovered is explained under each magic and item. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01283))_
- There's magic and magical items that can recover a character's mental power, such as the holy magic _Transfer Mental Power_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01283))_
- There's magic and magical items that can recover a character's mental power, such as the holy magic _Transfer Mental Power_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01283))_

### 《 **Recovery via Sleep** 》

- If a character sleeps for at least 6 hours in a row, they will fully recover their mental power upon awakening. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01285))_
- However, this method of recovery can only be done _once_ a day. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01285))_
- However, this method of recovery can only be done _once_ a day. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01285))_

## 5.1.7 Magic Expansion

- within 24 hours of your previous awakening (when your mental power was last recovered), your mental power will _not_ be recovered. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01288))_
- within 24 hours of your previous awakening (when your mental power was last recovered), your mental power will _not_ be recovered. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01288))_
- You can increase the effectiveness and certainty of magic by consuming extra mental power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01291))_
- This is called **magic expansion** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01291))_
- This is called **magic expansion** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01291))_
- Instead, you divide the mental power by your _skill level, rounded up_ , or in other words, the mental power you would consume when _not_ expanding is doubled, tripled...... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01292))_
- Magic expansion is done by doubling or tripling...... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01292))_
- At this time, you do _not_ double or triple the base mental power cost....... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01292))_
- the _amount_ of mental power consumed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01292))_

### **Example:**

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01294))_

> Suppose a sorcerer with sorcerer skill level 2 doubles the range of his _Energy Bolt_ spell and casts it on two enemies. The base mental power cost of _Energy Bolt_ is _5_ . For this reason, the mental power consumed _without_ expansion is 5 ÷ 2, rounded up to _3_ . Therefore, for a _doubled_ expansion, the amount of mental power consumed would be 3 x 2 = **6** . You would _not_ calculate (5 x 2) ÷ 2 = 5.

### **5.1.7.1 Expansion Types**

- Magic expansion includes the following types. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01296))_

### 《 **Targets Expansion** 》

- the mental power consumed will also be _doubled_ or _tripled_ ....... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01299))_
- For spells that can perform this expansion, **targets** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01301))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01296))_

> Magic expansion includes the following types.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01298))_

> If you expand a spell that affects one enemy (or ally), it can instead affect multiple at the same time.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01300))_

> In this case, you must roll the dice separately for each target to determine if they resist the magic, and other effects such as damage must also be determined separately.

### 《 **Area Expansion** 》

- the mental power consumed will also be _doubled_ or _tripled_ ....... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01304))_
- So for expansions such as _x 1.5_ and _x 8/3_ , the cost would be _double_ and _triple_ , respectively. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01304))_
- If the area is _doubled_ or _tripled_ ...... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01304))_
- So for expansions such as _x 1.5_ and _x 8/3_ , the cost would be _double_ and _triple_ , respectively. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01304))_
- Since it’s the area that gets expanded, the radius of a circle, a side of a square, etc., can only be increased by the _square root_ of the expansion rate. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01305))_
- Since it’s the area that gets expanded, the radius of a circle, a side of a square, etc., can only be increased by the _square root_ of the expansion rate. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01305))_
- For spells that can perform this expansion, **area** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01306))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01300))_

> In this case, you must roll the dice separately for each target to determine if they resist the magic, and other effects such as damage must also be determined separately.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01303))_

> If you expand a spell that affects a specific area, it can affect a larger area than originally intended.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01303))_

> If you expand a spell that affects a specific area, it can affect a larger area than originally intended.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01304))_

> You can also expand an area by a _fraction_ (x1.5, x8/3, etc.), in which case the fraction is _rounded up_ when determining the multiplier for mental power consumed.

### 《 **Volume Expansion** 》

- If the volume is expanded by _two_ or _three_ times...... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01308))_
- Some spells that affect a space or object can have their volume expanded. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01308))_
- the mental power consumed will also be _doubled_ or _tripled_ ....... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01308))_
- Rounding should be handled the same way you would for area expansion. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01308))_
- Since it’s the volume that gets expanded, the radius of a sphere, a side of a cube, etc., can only be increased by the _cube root_ of the expansion rate. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01309))_
- Since it’s the volume that gets expanded, the radius of a sphere, a side of a cube, etc., can only be increased by the _cube root_ of the expansion rate. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01309))_
- For spells that can perform this expansion, **target volume** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01310))_
- Note: On the table, _expansion rate_ is the multiplier for mental power consumed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01313))_
- When you cast a spell by consuming that multiplied amount of mental power, multiply the number in _area expansion length_ or _volume expansion length_ by the number (radius, side) that represents the spell’s _area of effect_ , and the result will be the spell’s _area_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01314))_
- For spells cast on a flat surface, use the number in the _area expansion length_ column, and for spells cast into space, use the number in the _volume expansion length_ column. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01314))_
- For example, when you cast the ancient magic _Light_ , whose area is _A space with a 10 meter radius_ , by consuming _double_ the mental power, the resulting area will be a spherical space with a radius of _10 x 1.3 =_ _**13 meters**_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01314))_
- For example, when you cast the ancient magic _Light_ , whose area is _A space with a 10 meter radius_ , by consuming _double_ the mental power, the resulting area will be a spherical space with a radius of _10 x 1.3 =_ _**13 meters**_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01314))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01311))_

> **Table 5-1: Area and Size Expansion**

## 5.1.8 Magic Contraction

- For spells that can perform this expansion, **distance** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01322))_

### 《 **Damage Expansion** 》

- For spells that deal damage, expansion can increase the certainty of that damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01324))_
- The damage you will deal is equal to the _greatest_ of all the strike rolls you made in this way ( _not_ the total). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01324))_
- For spells that can perform this expansion, **damage certainty** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01325))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01324))_

> you can make a damage check (strike roll) _twice_ or _three times_ .

### 《 **Final Score Increase** 》

- There's some magic whose effects are negated or reduced if they're resisted by the target, but will also let you _increase_ the magic's final score (resist target score) in order to prevent this. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01327))_
- This increase in final score may also take effect if a nullifying spell is cast later. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01327))_
- For spells that can perform this expansion, **final score** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01329))_
- This expansion requires a deep proficiency in magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01330))_
- Therefore, it cannot be done infinitely. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01330))_
- Final score can only be increased up to a maximum of _caster's rune master skill level ÷ 2 (rounded up)_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01330))_
- Therefore, it cannot be done infinitely. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01330))_
- Final score can only be increased up to a maximum of _caster's rune master skill level ÷ 2 (rounded up)_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01330))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01330))_

> This expansion requires a deep proficiency in magic. Therefore, it cannot be done infinitely. Final score can only be increased up to a maximum of _caster's rune master skill level ÷ 2 (rounded up)_ . That’s up to _+1 (double)_ for 1st~2nd level, _+2 (triple)_ for 3rd~4th level, and _+5 (sextuple)_ for 9th~10th level.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01328))_

> you can add a _+1_ or _+2_ bonus to your final score.

### 《 **Effect Expansion** 》

- For spells that can perform these special expansions, **effect** is written in the _expansion_ section of the magic details, and an explanation is given under each spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01332))_

### **5.1.7.2 Combined Expansion**

- Some magic allows for multiple expansion types. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01334))_
- However, at this time, the amount of mental power consumed will become extremely large. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01334))_
- In these cases, you can perform multiple expansions at the same time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01334))_

### **Example:**

- Suppose a sorcerer casts an _Energy Bolt_ on three enemies ( _x 3_ expansion) with a +1 to his final score ( _x 2_ expansion) and he makes 4 damage checks for each ( _x 4_ expansion). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01336))_

### **5.1.8**

### **Magic Contraction**

- However, this does _not_ reduce the amount of mental power consumed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01339))_
- Magic can also be **contracted** as opposed to expanded. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01339))_

### **5.1.8.1 Contraction Types**

### 《 **Area Contraction** 》

- You also cannot cast it in such a way that it hits the enemy directly in the eyes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01343))_
- The shape (usually a circle) _cannot_ be changed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01343))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01341))_

> Magic contractions include the following:

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01343))_

> You can make the area of effect narrower than originally intended.

### 《 **Volume Contraction** 》

- Again, the shape cannot be changed, and you cannot directly aim it at the enemy's eyes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01345))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01345))_

> Similar to area contraction, you can also reduce the volume of the targeted object or space.

### 《 **Duration Contraction** 》

- You may _only_ set it to wear off at the _end_ of a round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01347))_
- Magic with a duration can be optionally shortened. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01347))_
- However, you _cannot_ set a time that causes the effect to wear off in the middle of a round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01347))_
- Magic that’s already been cast cannot be canceled at will. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01347))_
- You must also decide how long you want the magic to last _before_ you cast it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01347))_

### 《 **Magic Power Decrease** 》

- This will reduce the final score and, if it's damage magic, _also_ reduce bonus damage (it’s not possible to _only_ reduce the final score or _only_ reduce bonus damage). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01349))_
- Also, if the side casting the magic rolls double sixes, the resisting side will _also_ need double sixes, no matter _how much_ magic power was declared to be decreased (if it's a monster, it _cannot_ resist). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01349))_
- Magic power can be optionally reduced. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01349))_
- If used on multiple targets, you must reduce it by the same amount for _all_ of them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01349))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01349))_

> You must declare how many points you’ll reduce _before_ you roll the dice.

### 《 **Effect Contraction** 》

- All magic that can expand their effects can also contract their effects. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01351))_

### **Combined Expansion 5.1.8.2 and Contraction**

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01353))_

> Although it's not often done, it's possible to perform magic expansion _and_ contraction at the same time. For example, you could _extend_ the area of effect while _reducing_ magic power. The mental power required at this time is decided by _expansion_ . It _cannot_ be reduced by contraction. For example, if you _double_ the area _and_ reduce magic power by _-4_ , the mental power consumed will still be _double_ .

## 5.1.9 Magic Types

- These characteristics are presented here as **magic types** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01359))_

### 《 **Damage** 》

- Spells of the damage type always have a clearly defined _strike power_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01361))_
- The key number is, of course, the indicated _strike power_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01361))_
- Conversely, If you roll double ones, it does _not_ deal any damage at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01361))_
- Also, at this time, no matter how high you roll during your strike roll, a _critical_ will _not_ occur. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01362))_
- In this case, you must make the strike roll after _subtracting 10_ from strike power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01362))_
- In this case, you must make the strike roll after _subtracting 10_ from strike power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01362))_
- In either case, the caster's magic power can be added as bonus damage to the result of the strike roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01363))_
- However, there’s an _exception_ if you roll _double ones_ during your strike roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01363))_
- Also, the target's adventurer level (or the monster's monster level) is subtracted from the damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01363))_
- In this case, no matter how much magic power your character (monster) has, they _cannot_ deal damage to the target at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01363))_
- At this time, if the target successfully resists, you _cannot_ make a strike roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01364))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01363))_

In this case, no matter how much magic power your character (monster) has, they _cannot_ deal damage to the target at all.

The key number is, of course, the indicated _strike power_ . If you roll 10 or greater during the strike roll, a _critical_ will occur and deal even mo

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01362))_

### 《 **Target Score** 》

- Some magic can nullify the effects of other magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01366))_
- Most magic is automatically nullified if a nullifying spell is used, but there are some that are not. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01367))_
- This is magic that has a _target score_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01367))_
- If you're trying to nullify magic that has a _target score_ with another spell, you must use that other spell's 2D roll to determine your final score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01368))_
- If this final score is greater than or equal to the _target score_ originally set for that first magic that was cast, then that first magic can be nullified. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01369))_
- If this final score is greater than or equal to the _target score_ originally set for that first magic that was cast, then that first magic can be nullified. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01369))_
- This _target score_ equals the final score of the first magic that was cast (in other words, the magic you're about to nullify). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01370))_
- Therefore, when an adventurer uses magic that has a _target score_ , that player should note down that score and remember it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01370))_
- Therefore, when an adventurer uses magic that has a _target score_ , that player should note down that score and remember it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01370))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01366, source-range-870a16bc-01370))_

This _target score_ equals the final score of the first magic that was cast (in other words, the magic you're about to nullify). Therefore, when an adventurer uses magic that has a _target score_ , that player should note down that score and remember it. However, if you feel this is cumbersome, or if you forget to make a note, then when it becomes necessary (nulli

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01368))_

> When casting any magic, no matter what kind of magic it is, you must roll 2D to check whether an automatic failure (double ones) will occur or not.

> When casting any magic, no matter what kind of magic it is, you must roll 2D to check whether an automatic failure (double ones) will occur or not. If you're trying to nullify magic that has a _target score_ with another spell, you must use that other spell's 2D roll to determine your final score. This final score equals _magic power + 2D roll_ (similar to a final score for other success rolls).

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01370))_

> However, if you feel this is cumbersome, or if you forget to make a note, then when it becomes necessary (nullifying magic has been used), you can roll 2D on the spot to determine a final score = _target score_ .

> This _target score_ equals the final score of the first magic that was cast (in other words, the magic you're about to nullify). Therefore, when an adventurer uses magic that has a _target score_ , that player should note down that score and remember it. However, if you feel this is cumbersome, or if you forget to make a note, then when it becomes necessary (nullifying magic has been used), you can roll 2D on the spot to determine a final score = _target score_ .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01371))_

### 《 **Counters (Negates)** 》

- For example, there are two kinds of ancient magic, _Decrease Weight_ (which makes an object lighter) and _Increase Weight_ (which makes an object heavier), but when both of these are cast on the same object at the same time, they will cancel each other out and disappear without taking effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01374))_
- For example, there are two kinds of ancient magic, _Decrease Weight_ (which makes an object lighter) and _Increase Weight_ (which makes an object heavier), but when both of these are cast on the same object at the same time, they will cancel each other out and disappear without taking effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01374))_
- Such magic, that is, magic that negates the effect of other magic, but at the same time is negated by that other magic without taking its own effect, is called **counters (negates):** type. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01375))_
- Such magic, that is, magic that negates the effect of other magic, but at the same time is negated by that other magic without taking its own effect, is called **counters (negates):** type. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01375))_
- When casting _counters (negates):_ magic to cancel the effect of certain magic, if the magic that was cast prior has a _target score_ , then according to the definition of _magic with a target score_ , the magic that's cast after _cannot_ cancel the prior magic unless its final score is greater than or equal to the determined _target score_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01376))_
- When casting _counters (negates):_ magic to cancel the effect of certain magic, if the magic that was cast prior has a _target score_ , then according to the definition of _magic with a target score_ , the magic that's cast after _cannot_ cancel the prior magic unless its final score is greater than or equal to the determined _target score_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01376))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01372))_

> Conversely, if the dice roll double sixes when determining the _target score_ (the success score of the first magic), you _must_ roll another double sixes in order to nullify that magic.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01374))_

> When magic is cast against other magic, their effects may completely cancel each other out.

### 《 **Counters (Overpowers)** 》

- This is magic of the **counters (overpowers):** type. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01378))_
- For example, the ancient magic _Darkness_ can nullify the effects of the spirit magic _Will-O-Wisp_ and still close the surrounding area in darkness, which means _Darkness_ itself is more effective. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01379))_
- For example, the ancient magic _Darkness_ can nullify the effects of the spirit magic _Will-O-Wisp_ and still close the surrounding area in darkness, which means _Darkness_ itself is more effective. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01379))_
- However, this is often not automatic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01380))_
- One, is when the target _resists_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01380))_
- The other, is when the prior magic has a _target score_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01381))_
- In this case, according to the definition of _magic with a target score_ , the magic cast after will _not_ take effect unless its final score is greater than or equal to the determined _target score_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01381))_
- In this case, according to the definition of _magic with a target score_ , the magic cast after will _not_ take effect unless its final score is greater than or equal to the determined _target score_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01381))_
- If the magic cast prior has a _target score_ and the magic cast after is _resistible_ , please follow the procedure below to make a check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01382))_
- If the magic cast prior has a _target score_ and the magic cast after is _resistible_ , please follow the procedure below to make a check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01382))_
- ② Next, the one casting the magic must roll 2D to find their final score (by adding their magic power). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01385))_
- If the final score does not reach the target score, the magic will _not_ be cast. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01386))_
- ④ If _final score in_ ② is greater than or equal to _target score in_ ①, the target may attempt to _resist_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01387))_
- If the target _failed to resist_ , the new magic cast _will_ take effect and the effect of the prior magic will be _negated_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01388))_
- The effect of the _prior_ magic will continue. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01388))_
- _Counters (overpowers):_ magic does not mean that certain magic is definitely stronger than other magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01389))_
- Therefore, if certain magic takes effect, and you cast that same magic on the same target _multiple_ times, the effect will _not_ be cumulative. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01390))_
- Therefore, if certain magic takes effect, and you cast that same magic on the same target _multiple_ times, the effect will _not_ be cumulative. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01390))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01376))_

> When casting _counters (negates):_ magic to cancel the effect of certain magic, if the magic that was cast prior has a _target score_ , then according to the definition of _magic with a target score_ , the magic that's cast after _cannot_ cancel the prior magic unless its final score is greater than or equal to the determined _target score_ .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01378))_

> When certain magic is cast against other magic, it can cancel the effect of the prior magic but still take its own effect afterwards.

### 《 **Mind** 》

- This kind of magic can only take effect on one target at a time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01392))_
- This is magic that affects the target's mental state. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01392))_
- If magic of the **mind** type is cast on a target, and other magic also of the _mind_ type is cast on that target, the _new_ magic will take effect, and the prior magic will be _nullified_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01392))_
- This kind of magic can only take effect on one target at a time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01392))_
- Note that many are _resistible_ magic, and some have _target scores_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01393))_

### 《 **Physical Ability** 》

- This is magic that affects the target’s physical abilities (strength, etc.). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01395))_
- Magic of this type can _only_ take effect on _humans_ , _fae_ , and _youma_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01395))_
- Also, with magic of the **physical ability** type, two or more cannot affect the same target at the same time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01396))_

### 《 **Weapon Enhancement** 》

- Two or more of this kind of magic cannot affect the same weapon at the same time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01399))_
- Weapons that have been given power by ancient magic, silver weapons, or mithril silver weapons are assumed to already have _non-removable_ (see below) **weapon enhancement** magic cast on them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01400))_
- Therefore, that power _cannot_ be nullified, so even if you cast new _weapon enhancement_ magic, it’ll be _useless_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01400))_
- Therefore, that power _cannot_ be nullified, so even if you cast new _weapon enhancement_ magic, it’ll be _useless_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01400))_

### 《 **Non-removable** 》

- Some magic, such as _Dispel Magic_ (ancient magic) and _Remove Curse_ (holy magic), can be used as counter magic that _removes_ other magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01402))_
- However, magic of the **non-removable** type _cannot_ be removed by other magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01402))_
- Some magic, such as _Dispel Magic_ (ancient magic) and _Remove Curse_ (holy magic), can be used as counter magic that _removes_ other magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01402))_
- It's also possible to remove _non-removable magic_ by directly using the power of a god (wicked god) with the holy/dark magic _Call God_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01403))_
- Although, the ancient magic _Perfect Cancellation_ **is** able to remove _nonremovable_ magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01403))_
- Also, sometimes there is counter magic that counters _certain_ magic, which if specified, will be removed by _that_ counter magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01403))_

### 《 **Curse** 》

- However, _Remove Curse_ (holy magic) **can** be used to remove it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01405))_
- _Dispel Magic_ (ancient magic) can be used to remove most magic, but for magic of the **curse** type, this is not possible. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01405))_

### 《 **Detection** 》

- These can be targeted by the ancient magic _Counter Sense_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01407))_

### 《 **Focus** 》

- Magic of the **focus** type is that in which the magic's effect lasts as long as the caster (the one who casts the magic) is _focused_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01409))_
- Magic has a fixed _duration_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01409))_
- Attack another with a weapon (or barehanded) Move more than 3 meters in one round _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01411))_
- If magic is cast on you, it's also possible to _resist_ it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01414))_
- you can still attempt to _evade_ it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01414))_
- Also, when the target is no longer within the magic's area of effect (out of reach, out of sight, etc.), the magic will _lose_ its effect, even if you continue to focus. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01415))_
- Also, when the target is no longer within the magic's area of effect (out of reach, out of sight, etc.), the magic will _lose_ its effect, even if you continue to focus. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01415))_
- If the caster suffers damage to their life force or mental power, their focus will be interrupted and the magic's effect will be _lost_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01416))_
- If your focus is interrupted due to damage, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01417))_
- your focus will be interrupted at the timing in which the damage is dealt. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01417))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01409))_

> While focusing, the caster cannot take the following actions:

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01417))_

> If the caster wishes to voluntarily release their focus (in order to take another action), they must declare it at the _beginning_ of the round, when no one else has made their action announcements yet.

### 《 **Illusion** 》

## 5.1.10 How to Read Magic Details

- Magic of the **illusion** type is effective on those that perceive their surroundings with _five senses_ or _pseudo_ senses. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01422))_
- There are three types of senses through which monsters perceive their surroundings: **five senses** , **pseudo** senses, and **magic** senses. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01422))_
- opponent’s sensory organs and give them false sensations. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01422))_
- Humans and fae perceive their surroundings through _five senses_ , so they can all be targeted by _illusions_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01422))_
- It is completely ineffective against monsters who perceive their surroundings through _magic_ senses, and illusions that deceive vision are useless against monsters who _lack_ vision. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01423))_
- If the target does not have the senses to deceive, this spell will have no effect at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01423))_
- In the case of player characters (adventurers), this may be when the players themselves suspect an illusion. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01424))_
- However, in order to do this, you must first be suspicious of the illusion. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01424))_
- The game master should decide in which cases it seems suspicious. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01424))_
- For NPCs and monsters, the game master should decide based on their intelligence. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01424))_
- The effects of spells of the _illusion_ type can be detected by a resistance roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01424))_
- For example, suppose a fake floor is created above a pit using a _Create Image_ (ancient magic) spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01425))_
- No matter how smart you are, you’re not likely to notice this. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01425))_
- For example, suppose a fake floor is created above a pit using a _Create Image_ (ancient magic) spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01425))_
- No matter how foolish you are, you should suspect that the floor is an illusion. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01426))_
- It might be unreasonable for a monster with low intelligence, such as a goblin, but a human of average intelligence would find it suspicious. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01428))_
- It might be unreasonable for a monster with low intelligence, such as a goblin, but a human of average intelligence would find it suspicious. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01428))_
- The target score is the final score from when the illusion spell was cast (if you forgot, roll the dice and determine it again). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01429))_
- If you’re successful, you’ll be able to see through the illusion and know the correct situation. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01429))_
- Once you fail, no matter how suspicious it may be, you won't be able to detect its true form (of course, you _can_ walk around suspicious floors). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01429))_
- However, you can only make this resistance roll _once_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01429))_
- However, you can only make this resistance roll _once_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01429))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01428))_

> It might be unreasonable for a monster with low intelligence, such as a goblin, but a human of average intelligence would find it suspicious.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01429))_

> In the event of such a suspicion, the target of the illusion can make a mental power resistance roll.

### 《 **Ritual** 》

- Magic of the **ritual** type can increase its effect (final score) due to carefully preparing and performing a ritual. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01431))_
- If it's ancient magic, the caster must have been taught at a school such as an _academy of sages_ , etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01432))_
- and if it's holy (dark) magic, they must be a believer. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01432))_
- In this case, you can gain +1 to your final score for every 10 people (rounded down). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01432))_
- Even humans who cannot use spells can participate in the ritual. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01432))_
- If additional casters who can use the same spell also participate in the ritual, you will gain +1 to your final score for each one. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01432))_
- However, the ritual procedures are very complicated, so those who have not been taught the procedure in advance will not be able to participate in the ritual. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01432))_
- If it's ancient magic, the caster must have been taught at a school such as an _academy of sages_ , etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01432))_
- _Fractions_ of the number of days must be _rounded down_ , so if you can't spend a _full_ day, the final score bonus will be _0_ , regardless of how many participants there are. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01433))_
- These bonuses are stacked in the form of _days x participants_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01433))_
- Unlike magic expansion, there is _no_ limit to the increase in final score through rituals. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01434))_
- Unlike magic expansion, there is _no_ limit to the increase in final score through rituals. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01434))_
- In the previous example, if 10 believers drop out, even if it's right after the ritual begins, just before the ritual ends, or at any timing in between, the final score bonus will be _2 x (1 + 2) =_ _**+6**_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01435))_
- If someone drops out during the ritual, their number will not be considered at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01435))_
- A ritual must be performed without sleep or rest, and is usually limited to 2 days. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01435))_
- A ritual must be performed without sleep or rest, and is usually limited to 2 days. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01435))_
- In the previous example, if 10 believers drop out, even if it's right after the ritual begins, just before the ritual ends, or at any timing in between, the final score bonus will be _2 x (1 + 2) =_ _**+6**_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01435))_
- The person who makes the success roll is the one who takes the center role in performing the ritual. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01436))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01429))_

### 《 **Lost** 》

- These are called **lost spells** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01439))_
- Among ancient magic, there are some spells known only to a limited number of sorcerers, and spells that were lost with the fall of the ancient kingdom. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01439))_
- Among ancient magic, there are some spells known only to a limited number of sorcerers, and spells that were lost with the fall of the ancient kingdom. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01439))_
- The game master gets to determine which spells are considered lost spells. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01440))_
- It's acceptable to have _all_ spells on the magic list be lost, or conversely, to have all magic be _known_ and _no_ spells be lost. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01440))_
- If a spell says _lost_ under its magic details, it may be easier to maintain balance when enjoying the game if you consider it a lost spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01441))_
- Unless the game master has a particular reason, please consider these to be lost spells. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01441))_

## 5.1.11 Ancient Magic and the Sorcerer Skill

- The skill for using ancient magic is called the _sorcerer skill_ , and those who have this skill are called _sorcerers_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01478))_
- Many of the spells and runes of this system were lost with the fall of the ancient kingdom, but a small amount of them have been passed down to modern Forcelia, and are now referred to as **ancient magic** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01478))_
- _High ancient_ are the great runes that were developed and studied during the time of the ancient kingdom. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01478))_
- The skill for using ancient magic is called the _sorcerer skill_ , and those who have this skill are called _sorcerers_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01478))_

### 《 **Base Mental Power Cost** 》

- The base mental power cost represents how much mental power is consumed when using that magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01454))_
- As previously explained, this base mental power cost divided by the corresponding rune master skill level equals the actual mental power consumed by the caster. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01454))_

### 《 **Distance** 》

- Basically, magic works on things that the caster can see and are within this distance. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01456))_
- To cast these spells in combat, you must be _engaged_ with the target. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01456))_
- At this time, there is no need to roll dice to determine whether you've touched your opponent, like with an attack hit check (you can touch them automatically). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01456))_
- This represents the distance the magic can reach. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01456))_
- Any exceptions will always be noted in the explanation that follows. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01456))_
- Basically, magic works on things that the caster can see and are within this distance. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01456))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01454))_

> The base mental power cost represents how much mental power is consumed when using that magic. As previously explained, this base mental power cost divided by the corresponding rune master skill level equals the actual mental power consumed by the caster.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01456))_

> If the distance is **touch** , the caster must touch the target with their hand or (for ancient magic) with their magical catalyst (see below).

### 《 **Area** 》

- This represents the target that the magic affects. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01458))_
- It is also possible for casters to cast it on themselves. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01460))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01461))_

> If it works on a **specific space or area** , then everything within that area is targeted. For example, a _Fireball_ explosion deals explosion damage to everything within a 3 meter radius. It makes no difference whether it's the caster's enemies, allies, or the caster. There are many other ways to write this, but it should be clear if you read the description.

### 《 **Duration** 》

- Wounds caused by a _Fireball_ explosion will remain, and if you receive healing by _Cure Wounds_ , those wounds will be recovered instantly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01463))_
- This represents how long the magic will remain in effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01463))_
- This includes **instant** , **18 rounds** , **1 day** , **permanent** , **until focus ends** or **until certain** **conditions are met** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01463))_
- For magic whose duration is _instant_ , the magic itself disappears in an instant, but its effects remain. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01463))_
- Wounds caused by a _Fireball_ explosion will remain, and if you receive healing by _Cure Wounds_ , those wounds will be recovered instantly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01463))_

### 《 **Effect** 》

- Once you've read the detailed description, you'll be able to recall the general effect of the magic just by looking at this. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01465))_
- This is a brief description of the magic's effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01465))_

### 《 **Type** 》

- **Counters (negates):** and **counters (overpowers):** describe magic that can be negated _by_ this magic, while **countered (negated) by:** and **countered (overpowered) by:** describe magic that can negate _this_ magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01467))_
- Magic of the **mind** , **physical ability** , and **weapon enhancement** types also omit _counters_ and _countered by_ , since they are sufficiently described. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01467))_

### 《 **Expansion** 》

### 《 **Resist** 》

- This represents whether the magic can be resisted, and what will happen if it is resisted. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01471))_
- **None** : No resist can be performed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01472))_
- Spells of the _damage_ type will have their strike power reduced by -10 and can no longer critical. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01474))_
- After these details are briefly written, a detailed description of each spell is written. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01475))_
- After these details are briefly written, a detailed description of each spell is written. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01475))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01472))_

> Spells always take their effect.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01473))_

> **Negated Effect** : If resisted, the spell does not take its effect.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01474))_

> **Reduced Effect** : If resisted, the spell only takes an incomplete effect.

### **Restrictions on Using 5.1.11.1 Ancient Magic**

- Ancient magic has powerful effects because it draws its source of power (which is sometimes called _mana_ ) directly from matter, but it is subject to the following two restrictions in order to cast the spells correctly: _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01480))_
- Ancient magic has powerful effects because it draws its source of power (which is sometimes called _mana_ ) directly from matter, but it is subject to the following two restrictions in order to cast the spells correctly: _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01480))_
- Due to complex gestures required, armor is limited to _cloth_ or _soft leather_ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01482))_
- Due to complex gestures required, armor is limited to _cloth_ or _soft leather_ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01482))_
- If you don't obey these two things, you won't be able to use ancient magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01483))_

### **Basic Abilities of the 5.1.11.2 Sorcerer Skill**

### 《 **High Ancient** 》

- Sorcerers can speak, read, and write in high ancient. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01488))_
- It seems that these runes were used in official texts and conversation in the ancient kingdom, but now much of it has been lost, except for the few that remain in spellbooks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01488))_
- It seems that these runes were used in official texts and conversation in the ancient kingdom, but now much of it has been lost, except for the few that remain in spellbooks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01488))_

### 《 **Low Ancient** 》

- This was an everyday language of the ancient kingdom, called **low ancient** , and was the language used by citizens and slaves. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01490))_
- Low ancient was used in informal texts in the ancient kingdom. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01490))_
- Sorcerers can speak, read and write in this language. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01490))_

### **Characteristics of Sorcerers 5.1.11.3 and Ancient Magic**

### 《 **Spellbook** 》

- Otherwise, even those with the greatest minds cannot possibly memorize all the words, actions, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01494))_
- A sorcerer must somehow preserve the ancient magic spells they've learned. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01494))_
- A spellbook is, so to speak, just a handwritten notebook, and the spellbook itself has no magical power of its own. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01495))_
- However, because it's a very important thing, it would be a disaster if it were lost or stolen, so some sorcerers may keep several copies in reserve. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01495))_
- For this reason, it is customary for sorcerers to write these spells down in a book called a **spellbook** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01495))_
- However, because it's a very important thing, it would be a disaster if it were lost or stolen, so some sorcerers may keep several copies in reserve. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01495))_
- When you go on an adventure, or you're in a situation where you'd need to use magic, you'd usually bring your spellbook with you. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01496))_
- A spellbook is used to review them, always confirming and ensuring your memory. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01496))_
- This is because the words and actions of a spell are usually sharply memorized in your head. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01496))_
- This is because the words and actions of a spell are usually sharply memorized in your head. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01496))_
- There are no special restrictions on spellbooks according to the rules. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01497))_

### 《 **Magical Catalyst** 》

- Sorcerers require a special medium to cast spells. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01499))_
- This is a **magical catalyst** , and it is through this that a sorcerer exercises the power of a spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01499))_
- Usually, a specially-made mage's staff is used as a magical catalyst, made from a wooden staff with a _Create Device_ spell cast on it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01499))_
- For example, Laverna, the witch of Orphan, uses an amethyst ring she wears on her right middle finger as her magical catalyst, and Cyanos, leader of the magic knights of Ramliearth, carries a small twig wand in his left hand as a magical catalyst. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01499))_
- For example, Laverna, the witch of Orphan, uses an amethyst ring she wears on her right middle finger as her magical catalyst, and Cyanos, leader of the magic knights of Ramliearth, carries a small twig wand in his left hand as a magical catalyst. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01499))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01496))_

> When you go on an adventure, or you're in a situation where you'd need to use magic, you'd usually bring your spellbook with you. However, no sorcerer looks at their spellbook every time they try to cast a spell. This is because the words and actions of a spell are usually sharply memorized in your head. A spellbook is used to review them, always confirming and ensuring your memory.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01499))_

> This is why you must have a mage's staff as a restriction when using ancient magic.

## 5.1.12 Ancient Magic List

- If magic is cast on the person who casts this spell, that magic will rebound back to the opponent who cast it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02009))_
- At the same time, the effect of _Magic Reflection_ will disappear. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02009))_
- In the case of magic that affects a space, _Magic Reflection_ has no effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02011))_
- However, this can only reflect magic which has an area of effect of _one_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02011))_
- Also, since the reflection is indiscriminate, even magic that’s _favorable_ towards the caster will be reflected. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02011))_
- In addition, spells that nullify magic, such as _Dispel Magic_ , cannot be reflected. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02011))_
- In addition, spells that nullify magic, such as _Dispel Magic_ , cannot be reflected. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02011))_
- However, this can only reflect magic which has an area of effect of _one_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02011))_
- If a caster using _Magic Reflection_ casts other magic on his or herself, the effect of _Magic Reflection_ will be lost at the same time the new magic is cast. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02012))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01843))_

> Each time you use this spell, you can only read _one_ language, if you want to read _two_ unknown languages at the same time, you must _double_ the spell's effect.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02010))_

> If your magic has been reflected, you must resist against your own magic yourself.

### **[ Decrease Weight ]**

- Distance=10 meters Area=One object Duration=18 rounds Effect=Reduce the weight of an object Type=Lost Counters (Negates): _Increase Weight_ Countered (Negated) By: _Increase Weight_ Expansion=Duration, distance, targets, effect (see description) Resist=None _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01550))_

- This spell is used on a single object and has the effect of reducing the target’s weight. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01551))_
- To reduce the weight by _half_ , you must expand it _7 times_ , and to reduce it to _1/10th_ , you must expand it _22 times_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01552))_
- When reducing the weight of an object, it’s reduced by 90% each, the rate of _double_ expansion would be _0.9 x 0.9 = 0.81 times_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01552))_
- This spell’s effect can be expanded. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01552))_
- the required strength can become _-2_ or _-3_ ...... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01552))_
- For other objects, it’ll be reduced by 10% each time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01552))_
- For _triple_ , it’d be _0.9 x 0.9 x 0.9 = 0.721 times_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01552))_
- Also, in this case, required strength can only be reduced to 1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01553))_

- The effect of the spell will disappear in an instant, but the object should burn on its own afterwards. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01572))_

### **[ Magic Protection ]**

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01582))_

> Base Mental Power Cost=4 Distance=Touch

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01583))_

> Area=One specific object

### Duration=Permanent

- If the targeted object is exposed to another spell that's not _detection_ type, it will resist that spell unless the opponent succeeds on a success roll against a target score equal to the final score from when _Magic Protection_ was cast. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01589))_
- This spell has the effect of giving magic resistance to objects that do not have it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01589))_
- If the resist fails, the spell will take its full effect, and furthermore the _Magic Protection_ spell itself will be removed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01589))_
- If the targeted object is exposed to another spell that's not _detection_ type, it will resist that spell unless the opponent succeeds on a success roll against a target score equal to the final score from when _Magic Protection_ was cast. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01589))_
- Even spells with a _resist_ of _none_ can be resisted by _Magic Protection_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01590))_
- For example, if you cast _Kindle_ on a bundle of straw that has _Magic Protection_ cast on it, if _Kindle_ cannot break through the protection of _Magic Protection_ (the final score is insufficient), it won't be able to ignite. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01590))_
- In this case, the spell will take no effect at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01590))_
- For example, if you cast _Kindle_ on a bundle of straw that has _Magic Protection_ cast on it, if _Kindle_ cannot break through the protection of _Magic Protection_ (the final score is insufficient), it won't be able to ignite. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01590))_
- This spell is powerless against spells that belong to _detection_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01591))_
- Also, if you cast it on something that naturally has resistance (humans, fae, youma, etc.), it will have no effect at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01592))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01583))_

> Area=One specific object

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01585))_

> Effect=Gives the targeted object resistance to magic

### **[ Light ]**

- This spell is used on an object or a point in space. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01602))_

### **[ Lock ]**

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01602))_

> This spell is used on an object or a point in space. It has no effect at all when used on living things. Using a single point as a light source, a space with a 10 meter radius is filled with white magical light. The further away you are from the light source, the more the effect of the light will fade. If you cast it on a portable object such as a shield, you can move the light source along with the object.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01604))_

> Base Mental Power Cost=3

### Distance=Touch

- This spell can be used on objects that open and close, such as doors, treasure chest lids, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01608))_

### **2nd Level Ancient Magic List**

### **[ Oak ]**

- This simplified version of a wood golem came to be called an _Oak_ because the material used was many oak tree branches, so that eventually became the name of the spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01612))_
- Or otherwise, they can specify a location, and have the oak attack anyone invading that location. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01612))_
- Oaks can only fight, and the caster must specify whom to attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01612))_
- After the spell's duration expires, the oak will turn back into a branch. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01612))_
- This simplified version of a wood golem came to be called an _Oak_ because the material used was many oak tree branches, so that eventually became the name of the spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01612))_

- Oaks can only fight, and the caster must specify whom to attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01612))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01608, source-range-870a16bc-01612))_

As a result, the door or lid will never open unless you chant the password you chose when casting the spell. This password does _not_ have to be in high ancient. This spell creates a 1-meter tall, goblin-esque wood golem from a tree branch. ### **[ Sense Enemy ]**

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01612))_

> This spell creates a 1-meter tall, goblin-esque wood golem from a tree branch. This simplified version of a wood golem came to be called an _Oak_ because the material used was many oak tree branches, so that eventually became the name of the spell. Oaks can only fight, and the caster must specify whom to attack. Or otherwise, they can specify a location, and have the oak attack anyone invading that location. After the spell's duration expires, the oak will turn back into a branch.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01615))_

> Base Mental Power Cost=6

### Distance=Caster

- However, if the theft is committed in order to cause problems for someone, you will be able to sense that intention. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01620))_
- Aggressive intentions in this case refers to a desire to deal physical or mental damage to the caster, so cases such as simply wanting to steal the caster's money will not be detected. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01620))_
- Aggressive intentions in this case refers to a desire to deal physical or mental damage to the caster, so cases such as simply wanting to steal the caster's money will not be detected. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01620))_

> Type=Detection Expansion=Duration Resist=None

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01620))_

> By casting this spell, the caster can learn of the presence of anything that has aggressive intentions towards them. Aggressive intentions in this case refers to a desire to deal physical or mental damage to the caster, so cases such as simply wanting to steal the caster's money will not be detected. However, if the theft is committed in order to cause problems for someone, you will be able to sense that intention.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-01621))_
