### **[ Reincarnation ]**

### **[ Rescue ]**

- Reborn babies have their ability scores preserved (they have the same ability scores after becoming adults). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02925))_
- This spell is cast immediately after an individual's death. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02925))_
- At that stage they will be able to use all their previous memories and skills. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02925))_
- The soul of the target of this spell will be reborn as a baby in another location within one week. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02925))_
- When they remember their previous personality depends on the final score from when the spell was cast (see Table 5-8: Reincarnation Memory). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02925))_
- This spell is cast immediately after an individual's death. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02925))_
- Reborn babies have their ability scores preserved (they have the same ability scores after becoming adults). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02925))_
- The soul of the target of this spell will be reborn as a baby in another location within one week. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02925))_
- When you cast this spell, the hand of the gods will help the caster and their companions escape to a safe location (the caster's home, a temple, a familiar city, etc.). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02926))_
- If you _double_ the mental power consumed, it will target _20_ people within a _14_ meter radius, and if you _triple_ it, it will target _30_ people within a _17_ meter radius. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02927))_
- If you _double_ the mental power consumed, it will target _20_ people within a _14_ meter radius, and if you _triple_ it, it will target _30_ people within a _17_ meter radius. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02927))_
- The caster can remove anything from the target of this spell, even within the area of effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02928))_
- Conversely, you _cannot_ force this spell to take effect on those who do _not_ agree to it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02928))_
- The caster can remove anything from the target of this spell, even within the area of effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02928))_
- You can help them recover their memory through constant supervision, so you can roll 2D once every year, and if you roll a 10 or greater, they will be able to regain their personality. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02929))_

### **Pharis Special Holy Magic List [ Sense Evil ] (Spell Level=1)**

- Think of this spell _not_ as constantly detecting people called _villains_ , but only the _moment_ when someone is thinking _evil thoughts_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02936))_
- _Sense Evil_ is a spell that allows you to see through those who harbor (so-called evil) feelings that would destroy the justice and order taught by Pharis. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02936))_
- In other words, this spell works on the _evil intent_ of those that have minds, but is _not_ a spell that detects _people_ who commonly have evil intent (these kinds of people are usually called villains). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02936))_
- Think of this spell _not_ as constantly detecting people called _villains_ , but only the _moment_ when someone is thinking _evil thoughts_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02936))_
- This is a spell that erases unnatural beings belonging to spirits, undead, daemons, and magical creatures from the material world. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02941))_
- This spell has no effect on humans, fae, youma, titans, mythical beasts, magical beasts, animals, titan creatures, plants, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02943))_
- However, creatures summoned by _summon_ dragon roar magic can be targeted by this spell _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02943))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02931))_

> The spells listed here can only be used by those who believe in a specific god among characters with the priest skill.

### **[ Jihad ] (Spell Level=10)**

- This spell is used when a high priest of Pharis discovers a great evil that absolutely cannot be overlooked. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02951))_
- Gather as many Pharis believers as you can to a temple, and cast this in front of them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02951))_
- As a result, anyone who agrees to the spell, including the person who cast it, will experience the following effects. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02951))_
- Those with fighter skill 0 will be temporarily given 2 levels) 2. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02952))_
- Absolute obedience to the caster's commands (even if it is a suicidal act) It has no impact at all on those who do not agree to the spell, but _no_ devout follower of Pharis would not agree to the high priest's spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02952))_

### **Myrii Special Holy Magic List**

### **[ Battle Song ] (Spell Level=5)**

- Type=Mind, target score Expansion=Final score Resist=None As the god of war, Myrii is said to lend his power to those who fight justly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02960))_
- Those under the influence of this spell gain a +2 bonus to attack power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02960))_
- Priests of Myrii can receive Myrii's assistance by offering a song in holy prayer to Myrii. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02960))_
- Basically, this spell takes effect on anything within the reach of the caster's singing voice, but the caster can also exclude any target from being targeted by the spell when casting the spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02960))_
- Basically, this spell takes effect on anything within the reach of the caster's singing voice, but the caster can also exclude any target from being targeted by the spell when casting the spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02960))_

### **[ Divine Armor ] (Spell Level=7)**

### **[ Divine Weapon ] (Spell Level=7)**

- with this spell, arrows are automatically generated as well. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02969))_
- There is no required strength, and its strike power is treated as 30 (if the caster uses it as a twohanded weapon, its strike power is _35_ ). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02969))_
- This spell has no effect if the caster already has something else in their hand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02969))_
- This _weapon_ can also take effect against monsters that can only be wounded by silver or magic weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02969))_
- When you cast this spell, a vaguely glowing weapon-like force field appears in the caster's hand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02969))_
- Also, it's not possible to give this _weapon_ to someone else. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02969))_
- This _weapon_ can also take effect against monsters that can only be wounded by silver or magic weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02969))_

### **[ Weak Point ] (Spell Level=3)**

- Effect=Find the target's weak point, reducing the critical target of anyone who attacks them by -1 Type=Target score Expansion=Final score, duration, distance, targets Resist=Negated effect _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02979))_
- This spell shows you your target's weak point and guides your attack there. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02981))_
- This effect is expressed as a -1 to the attacker's critical target. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02981))_
- In other words, the normal critical target of 10 becomes _9_ (9 to _8_ for a thief). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02981))_
- Note that the critical target can only be lowered for _weapon_ attacks, while the critical target of damage magic _cannot_ be lowered. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02982))_
- Note that the critical target can only be lowered for _weapon_ attacks, while the critical target of damage magic _cannot_ be lowered. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02982))_
- In this case, even if the defense roll result is not only 2 (double ones) but also _3_ , no damage reduction will be possible. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02983))_
- In this case, even if the defense roll result is not only 2 (double ones) but also _3_ , no damage reduction will be possible. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02983))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02975))_

> Base Mental Power Cost=15 Distance=10 meters Area=One Duration=18 rounds

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02981))_

> This spell shows you your target's weak point and guides your attack there. This effect is expressed as a -1 to the attacker's critical target. In other words, the normal critical target of 10 becomes _9_ (9 to _8_ for a thief).

### **[ Legend ] (Spell Level=7)**

- The caster can make the target of the spell memorize specific text without error. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02990))_
- This is a spell for passing down oral instruction. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02990))_
- Also, it must be pronounced and heard directly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02991))_
- This spell allows you to increase the amount of text your target memorizes by expanding its effect (for example, they can memorize up to _20_ sentences of text if you consume _double_ the mental power). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02991))_
- The text must be limited to 10 sentences. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02991))_

- In other words, text of this amount can be easily memorized. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02992))_
- For reference, this spell's description, including this last part, is 8 sentences long. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-02992))_

### **[ Guard Trading ] (Spell Level=5)**

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03002))_

> A _defensive_ success roll should be judged by whether it means you _luckily survived_ . If the game master is unsure, treat it as something where it _can't_ be used. In particular, it can _never_ be used on checks for hitting others with attacks or checks for casting magic.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03005))_

> Base Mental Power Cost=25

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03008))_

> This is a spell for protecting trade. If you try to engage in an illegal business transaction with the caster who cast this spell (e.g. trying to sell for more than the intended price, trying to buy for less than the intended price, etc.), the caster will discover it immediately. Also, when someone tries to steal or take something from the caster, it will be discovered because it means they're trying to buy it for _free = less than the intended price_ .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03006))_

> Distance=Caster Area=Within the caster's view Duration=1 day

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03008))_

### **[ Peace ]**

### **(Spell Level=3)**

- Therefore, priests of Marfa can use this spell to take away the feelings of hatred that have arisen in each other's hearts and prevent them from fighting. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03019))_

## 5.2 Common Runes

- You can determine the quality of a metal by its color, its purity by its strength, and the amount by what you can see in range. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03037))_
- No material can block the effects of this spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03037))_
- _Sense Metal_ is a spell that allows you to sense metals or veins containing metals in the surrounding area, as well as their type, purity, and amount. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03037))_
- For the duration of the spell, the priest of Braki will see their surroundings in a unique color. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03037))_
- **Common runes** were invented by Mana Rai, the great sage of Oran, and it involves putting the power of ancient magic into items (mainly rings) and activating it using a password in _common_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03051))_
- Common runes, like other magic, consume mental power when used. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03052))_
- The amount of mental power consumed is determined by each common rune. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03052))_
- Common runes can also be expanded by consuming extra mental power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03053))_
- However, no matter how high your rune master skill is, you _cannot_ reduce the amount of mental power consumed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03053))_
- Common runes can be used by anyone who knows the password. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03053))_
- However, increasing the final score is the one thing you _cannot_ do. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03053))_
- When using common runes, if magic power is required for a game procedure, treat it as **0** regardless of the user. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03053))_
- Theoretically, any ancient magic can be made into common runes, but the sorcerers' guild (academy of sages) limits what gets put on the market, to prevent the reputation of magic and sorcerers from being damaged due to misuse of magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03054))_
- Theoretically, any ancient magic can be made into common runes, but the sorcerers' guild (academy of sages) limits what gets put on the market, to prevent the reputation of magic and sorcerers from being damaged due to misuse of magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03054))_

## 5.3 Spellsongs

- It only appears on the market when a character discovers it, or has already discovered it, through an adventure (which means it is no longer a lost spell). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03060))_
- It only appears on the market when a character discovers it, or has already discovered it, through an adventure (which means it is no longer a lost spell). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03060))_
- became common runes, it’s obvious that thieves would use them to commit evil deeds. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03061))_
- In such cases, please make sure to make a decision such as _The sorcerers' guild would never allow them on the market because they'd bring down the reputation of magic and sorcerers_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03061))_
- In such cases, please make sure to make a decision such as _The sorcerers' guild would never allow them on the market because they'd bring down the reputation of magic and sorcerers_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03061))_
- Also, when creating a new common rune, its mental power consumption is the same as the base mental power cost of ancient magic, and the price should be _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03063))_
- However, instead of magic power, the baseline score is _bard skill level + mental power bonus (_ _**not** intelligence bonus!)_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03067))_
- A **spellsong** is a song with magical power that was created during the ancient kingdom period. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03068))_
- Those with the bard skill can use magic, although weak, by singing these spellsongs. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03068))_
- Also, the effect of a spellsong _cannot_ be expanded. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03069))_
- Using spellsongs does _not_ consume a bard's mental power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03069))_
- However, bards are never called rune masters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03070))_
- A spellsong remains in effect as long as the bard sings it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03071))_
- However, once the song is interrupted, the effect disappears. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03071))_
- However, unlike _focus_ , it’s _not_ interrupted by damage dealt to life force or mental power (unless, of course, you fall unconscious as a result of the damage). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03071))_
- Resistance rolls against spellsongs are generally made only once at the beginning of the song (depending on the spellsong, they may be on the fourth round). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03071))_
- Once you successfully resist, you do not need to make any further resistance rolls and will not suffer from the effect, even if the spellsong continues. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03071))_
- Resistance rolls against spellsongs are generally made only once at the beginning of the song (depending on the spellsong, they may be on the fourth round). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03071))_
- However, unlike _focus_ , it’s _not_ interrupted by damage dealt to life force or mental power (unless, of course, you fall unconscious as a result of the damage). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03071))_
- Furthermore, you must sing the lyrics in high ancient at the same time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03072))_
- This instrument must be unique to each bard, and the spellsong cannot be used when playing another instrument. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03072))_
- To use a spellsong, you must play an instrument. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03072))_
- You can learn _one_ spellsong per bard level. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03073))_
- However, it has no effect on those who are _unable_ to hear. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03073))_
- Every time your level goes up, you have to learn a new spellsong. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03073))_

### **5.3.1 Spellsong List**

- The types and effects of spellsongs are shown below. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03075))_
- On the list, spellsongs with the note that says _mind_ are spellsongs that affect the mind of the listener. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03076))_
- These can be considered in the same way as magic that When a bard uses the ability of a spellsong, you resolve the effect in the around _mental power consumed x 1000 gamels_ as a guideline. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03076))_

### **Filling out the Character Sheet**

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03076))_

These can be considered in the same way as magic that When a bard uses the ability of a spellsong, you resolve the effect in the around _mental power consumed x 1000 gamels_ as a guideline.

> In other words, each one _counters (overpowers):_ and cannot take an effect at the same time as magic or other spellsongs that also belong to _mind_ .

### **Early Bird (Mind)**

- This spellsong has the effect of waking up sleeping creatures. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03081))_
- At the same time, as long as this song continues, listening creatures will not be able to sleep. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03081))_

### **Curiosity (Mind)**

- This spellsong has the effect of arousing the curiosity of those who hear it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03083))_
- Those who fail to resist will be tempted to catch a glimpse of the performer. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03083))_
- The result is that they'll come out of their hiding place and reveal themselves to the performer. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03083))_
- It has no effect on those who can already see the performer. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03083))_
- The resistance roll is made on the fourth round after the song begins (count the _beginning_ of the song as the _first_ round, and make the roll when the bard's _action order_ occurs on the _fourth_ round). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03083))_
- The resistance roll is made on the fourth round after the song begins (count the _beginning_ of the song as the _first_ round, and make the roll when the bard's _action order_ occurs on the _fourth_ round). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03083))_

### **Summon Small Animals (Mind)**

- You can summon any hand-sized mammals or birds (mice, squirrels, small cats, small birds, etc.). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03085))_
- This spell has the effect of summoning specific small animals nearby. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03085))_
- If the summoned small animals are attacked, the effect of the spellsong will be broken and they will all run away. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03086))_
- 109 within earshot of the song will gather around the bard. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03086))_
- 109 within earshot of the song will gather around the bard. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03086))_

### **Sing (Mind)**

- Those affected will not be restricted in their actions, but as long as the spellsong continues, they will _also_ be singing and will not be able to cast magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03088))_
- However, it has _no_ effect on creatures that do _not_ sing. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03088))_
- There’s no need to remember the lyrics. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03088))_
- Anyone who hears this spellsong will start singing the same lyrics along with the bard's song. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03088))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03085))_

### **Dance (Mind)**

- Those who hear this spellsong will begin to move their limbs and dance according to the melody on their own. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03090))_
- You will also be unable to use most skills’ abilities. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03090))_
- Those affected suffer a -4 penalty to attack power and evasion speed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03090))_
- Since you can't use your musical instrument, you can't use spellsongs either. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03090))_
- Ancient magic and spirit magic cannot be cast. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03090))_

### **Charm (Mind)**

- As long as the spellsong continues, those who are charmed will listen to the song in ecstasy. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03092))_
- This spellsong has the effect of charming those who hear it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03092))_
- Those who are, are not charmed by the bard him or herself, but rather charmed by their singing voice and performance. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03092))_
- What you want them to do must be carefully woven into the lyrics. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03093))_
- This weaving of lyrics must be in a language that is understandable to those who hear it (conversely, it does _not_ have to be in high ancient). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03093))_

### **Nostalgia (Mind)**

- Those who fail to resist will feel the urge to return to their roost and leave. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03103))_
- However, it has _no_ effect on those who do not _have_ a roost. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03103))_
- This is a spellsong that evokes feelings of nostalgia in the hearts of those who hear it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03103))_
- The effect begins on the fourth round after starting. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03103))_
- The same applies if the place where the person is listening to the spellsong _is_ their roost. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03103))_
- The effect begins on the fourth round after starting. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03103))_
- The effect of this song lasts for one hour even after the spellsong is no longer heard. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03104))_
- However, if the person returns to their roost within one hour, the effect will vanish at that point. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03104))_

- The effect of this song lasts for one hour even after the spellsong is no longer heard. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03104))_

### **Morale (Mind)**

- Those who hear this spell will be able to muster up the courage within their own hearts and fight bravely against the enemy. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03106))_
- The resulting check score will be attack power (points) plus 1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03106))_
- Those who hear this spell will be able to muster up the courage within their own hearts and fight bravely against the enemy. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03106))_

### **Peace (Mind)**

- Those affected suffer a -1 penalty to attack power (points). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03108))_
- This spellsong has the opposite effect of _Morale_ , causing those who hear it to lose their will to fight. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03108))_

### **Lullaby (Mind)**

- This is a spellsong that puts the listener to sleep. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03110))_
- If the performance is interrupted _before_ it is completed, the effect of the spellsong will disappear at that point, but once the spellsong _is_ completed, you will enter a natural state of sleep, so there is no need to play it any further. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03110))_
- Once it starts, the effect begins on the fourth round (resistance rolls are made at this point), and the spell is complete after 12 rounds. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03110))_
- Once it starts, the effect begins on the fourth round (resistance rolls are made at this point), and the spell is complete after 12 rounds. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03110))_

### **Healing**

- Every time you listen to this spellsong for one hour, you can recover 1 point of life force (points). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03112))_
- you will wake up immediately. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03112))_
- If you interrupt the spellsong midway through, there will be no effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03112))_

### **Requiem**

- With a song that mourns the souls of the dead, it has the effect of weakening the undead. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03114))_
- For undead that fail to resist, roll 2D and look at Table 5-10: Requiem to determine the effect it has on them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03114))_
- The effect begins on the fourth round after starting the spellsong. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03114))_
- The effect begins on the fourth round after starting the spellsong. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03114))_
- If this spellsong is sung to the deceased for over an hour during their funeral, the deceased will _never_ become undead. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03115))_

### **Resistance**

- This spellsong activates the listener's physical and mental functions, giving them +1 to life force and mental power, as well as the resistance rolls of each. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03117))_

### **Restore Mental Power (Mind)**

- Every time you listen to this spell for one hour, you can recover 1 point of mental power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03119))_
- If you interrupt the spellsong midway through, there will be no effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03119))_
- Those who wish to take part in the effect of this spellsong must remain completely relaxed for the duration of the performance. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03119))_

### **Vibrato**

- This spell is effective on objects that break due to vibrations, such as thin glass, ceramics, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03122))_
- These objects will shatter on the fourth round after the spellsong begins. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03122))_
- This spell is effective on objects that break due to vibrations, such as thin glass, ceramics, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03122))_
- These objects will shatter on the fourth round after the spellsong begins. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03122))_

### **Filling out the Character Sheet**

- Characters with the bard skill write the names of the spellsongs they have learned in the designated field ( _Spellsongs_ ) on their character sheet. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03125))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03195))_

> The side that's following must use _thief skill level + agility bonus_ as their baseline score, while the side being followed must use _adventurer level + intelligence bonus_ , and success or failure is determined by comparing the final scores of their success rolls.

### **[** ⑭ **Disguise [Dexterity] (No equipment restriction)**

- Things like height and physique can be changed to a certain extent, but there are still limits. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03198))_
- This is the ability to change one's face and appearance using special makeup. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03198))_
- Things like height and physique can be changed to a certain extent, but there are still limits. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03198))_
- Especially when disguised as someone whose characteristics the thief doesn't know in detail, it's best to add a difficult modifier. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03199))_
- The game master should add modifiers to success rolls to account for the unnaturalness of the disguise (such as disguising as the opposite sex, etc.). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03199))_
- The game master should add modifiers to success rolls to account for the unnaturalness of the disguise (such as disguising as the opposite sex, etc.). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03199))_

> This is the ability to change one's face and appearance using special makeup. It's easy to just act like a completely different person, but it's difficult to disguise yourself as someone specific. Things like height and physique can be changed to a certain extent, but there are still limits.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03199))_

> If the thief is disguised as a specific person and they encounter an acquaintance of that person, they must make a success roll to determine success or failure.

### **[** ⑮ **Treasure Appraisal [Intelligence] ]**

### **(No equipment restriction, not retriable)**

- This can also be used to judge whether someone is rich or not based on their attire, demeanor, and how much their purse bulges. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03202))_
- This is the ability to instantly judge whether something is valuable as a treasure or not, and which one is likely to be more valuable. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03202))_
- However, magical items and books whose contents need to be understood are completely excluded. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03202))_
- The game master should set the target score each time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03202))_

> If the thief is disguised as a specific person and they encounter an acquaintance of that person, they must make a success roll to determine success or failure. The game master should add modifiers to success rolls to account for the unnaturalness of the disguise (such as disguising as the opposite sex, etc.). Especially when disguised as someone whose characteristics the thief doesn't know in detail, it's best to add a difficult modifier.

> If you take your time, you can determine specific prices for items such as jewelry, gold and silver, as well as works of art.

### **[** ⑯ **Find/Sense Trap [Intelligence]] (No equipment restriction, not retriable)**

- This is the ability to sense and find traps set indoors, such as doors, hallways, boxes, drawers, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03204))_
- This is the ability to sense and find traps set indoors, such as doors, hallways, boxes, drawers, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03204))_
- This check (success roll) is based on _difficulty_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03205))_
- In the case of a success, please accurately tell them of the presence of the trap and the difficulty to disarm it, and in the case of a failure, or if a trap is not present in the first place, make sure to tell them that it cannot be found. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03205))_
- The **find** ability is used by the thief to actively identify a location and determine if a trap is set there. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03205))_
- Never say _no_ , even when there are no traps. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03205))_
- This is determined by making a success roll just before the trap is about to go off. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03206))_
- For _sense_ , make the difficulty the same as _find_ , but impose a -4 modifier on the player side. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03206))_
- Traps that have been successfully _sensed_ can always be found by attempting to _find_ them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03206))_
- **Sense** is the ability to sense the presence of a trap based on past experience and knowledge without actively taking any action. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03206))_
- This is determined by making a success roll just before the trap is about to go off. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03206))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03205))_

> The **find** ability is used by the thief to actively identify a location and determine if a trap is set there. This check (success roll) is based on _difficulty_ . In the case of a success, please accurately tell them of the presence of the trap and the difficulty to disarm it, and in the case of a failure, or if a trap is not present in the first place, make sure to tell them that it cannot be found. Never say _no_ , even when there are no traps.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03206))_

> If successful, the presence of a trap will be sensed at a perilous point, and the game master must inform the player of the type of trap.

### **[** ⑰ **Remove/Set Trap [Dexterity] ]**

### **(Equipment restriction, not retriable)**

- Removal also involves attempting a success roll against the trap's difficulty. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03209))_
- You can attempt to **remove** traps discovered through _find_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03209))_
- Unless the game master has specifically set traps that are _easy to find but difficult to remove_ , or conversely, _difficult to find but easy to remove_ , the difficulty of finding and removing should be the same. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03210))_
- Traps that you fail to find _cannot_ be removed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03210))_
- The effect of an activated trap depends on the type of trap. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03210))_
- Unless the game master has specifically set traps that are _easy to find but difficult to remove_ , or conversely, _difficult to find but easy to remove_ , the difficulty of finding and removing should be the same. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03210))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03209))_

> You can attempt to **remove** traps discovered through _find_ . Removal also involves attempting a success roll against the trap's difficulty.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03210))_

> If you fail, the trap will be activated if the dice roll was 6 or more beneath the target score.

## 6.3 Ranger Skill

- If a third party attempts to _sense_ , _find_ , or _remove_ a trap set by a player, the game master must attempt a success roll on their behalf. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03213))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03213))_

> For **set** , you can set traps up to a difficulty equal to your thief skill level without failing (you cannot set traps of a higher level).

### **[** ② **First-Aid [-] ]**

- Owners of the ranger skill demonstrate a variety of abilities in outdoor activities, as explained below. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03217))_

### **(No equipment restriction)**

- If an adventurer's life force falls to 0 or less due to wounds, it will not recover naturally and there is a risk of death if left untreated. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03219))_
- **First-aid** provided by a ranger will restore your life force to 1 point, allowing you to regain consciousness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03219))_
- It cannot fail, but it will take about 30 minutes of game time, and it cannot be done while in a state where you can't focus, such as in the middle of combat, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03219))_
- It cannot fail, but it will take about 30 minutes of game time, and it cannot be done while in a state where you can't focus, such as in the middle of combat, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03219))_
- The armor you wear must have a required strength of up to _half_ your own strength, and must be ring mail or non-metal _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03220))_
- The ranger skill is also limited by equipment, just like the thief skill, if you want to make full use of its abilities. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03220))_
- The ranger skill is also limited by equipment, just like the thief skill, if you want to make full use of its abilities. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03220))_
- The ranger skill has some similar abilities to the thief skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03221))_
- However, while the thief skill can be used both outdoors and indoors, the ranger skill is mostly useful only outdoors. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03221))_
- However, while the thief skill can be used both outdoors and indoors, the ranger skill is mostly useful only outdoors. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03221))_

### **[** ③ **Camouflage**

- ① Footprint ⑨ Map Creation Tracking ② First Aid ⑩ Weather Prediction ③ Camouflage ⑪ Plant and Animal Check ④ Listen ⑫ Projectile Use ⑤ Sense Danger ⑬ Follow ⑥ Stealthy Step ⑭ Surprise Attack ⑦ Survivalism ⑮ Find/Sense Trap ⑧ Search ⑯ Remove/Set Trap _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03223))_

### **[Intelligence] ]**

- **(Equipment restriction)** This is the ability to hide behind rocks, hide in bushes, or use such things to hide something. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03225))_
- Since it's an ability that uses natural terrain, it cannot be used indoors. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03225))_

### **[** ④ **Listen [Intelligence] ]**

### **(Equipment restriction)**

- Rangers can listen to even the smallest sounds and can choose the sound they want to hear from a wide range of sounds. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03229))_
- This is handed the same way as the thief's ability. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03229))_

### **[** ① **Footprint Tracking [Intelligence] ]**

### **(No equipment restriction, not retriable)**

- This is the ability to track footprints left on the ground. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03232))_
- The game master determines the difficulty by taking into account factors such as the condition of the ground and the amount of time that has passed since the footprints were made. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03232))_
- The game master determines the difficulty by taking into account factors such as the condition of the ground and the amount of time that has passed since the footprints were made. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03232))_

### **[** ⑤ **Sense Danger [Intelligence]]**

### **(No equipment restriction)**

- Rangers acquire a good intuition, which can be called a sixth sense. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03235))_
- The target score is determined by the game master depending on the situation, but in general it should be set high. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03235))_
- This ability is the ability used to sense when danger is approaching. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03235))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03232))_

> This is the ability to track footprints left on the ground. The game master determines the difficulty by taking into account factors such as the condition of the ground and the amount of time that has passed since the footprints were made.

### **(Equipment restriction)**

- Rangers can walk silently and unnoticed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03238))_

- This is the ability to search for hidden things. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03244))_

- Rangers have a good sense of direction and can map out the places they've been. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03246))_

### **[** ⑫ **Projectile Use [Dexterity, Strength] ]**

### **(No equipment restriction)**

- The ranger skill is treated the same as the fighter skill (attack power, bonus damage modifiers) _only_ when attacking with projectiles. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03257))_
- When using the thief skill for defense, you must be lightly armed (required strength of weapons and armor must be _half_ your own strength or less). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03257))_
- The ranger skill is _not_ relevant to the use of or defense from melee weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03257))_

### **[** ⑬ **Follow [Agility] (Equipment restriction)**

- However, it can _only_ be used in nature. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03259))_
- It _cannot_ be used indoors or in a city. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03259))_
- This handled the same way as the thief skill's ability of the same name. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03259))_

### **[** ⑭ **Surprise Attack [Intelligence]] (No equipment restriction)**

- The ranger skill is used for surprise attacks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03261))_

### **[** ⑮ **Find/Sense Trap [Intelligence]] (No equipment restriction, not retriable)**

- This is basically the same ability as that of the thief skill, but it _only_ applies to traps that use outdoor nature. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03263))_
- This includes those that are made by tying grass into a ring, or are hung from a tree using string, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03263))_
- Indoor mechanical traps cannot be _found/sensed_ with the ranger skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03263))_

### **[** ⑯ **Remove/Set Trap [Dexterity]] (Equipment restriction, not retriable)**

- Indoor mechanical traps cannot be _removed_ or _set_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03265))_

## 6.4 Sage Skill

- Academic fields in _Sword World_ are undifferentiated, and owners of this skill are skilled in all disciplines. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03270))_
- Many sorcerers have this skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03270))_
- The sage skill represents a comprehensive understanding and depth of knowledge in all academic fields. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03270))_
- As an optional rule, you can set a target score or difficulty for text or conversations so that it cannot be understood if you fail a success roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03272))_
- Even in the same language, being able to read and speak is different, so if you want to become proficient in _one_ language, you need _2_ levels of experience. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03272))_
- However, _runes_ cannot be chosen. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03272))_
- The player chooses which language to learn, along with whether they will _speak_ or _read_ it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03272))_
- You can also _write_ in any language that you can read and speak. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03272))_
- Characters who cannot read common must _first_ learn how to read common _before_ learning other languages. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03274))_
- There are limitations to learning languages through the sage skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03274))_
- Then, a character who cannot read low ancient (but _has_ learned common) must learn to read low ancient _before_ learning any other language. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03274))_
- Then, a character who cannot read low ancient (but _has_ learned common) must learn to read low ancient _before_ learning any other language. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03274))_
- There are limitations to learning languages through the sage skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03274))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03270))_

> There is no requirement that you have to have the sage skill and the sorcerer skill at the same time, but if you _do_ acquire the sage skill and the sorcerer skill at the same time, you will receive a _bonus_ on experience points consumed.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03273))_

> The sage skill includes the following abilities:

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03274))_

> If you can read both common _and_ low ancient, you are free to learn subsequent languages.

### **[** ① **Monster Check [Intelligence]] (Not retriable)**

- This includes knowledge of the various monsters that live in Alecrast. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03283))_
- **[** ③ **Astrology [Intelligence] ] (Not retriable)** Sages have a wide range of knowledge. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03283))_
- From the movement of celestial bodies, you can know the signs of events that will occur in the near future. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03284))_
- Signs should be given as rough information about the incident. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03284))_
- It's enough to let you know that a king passes away or that a major war is about to happen (you can think of it as just a headline in a newspaper, etc.). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03284))_
- However, this ability _only_ allows you to know about events related to the country or the world, and when _no_ events occur, _no_ signs appear. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03284))_
- It is entirely up to the game master to decide what kind of sign there is. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03284))_
- Each monster has a score called _rarity_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03285))_
- Using _sage skill level + intelligence bonus_ as the baseline score, make a success roll with this rarity as the target score, and if you succeed, you know the monster. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03285))_
- If the check fails, the character has no knowledge of the monster. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03286))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03283))_

> When a sage sees a monster or hears its name, this ability may allow them to discover its true identity.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03284))_

> You can also set a difficulty and ask for a success roll.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03285))_

> Each monster has a score called _rarity_ . Using _sage skill level + intelligence bonus_ as the baseline score, make a success roll with this rarity as the target score, and if you succeed, you know the monster.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03286))_

> Even if the _player_ knows, they must treat it as if they had no prior knowledge.

### **[** ② **Languages [Intelligence]]**

- Sages can learn different languages depending on their skill level. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03288))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03285))_

## 6.5 Bard Skill

- Those with the bard skill are knowledgeable about ancient folklore and poetry, and make a living by performing them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03307))_

### **[** ⑤ **Research [Intelligence] ]**

### **[** ⑥ **Treasure Appraisal [Intelligence] ] (Not retriable)**

- **(Not retriable)** When a sage fails a success roll on a check such as _monster check_ , _treasure appraisal_ (described below), or _knowledge_ and is unable to figure it out, the sage can later find out by **researching** literature. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03299))_
- **(Not retriable)** When a sage fails a success roll on a check such as _monster check_ , _treasure appraisal_ (described below), or _knowledge_ and is unable to figure it out, the sage can later find out by **researching** literature. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03299))_
- However, if the difference from the target score is 6 or greater, there _is_ a possibility of this kind of misunderstanding. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03300))_
- This ability is used to know the value and utility of treasure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03300))_
- The _rarity_ of that treasure becomes the target score for a success roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03300))_
- For standard ancient kingdom treasure-- many of which are magical items --the target score is around 15. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03300))_
- If the true value is 100 gamels, you will _not_ think that it's worth 10,000 gamels just because you failed the check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03300))_
- If the true value is 100 gamels, you will _not_ think that it's worth 10,000 gamels just because you failed the check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03300))_
- Also, if you don't have a place with a wealth of literature (the best place is the academy of sages = sorcerers' guild in each city), research itself is impossible. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03301))_
- Research basically takes about one week for each item (this may be increased or decreased at the game master's discretion). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03301))_
- During this time, you cannot do much other than research. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03301))_
- When something they didn't know (something that failed) becomes a success, the sage has found a description of it in the literature and has access to that knowledge. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03302))_
- Basically, these connoisseurs and specialty shops will only exist in large towns. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03303))_
- Also, any item can be picked up by a specialty shop for half the appraised value. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03303))_
- The price value determined by this ability is the price that a connoisseur (or obsessor) would accept, i.e., the _maximum_ price. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03303))_
- Basically, these connoisseurs and specialty shops will only exist in large towns. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03303))_

- **((Appraisal only) Not retriable)** This ability represents knowledge of poisons and medicines. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03318))_
- **((Appraisal only) Not retriable)** This ability represents knowledge of poisons and medicines. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03318))_

- Regardless of the result or dice roll, you will need materials and at least one week to attempt to create a medicine. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03320))_

- Therefore, game masters should _not_ allow PCs to create or use poisons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03321))_
- Therefore, game masters should _not_ allow PCs to create or use poisons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03321))_

### **[** ③ **Languages (Spoken)**

### **[Intelligence] ]**

- Unlike the sage skill, there is _no_ restriction on the languages that can be learned. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03326))_
- This ability is almost the same as the sage skill's ability of the same name, but it _only_ applies to speaking, not reading. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03326))_
- In other words, bards can converse in languages other than their native language, but they _cannot_ read (or write, of course) text. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03326))_
- However, of course, you cannot learn runes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03326))_
- Unlike the sage skill, there is _no_ restriction on the languages that can be learned. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03326))_

- If you fail, you will fall off the horse. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03341))_

### **[** ⑤ **Folklore Knowledge [Intelligence] ]**

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03331))_

> **(Not retriable)** By the nature of their business, a bard is familiar with myths and folklore passed down in various regions, as well as ancient poetry. This is because telling these stories is an important part of their job. Also, some of these legends include things that can serve as clues for your adventures. For example, a saga that tells of a battle between a monster and a hero may contain information about the monster's weakness. Legends about the ancient kingdom can sometimes lead adventurers to ancient ruins.

### **[** ③ **Ship Steering [Dexterity]]**

- However, sailing ships require the full-fledged _sailor skill_ , and adventurer level cannot be used as a substitute. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03366))_
- Adventurers can handle small boats using oars or poles. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03366))_
- Note that when a thief climbs up or down a wall or cliff _without_ using a rope, the conditions marked * are not possible. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03367))_
- Any adventurer can operate a small vessel (boat, raft, etc.) that is operated using oars or poles. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03368))_
- In special cases such as _avoiding collision with an approaching ship_ or _surviving rapids_ , the adventurer operating the ship must succeed on a success roll using _adventurer level + dexterity bonus_ as the baseline score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03368))_
- The difficulty is determined by the game master. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03368))_
- If you fail, the boat will capsize. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03368))_

### **Example:**

- The target score is 10+2+4= _16_ , taking into account the conditions of _vertical cliff with a foothold_ and _chain mail._ In other words, if he rolls 2D and gets a score of 10 or greater, he’ll succeed at climbing. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03370))_
- The baseline score is _adventurer level + agility bonus_ = _6_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03370))_

- If you fall, please refer to Falling Damage below to determine the damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03378))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03370))_

> An adventurer with adventurer level 4 and an agility bonus +2, attempts to climb a vertical natural cliff using a rope. He's wearing chain mail armor. The baseline score is _adventurer level + agility bonus_ = _6_ . The target score is 10+2+4= _16_ , taking into account the conditions of _vertical cliff with a foothold_ and _chain mail._ In other words, if he rolls 2D and gets a score of 10 or greater, he’ll succeed at climbing.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03372))_

> If this adventurer is a thief, he can remove and discard the heavy armor he is wearing, and he can use his thief skill to attempt the climb more safely.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03374, source-range-870a16bc-03377))_

> First of all, climbing, which is a common ability of adventurers, always requires assistance such as rope. Without such a thing, it’s _not_ possible to climb 121 time, only double ones will result in a failure.

> A check must be made every 10 meters. This is then treated as if you fell from the height you climbed or the height remaining on the way up. ### **Example 1:**

- 5 - 2 = _3 meters_ is added to the 20 meters from the two attempts, which means he has climbed **23 meters** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03383))_
- Now he rolls 2D and gets a 5. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03383))_
- The first and second attempts are successful, but the third attempt fails. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03383))_
- In this case, a success roll is attempted three times. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03383))_
- 5 - 2 = _3 meters_ is added to the 20 meters from the two attempts, which means he has climbed **23 meters** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03383))_

### **Example 2:**

- If the result of the 2D rolled to check the distance the adventurer climbed was _double ones_ or _3_ , the adventurer would've fallen from a height of _20_ or _21 meters_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03385))_

### **Falling Damage**

- If an adventurer falls, damage will be determined as follows. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03387))_
- First, _fall height x 3_ is the base damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03388))_
- The adventurer will suffer this damage to their life force. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03388))_
- If the defense roll comes up _double ones_ , all damage reduction is lost, including adventurer level. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03389))_
- However, an adventurer can reduce this damage depending on their adventurer level and armor. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03389))_
- The base damage minus this damage reduction is the actual damage the adventurer suffers. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03390))_
- On the other hand, if you have something like spikes sticking out, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03391))_
- **Falling damage** = fall height x 3 - (defense roll result + adventurer level) Base falling damage (fall height x 3) is for falling on normal ground. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03391))_
- This decision should be made by the game master. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03391))_
- Owners of the thief skill can greatly reduce this damage with their _tumble_ ability. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03392))_
- If the result of this subtraction is 0 or less, even if the thief falls, they can land cleanly on their feet or stand up in one motion. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03392))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03387, source-range-870a16bc-03391))_

> If an adventurer falls, damage will be determined as follows. **Falling damage** = fall height x 3 - (defense roll result + adventurer level) Base falling damage (fall height x 3) is for falling on normal ground. If you fall on soft grass or on the water surface, calculate by reducing factors, such as _fall height x 2_ , _fall height x 1_ , etc. On the other hand, if you have something like spikes sticking out, etc. increase the factor to increase the damage. This decision should be made by the 

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03389))_

> Just like when reducing damage in weapon combat, make a defense roll using the armor's defense power as the key number, and add your adventurer level to the result to determine the damage reduced.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03391))_

> **Falling damage** = fall height x 3 - (defense roll result + adventurer level) Base falling damage (fall height x 3) is for falling on normal ground. If you fall on soft grass or on the water surface, calculate by reducing factors, such as _fall height x 2_ , _fall height x 1_ , etc. On the other hand, if you have something like spikes sticking out, etc. increase the factor to increase the damage. This decision should be made by the game master.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03392))_

> If an owner of the thief skill falls, the height can be calculated by subtracting _thief skill level + agility bonus_ meters from the height of the fall.

### **Example:**

- A thief with thief skill level 3 and dexterity bonus +2 can fall from a height of 5 meters or less without suffering any damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03394))_

### **[** ⑤ **Long Jump/High Jump [Agility] ]**

- **Long jump** and **high jump** are common adventurer abilities, and are also abilities of the thief skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03396))_
- The baseline score is _thief skill level + agility bonus_ or _adventurer level + agility bonus (Table 6-3: Determining Long Jump Target Scores)._ _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03396))_
- The latter allows you to jump farther and higher. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03396))_
- However, to attempt a pole long jump, you need a pole that is at least _half_ as long as the distance you are attempting to jump. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03397))_
- In this case, the distance you can jump will be further improved. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03397))_
- In addition, the thief skill allows you to attempt _pole_ long jumps and _pole_ high jumps using a long pole. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03397))_
- To attempt a pole high jump, you will need a pole that is about the _same length_ as the height you are attempting to jump (Table 6-4: Determining High Jump Target Scores). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03397))_

### **Example 1:**

- An adventurer with adventurer level 4, agility bonus +2, wearing chain mail, is in a cave beneath a volcano. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03399))_
- A narrow rocky area continues, but eventually it is interrupted by a 7 meter long chasm. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03399))_
- Below is boiling lava. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03400))_
- At this time, considering the following conditions: _7 meter width_ , _chain mail_ , _the rocky area is too narrow to make a proper run-up_ , and _death is inevitable if you fall_ , the target score is 20+4+2+2= **28.** The baseline score is _6_ , so anything other than _double sixes_ is a failure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03400))_

### **Example 2:**

- The baseline score is 3, so it's a success if he rolls 8 or greater on 2D. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03402))_
- The adventurer throws away his luggage and takes on a light outfit with only a short sword strapped to his waist. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03402))_
- The target score at this time is 9 + 0 - 2 + 2 + 2 = **11** , taking into account the following conditions: _7 meters wide_ , _no armor_ , _no luggage_ , _insufficient run-up_ , and _danger_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03402))_
- The adventurer has 1 level in the thief skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03402))_
- Thinking it'd be impossible to jump like this, he takes off his heavy chain mail. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03402))_
- This will allow him to utilize his thief skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03402))_
- The adventurer throws away his luggage and takes on a light outfit with only a short sword strapped to his waist. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03402))_
- Jumping over obstacles with a high jump does _not_ guarantee a safe landing. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03403))_
- It usually doesn't cause much damage, and if you're a thief, you'll be able to land it perfectly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03403))_
- However, this can be a problem if the ground you jump over is significantly lower than the ground you took off from. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03403))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03402))_

## Chapter 7: General Skills

## 7.2 Merchant Skill

### **Notes on General Skills**

### **7.1**

- In other words, adventurer level can even be called hero level. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03416))_
- higher your adventurer level, the more qualified you are to be called a hero. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03416))_

### **The Difference Between General Skills and Adventurer Skills**

- Of course, the opposite is also true. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03418))_
- The details will be explained later, but the abilities of the two actually overlap quite a bit, and a good ranger can also become a good hunter. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03418))_
- Of course, being a hunter is also a respectable profession, so you should never look down on it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03419))_
- However, they are not called heroes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03419))_
- People with higher levels in general skills should also be given due respect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03419))_
- They are unrelated to these _adventures_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03419))_
- Therefore, a good ranger has the potential to be called a hero by the people, but a hunter, no matter how good he is, has a hard time getting people to recognize him as a hero. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03419))_
- Therefore, a good ranger has the potential to be called a hero by the people, but a hunter, no matter how good he is, has a hard time getting people to recognize him as a hero. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03419))_
- Many of you may be thinking, _Then there's no need to separate the two, right?_ However, you can't do that. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03420))_
- This is because the hunter skill and the ranger skill are completely different things. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03420))_
- This is because the hunter skill and the ranger skill are completely different things. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03420))_
- The reason for this is that the hunter skill is a general skill, while the ranger skill is, as precisely called, an adventurer skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03422))_
- For example, the famous playwright William Tell, who was just a hunter, was considered a hero for shooting an apple off his child's head. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03423))_
- If you understand this mentality, then on the other hand, it would be acceptable to recognize adventurer skills for skills that would be called heroes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03423))_
- In such cases, it is okay to grant adventurer skills to masters of general skills. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03423))_
- However, basically in _Sword World RPG_ , only those who are masters of adventurer skills are worthy of being called heroes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03423))_
- If you understand this mentality, then on the other hand, it would be acceptable to recognize adventurer skills for skills that would be called heroes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03423))_
- For example, the famous playwright William Tell, who was just a hunter, was considered a hero for shooting an apple off his child's head. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03423))_
- However, basically in _Sword World RPG_ , only those who are masters of adventurer skills are worthy of being called heroes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03423))_
- Of course, adventurer skills are the necessary skills to live as an adventurer. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03424))_
- However, the biggest difference between the two is whether or not you can become a protagonist-- called a hero -- who appears in fantasy novels, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03424))_
- General skills, so to speak, can be said to be the skills necessary for daily life. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03424))_

### **Merchant Skill**

### **7.2**

- A person holding this is given the right to open a shop in public. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03427))_
- However, you are free to buy, sell, or transfer personal items. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03427))_
- Merchants are a class with a lot of freedom in _Sword World_ , and often travel for trade purposes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03427))_
- Therefore, if some of the adventurers have this skill, they can also sell the items they obtain from their adventures in shops or on the street. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03427))_
- Merchants are usually issued a _merchant's pass_ by the king as proof of their status. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03427))_
- Among them are peddlers who are always traveling and doing business. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03427))_
- This is a skill related to trade. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03427))_
- A person without this certificate cannot conduct transactions in public. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03427))_
- Therefore, if some of the adventurers have this skill, they can also sell the items they obtain from their adventures in shops or on the street. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03427))_
- Even though they're all called merchants, there are many different types of merchants _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03428))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03431))_

> There are many general skills. There are probably as many general skills as there are occupations, hobbies, etc. There is no way we can create rules for them one by one, etc. Therefore, we will only introduce the necessary general skills, and leave everything beyond that to each game master. If a game master deems it necessary, please make a general skill into a rule.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03429))_

> For example, some merchants may sell arts and crafts, while others might sell fresh produce, etc. Therefore, merchants are well versed in the art of trading one type of item, and are no different from ordinary people when it

## 7.3 Craftsman Skill

- In _Sword World_ , all production is done by hand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03449))_

### **[** ① **Value Check [Intelligence]]**

- **(Not retriable)** This is almost the same ability as the thief and sage skills' _treasure appraisal_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03441))_
- The result is a specific price converted to silver coins. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03441))_

- However, you can only make a check for items that the merchant deals with. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-03441))_
