### **[Living Doll (Injected/Magic)]**

- This is a very rare type of poison, and only a few know the recipe. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04380))_
- If a woman is wounded by a knife poisoned with this and fails to resist, her body will shrink and become a little person, about 30 cm tall, and will enter a state of suspended animation. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04380))_
- This is a very rare type of poison, and only a few know the recipe. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04380))_

### 12.2 Rules for Poison, Illness and Infection

- When a man is affected by this poison and fails to resist, his body undergoes abnormal mutations as a side effect, turning him into a mutant monster. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04383))_
- This poison has its normal effect only on women. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04383))_
- This poison has its normal effect only on women. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04383))_

### **Illnesses**

### **How to Read Illness 12.2.2.1 Details**

- Illness details are expressed as follows. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04389))_

### **[ Common Cold ]**

- Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=1 day/5/7 (14) Fatal Depth=5 _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04392))_
- As the cold progresses, you may develop a high fever and also have difficulty breathing. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04394))_
- This is what they call a cold. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04394))_
- Some people may experience diarrhea symptoms or joint pain. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04394))_
- You suffer a -1 penalty on all success rolls while you have a cold. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04394))_
- It’s accompanied by a slight fever, cough, sneezing, runny nose, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04394))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04389))_

### 《 **Symptoms** 》

- This briefly describes the conditions that appear in the illness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04396))_

### 《 **Rarity** 》

- If an owner of the sage skill or healer skill succeeds on a success roll using _skill level + intelligence bonus_ as the baseline score and **rarity** as the target score, they can look at a sick patient and identify which illness they have. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04398))_
- This score is used when making a check to see if you know the illness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04398))_
- This means not only knowing the name of the disease, but also its characteristics and how to deal with it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04398))_
- This means not only knowing the name of the disease, but also its characteristics and how to deal with it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04398))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04396))_

> This briefly describes the conditions that appear in the illness.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04398))_

> If a disease can be identified, owners of the healer skill can provide appropriate treatment.

### 《 **Progression Speed/Progression**

### **Intensity/ Healing Score** 》

- When the _depth_ becomes 0, you are completely recovered. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04401))_
- Adventurer level may also be hero level, but _even if you're a hero, you cannot win against illness_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04401))_
- If the result is greater than or equal to the _healing score_ , the illness will be cured and the _depth_ will be reduced by 1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04401))_
- The degree of progression of an illness is indicated by a score called **depth** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04401))_
- The patient's adventurer level and life force bonus have _no_ influence on this check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04401))_
- Immediately after contracting an illness, the _depth_ is 1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04401))_
- After the time indicated by **progression speed** has elapsed since you contracted the illness, roll two dice and look at the result. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04401))_
- After a certain period of time has elapsed after becoming ill, the illness either progresses further or gets better. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04401))_
- If the roll is greater than or equal to the _progression intensity_ but less than the **healing score** , the illness neither worsens nor improves, and the _depth_ remains the same. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04401))_
- After a certain period of time has elapsed after becoming ill, the illness either progresses further or gets better. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04401))_
- Immediately after contracting an illness, the _depth_ is 1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04401))_
- After the time indicated by **progression speed** has elapsed since you contracted the illness, roll two dice and look at the result. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04401))_
- For example, by working with salamander, fever, which is the main symptom of a cold, can be alleviated. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04402))_
- When a shaman performs such a treatment, they gain a +1 bonus on progression/healing checks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04402))_
- For example, by working with salamander, fever, which is the main symptom of a cold, can be alleviated. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04402))_
- In addition to these, depending on the illness, there are herbs and special medicines that can provide bonuses to progression/healing checks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04403))_
- If this does _not_ completely cure the illness, it’ll be _useless_ to cast these spells on the same patient until the caster's magic power increases. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04404))_
- The target score is double the _healing score_ (added in parentheses). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04404))_
- You can continue to make this check until you fail or until the illness is completely cured. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04404))_
- When the holy magic _Cure Disease_ or _Refresh_ , or the spirit magic _Restore Health_ are cast, the caster must make a success roll using their magic power as the baseline score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04404))_
- If a healer who knows the true nature of an illness cares for the patient for more than one hour per day, they can make a similar check using that healer's _healer skill level + intelligence bonus_ as the baseline score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04405))_
- If after some time has passed, an illness progression check occurs, the healer can also make a healing check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04405))_
- However, even if they fail, the same healer can continue to care for the patient. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04405))_
- If after some time has passed, an illness progression check occurs, the healer can also make a healing check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04405))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04398))_

### 《 **Fatal Depth** 》

- If, as an illness progresses, its _depth_ reaches the **fatal depth** , the character will die from the illness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04407))_

### 《 **Infection Power** 》

- who is caring for the patient. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04409))_
- When an illness is _very strong_ or _strong_ , there is a high possibility that the infection will spread to the healer, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04409))_
- If it’s **very strong** , it has the infection power of a plague. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04409))_
- When it's **very weak** , it's not infectious and is unlikely to spread to other people. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04409))_
- If the game master determines that there is a possibility of contracting the illness, have the character make a progression check for the illness (compare a 2D roll with progression intensity). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04409))_
- **Strong** is about the same level as influenza. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04409))_
- If they fail, 164 the character has contracted the illness at _depth_ 1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04409))_
- As in the example, **moderate** means it's just a cold, and **weak** means it's not very infectious. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04409))_
- As in the example, **moderate** means it's just a cold, and **weak** means it's not very infectious. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04409))_

- A detailed description and data on the illness is included at the end. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04411))_

### **12.2.2.1 Illness Samples**

- Here are some examples of illnesses seen in _Sword World_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04413))_
- Most of the illnesses listed here are incurable diseases that are difficult to heal and have a high mortality rate. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04413))_
- Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=3 days/6/9 (18) Fatal Depth=3 _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04415))_
- The chance of being infected by a character before death is not particularly high, but if you touch the green goo left behind by a character after death, there's a very high chance of being infected. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04417))_
- When the lethal depth of 3 is reached, the entire body rots, dissolves and washes away. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04417))_
- Immediately after infection, itchy green spots about 3 cm in diameter appear all over the body, but at _depth_ 2, the spots emit a putrid odor and a dirty pus begins to flow out. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04417))_
- This is said to be an illness that occurs when the power of gnome in the body weakens and the power of undine becomes stronger. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04417))_
- The chance of being infected by a character before death is not particularly high, but if you touch the green goo left behind by a character after death, there's a very high chance of being infected. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04417))_
- Immediately after infection, itchy green spots about 3 cm in diameter appear all over the body, but at _depth_ 2, the spots emit a putrid odor and a dirty pus begins to flow out. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04417))_

### **[ Cotton Weaver ]**

- Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=1 week/5/10 (20) Fatal Depth=3 _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04421))_
- At _depth_ 1, the character's actions are not affected at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04423))_
- Although the progression speed is not that fast, it is an incurable disease that is difficult to treat once contracted. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04423))_
- If you pluck a little bit of the cotton, it will instantly disappear, but at 2, the character will fall into a coma and the cotton will come out no matter how much you pluck. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04423))_
- The cause is a very small wound on the fingertip, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04423))_
- This is a strange disease that occurs due to a significant decrease in the power of undine within the body. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04423))_
- When infected with this illness, the wound will not close at all, but moreover, something that looks like cotton will start to bulge out of the wound. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04423))_
- This is a strange disease that occurs due to a significant decrease in the power of undine within the body. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04423))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04419))_

> Symptoms=Cotton-like tissue peels off from the wound

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04420))_

> Rarity=14

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04422))_

> Infection Power=Weak

### **[ Stone Skin ]**

- Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=2 weeks/7/11 (22) Fatal Depth=4 Infection Power=Weak _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04427))_
- However, the disease will continue to progress, and eventually your entire body will become a stone statue. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04428))_
- At _depth_ 2, your knees become stiff. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04428))_
- This is a terrifying illness in which the power of gnome becomes so strong that the surface of the body turns to stone beginning with the feet. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04428))_
- At _depth_ 3, your body will become stiff up to the waist, and at _depth_ 4, your chest will become stiff, at which point your character will be unable to breathe and will die of suffocation. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04428))_
- At _depth_ 1, your ankles will become stiff and you won't be able to walk. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04428))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04425))_

> Symptoms=The surface of the body gradually becomes hard like stone

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04426))_

> Rarity=14

### **[ Dance Macabre ]**

- Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=Half day/6/9 (18) Fatal Depth=4 Infection Power=Strong _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04431))_
- When a person contracts this illness, their body begins to dance on its own, regardless of their consciousness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04432))_
- While suffering from this illness, you’ll suffer a -4 penalty to your attack power and evasion speed, and you will not be able to cast ancient magic or spirit magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04432))_
- As the _depth_ of the illness increases, the dancing becomes more intense. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04432))_
- Since the person him or herself feels festive while suffering from the illness, it’s believed that this is an abnormal manifestation of the power of leprechaun, which is a mind spirit. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04432))_
- Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=3 days/6/9 (18) Fatal Depth=5 _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04434))_
- The reason for the outbreak of the disease is unknown, but there are many known cases where excessive use of the spirit magic _Sleep_ has brought the disease to shamans. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04436))_
- It may also be triggered by an unnatural sleep caused by the ancient magic _Sleep Cloud_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04436))_
- It’s believed that this illness is caused by the power of the mind spirit sandman becoming too strong. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04436))_
- The patient will then die when it reaches the fatal depth of 5. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04436))_
- As the illness progresses, the patient will begin to complain of extreme fatigue with even the slightest amount of exercise, and the amount of time spent sleeping each day will increase (use _depth x 3 + 9_ as a standard). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04436))_
- It may also be triggered by an unnatural sleep caused by the ancient magic _Sleep Cloud_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04436))_
- The patient will then die when it reaches the fatal depth of 5. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04436))_
- It’s believed that this illness is caused by the power of the mind spirit sandman becoming too strong. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04436))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04430))_

> Symptoms=The body keeps dancing separate from the person's will Rarity=13

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04435))_

### **[ Balloon Burst ]**

- Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=3 days/7/9 (18) Fatal Depth=3 Infection Power=Weak _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04445))_
- This illness causes difficulty breathing, along with body swelling, then you become unable to move, and eventually your body ruptures and you die. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04446))_
- This illness causes difficulty breathing, along with body swelling, then you become unable to move, and eventually your body ruptures and you die. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04446))_

- At depth 2, your body becomes abnormally swollen and round, and you'll be in severe pain and unable to move. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04447))_
- At depth 1, you suffer from severe breathing difficulties and will be unable to speak or cast spells. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04447))_
- This illness is not seen in cold regions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04448))_

### **[ Vampire Skin ]**

- At depth 2, vision is weakened and most success rolls suffer a -2 penalty. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04454))_
- At depth 3 you lose your vision, and at depth 4 you fall into a coma. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04454))_
- This is an illness in which the power of dark spirits becomes unnecessarily strong, causing a hypersensitivity to light. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04454))_
- At depth 1, painful blisters resembling burns accompany areas exposed to sunlight. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04454))_

### **[ Lycanthropy ]**

- Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=Special/7/- (16) Fatal Depth=None Infection Power=Very weak _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04463))_
- This illness, which turns humans into lycanthropes, is thought to be different from normal illnesses caused by abnormalities in spirit power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04464))_
- Some sages claim that this is a type of curse (although these sages' claim is not accepted, as it cannot be removed with a _Remove Curse_ spell). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04464))_
- This illness, which turns humans into lycanthropes, is thought to be different from normal illnesses caused by abnormalities in spirit power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04464))_
- If the roll is a 6 or less, you'll be infected with lycanthropy (the game master must secretly roll the dice if the adventurer does not know that lycanthropy is an infectious disease). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04465))_
- Those infected with lycanthropy will have swollen wounds and a high fever. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04466))_
- Once the score is equal to or less than the number of days, the character becomes a full lycanthrope and transforms into a beast form. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04466))_
- (the target score is 16), but once you become a beast, you _cannot_ be cured again. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04467))_
- Before becoming a full lycanthrope, it can be cured with a spell such as the holy magic _Cure Disease_ , etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04467))_

### **[ Rat Disease ]**

- Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=1 day/5/7 (14) Fatal Depth=5 _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04470))_
- Infection occurs when bitten by a rat, but it's rarely transmitted from person to person. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04472))_
- If the _depth_ reaches 2 or greater, you will be unable to walk or stand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04472))_
- Progression Speed/Progression Intensity/ Healing Score=1 week/5/10 (20) Fatal Depth=5 _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04474))_
- Infection Power=Very strong (Infection through gaze) When you're exposed to extremely strong fear, such as due to combat, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04475))_
- When the illness is at depth 1, symptoms include being unable to move due to being seized by fear and having nightmares every night. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04475))_
- Patients in this state go berserk and begin to attack things around them at random. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04475))_
- leprechaun, the mind spirit, manifests abnormally and causes this illness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04475))_

- When the depth is at 2 or greater, changes will appear in your body shape. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04475))_
- You'll become hunched over, your hands abnormally long, your face contorted, and your eyes blazing red. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04475))_
- This illness was given its name because the entire body turns red and hair falls out. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04475))_
- Infection Power=Very strong (Infection through gaze) When you're exposed to extremely strong fear, such as due to combat, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04475))_
- This illness is transmitted when eye contact is made with someone who's been infected. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04476))_
- In other words, roll two dice, and if the roll does not total 5 or greater, you’ll be infected. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04477))_

### **12.2.3 Herbs**

- Below is a list of **herbs** known in _Forcelia_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04485))_
- Each has a provided rarity. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04485))_
- Each herb has a **base trade value** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04486))_
- You basically have to take them every day to receive a bonus on illness progression/healing checks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04486))_
- If an adventurer is lucky enough to obtain these items and sell them, a merchant will buy them for half the prices provided here. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04486))_
- The prices are for one day's worth for one adult. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04486))_

### **[ Arnica ]**

### Rarity=10

- Each plant has 2 to 3 flowers. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04490))_
- This is a perennial plant that’s often found in high mountains. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04490))_
- The flowers can only be stored for 2 to 3 days. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04490))_
- One flower is enough for one day for an adult. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04490))_
- The flowers can only be stored for 2 to 3 days. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04490))_

### **[ Dulcamara ]**

### Rarity=13

### Base Trade Value=320 gamels

- When used on illnesses that cause severe inflammation of the skin, such as green slime rot, it provides a +2 bonus on progression/healing checks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04498))_
- Seeds can be stored for long periods of time as long as they're in good condition. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04498))_
- This is a vine-like shrub that can be found in highlands. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04498))_
- Its seeds are ground and mixed with oil to make an ointment that's used to treat skin diseases. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04498))_
- Dozens of seeds can be harvested from one plant, and 2 to 3 seeds are enough for one adult per day. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04498))_
- When used on illnesses that cause severe inflammation of the skin, such as green slime rot, it provides a +2 bonus on progression/healing checks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04498))_

- This is an herb that grows in the wilderness and is the staple food of the cockatrice, a magical beast. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04502))_
- One henruda plant is enough for one adult for one day. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04502))_

## 12.3 Work Requests to NPCs

- You found a beautiful gemstone and would like to have it forged into a ring. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04517))_
- As you progress through your adventures, such things will happen. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04517))_
- Also, if there’s no one available to do this for you, there is of course no point in paying any amount of money. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04518))_
- If the task becomes a little more complex, use numbers such as x _40_ or x _50_ instead of x 30. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04518))_
- In these cases, adventurers can request NPCs to do this kind of work. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04518))_
- For example, if you request an appraisal from a 5th level sage, you will have to pay 5 x 5 x 30 = **750** gamels. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04518))_
- For example, if you request an appraisal from a 5th level sage, you will have to pay 5 x 5 x 30 = **750** gamels. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04518))_
- If the task becomes a little more complex, use numbers such as x _40_ or x _50_ instead of x 30. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04518))_
- In this case, higher compensation is required. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04519))_
- If the task you're requesting from an NPC is something that will completely restrain that NPC, you will need to **hire an NPC** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04519))_
- The average price per day is about _NPC's skill level multiplied by 50 to 200 gamels_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04519))_
- Even in villages, there are likely to be people with a fairly high farmer skill level (although it’s unlikely that you’ll ask such people for anything). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04520))_
- 154) to find out which skill level NPCs are in which locations. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04520))_
- For requests for such work, you'd usually give 10% to 30% as an advance payment. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04521))_
- If the contractor willfully abandons the job, it is customary to return double the advance payment to the client. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04521))_
- Adventurers _themselves_ cannot go around charging these same rates. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04522))_
- This is the price an _adventurer_ must pay when making a request to an _NPC_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04522))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04518))_

> If it’s a very simple task like those above, you can have it done for _NPC skill level x skill level x 30 gamels_ .

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04518))_

> In these cases, adventurers can request NPCs to do this kind of work. If it’s a very simple task like those above, you can have it done for _NPC skill level x skill level x 30 gamels_ . For example, if you request an appraisal from a 5th level sage, you will have to pay 5 x 5 x 30 = **750** gamels. If the task becomes a little more complex, use numbers such as x _40_ or x _50_ instead of x 30. Also, if there’s no one available to do this for you, there is of course no point in paying any amount 

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04521))_

> If the work _fails_ , you do _not_ have to pay the remainder.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04522))_

> If you don't have clients, you won't get paid (so just patiently wait for work at the adventurer shop, okay?).

### **[ Lignia Stone ]**

### Rarity=12

- This is an amber stone that comes from the body of the mythical beast lynx. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04513))_
- If you crush, brew, and drink this stone, you will receive a +4 bonus on progression/healing checks for illnesses that affect the mind (such as dance macabre). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04514))_
- If you crush, brew, and drink this stone, you will receive a +4 bonus on progression/healing checks for illnesses that affect the mind (such as dance macabre). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04514))_

## Chapter 13: Monsters!

## 13.1 Types of Monsters

- This is based on the types set by Laverna, the famous witch of Orphan, who compiled the _Natural History of Alecrast_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04531))_
- The meaning of each type are as follows. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04531))_
- There are 12 types of monsters in Alecrast: **fae** , **youma** , **spirits** , **titans** , **mythical beasts** , **magical beasts** , **animals** , **plants** , **undead** , **magical creatures** , **daemons** , **humans** , and **others** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04531))_
- Also, they will not be wounded even if attacked by the spirit power they control. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04532))_
- Poisons, illnesses, and mental attacks are all meaningless against spirits. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04532))_
- certain that spirits are involved in the activities of life itself. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04532))_
- These mad spirits are extremely dangerous. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04533))_
- They cannot adapt to the material world and will act destructively towards everything in it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04533))_
- Spirits that appear in the material world in response to a shaman's summoning usually return to the spirit world after fulfilling their role (when you use spirit magic). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04533))_
- A mad spirit will return to the spirit world as soon as its life points or spirit points reach 0. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04533))_
- Spirits that appear in the material world in response to a shaman's summoning usually return to the spirit world after fulfilling their role (when you use spirit magic). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04533))_

### 《 **Fae** 》

- However, some races, such as high elves and fairies, still have the ability to travel between the two worlds. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04535))_
- They are relatively friendly towards humans, but do not actively try to connect with them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04535))_
- Fae are a demi-human race that originally lived in the fairy world, but for various reasons they came to live in this world and were unable to return to their original world. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04535))_
- However, some races, such as high elves and fairies, still have the ability to travel between the two worlds. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04535))_
- No matter how much they use it, it will not consume their mental points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04536))_
- However, they cannot expand the power of their spells. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04536))_
- Elves, dwarves, and grassrunners are also fae, and these three races can also be player characters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04537))_
- Regardless of a spirit's rarity (a score related to how well-known the monster is, which will be explained later), a shaman knows all spirits. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04538))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04535))_

> Fae are a demi-human race that originally lived in the fairy world, but for various reasons they came to live in this world and were unable to return to their original world. However, some races, such as high elves and fairies, still have the ability to travel between the two worlds. They are relatively friendly towards humans, but do not actively try to connect with them.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04536))_

> Spirits can use an unlimited amount of spirit magic depending on their power.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04536))_

> Spirits can use an unlimited amount of spirit magic depending on their power. No matter how much they use it, it will not consume their mental points. However, they cannot expand the power of their spells.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04538))_

> When encountering a spirit, a shaman can recognize a spirit without the need for a success roll.

### 《 **Youma** 》

- Youma are a race of fae that have evil personalities. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04540))_
- They have a very strong hostile relationship with the fae, and of course they are not friendly with humans either. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04540))_
- Typical spirits include sylph, undine, and will-o-wisp. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04541))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04538))_

> Regardless of a spirit's rarity (a score related to how well-known the monster is, which will be explained later), a shaman knows all spirits. When encountering a spirit, a shaman can recognize a spirit without the need for a success roll.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04540))_

> They may also use spirit magic or dark magic.

### 《 **Titans** 》

- Typical examples of youma include goblins and gremlins. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04543))_
- Some sages claim that, like the gods, they were born directly from the primordial titans, and that the gods were the greatest titans among them (though of course priests and theologians strongly deny this). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04544))_
- The origin of the titans is unclear. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04544))_
- Some are said to be titan species of fae or youma, but others are clearly not. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04544))_

### 《 **Spirits** 》

- They are closely related to the natural elements of earth, water, fire, wind and light, as well as darkness, and emotions and the nature of the heart, which are the functions of the mind. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04546))_
- Spirits have a will, but they can be called energy rather than living beings. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04546))_
- In addition, spirits may reside in natural creatures and objects, and above all, it seems _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04546))_
- Among the titans, some races are relatively gentle, while others are frenetic and hostile. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04547))_
- Their bodies are much larger than that of a human. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04547))_
- Their strong bodies hold terrifying power, so it is best not to deal with them unless you're a very experienced adventurer. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04547))_
- Their strong bodies hold terrifying power, so it is best not to deal with them unless you're a very experienced adventurer. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04547))_

### 《 **Mythical Beasts and**

### **Magical Beasts** 》

- These include three types: things that have existed long before humans, such as dragons, things that live like humans, and artificial creatures created through the experiments of ancient sorcerers that have turned wild. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04550))_
- Many have a mixture of features from multiple animals. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04550))_
- Mythical beast and magical beast are general terms for monsters that have living bodies, unlike spirits or undead. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04550))_
- Mythical beast and magical beast are general terms for monsters that have living bodies, unlike spirits or undead. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04550))_
- These include three types: things that have existed long before humans, such as dragons, things that live like humans, and artificial creatures created through the experiments of ancient sorcerers that have turned wild. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04550))_
- Some of them have high intellect, while others have become wild and have lost most of their intellect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04551))_
- Many of them have special abilities, like using ancient magic, or having undifferentiated spirit power (fire, ice, life, mind, etc.) in their bodies (in these cases, attacks involving that spirit power have no effect on them). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04551))_
- They are also not affected by illness or poison, and do not sleep in the normal sense of the word. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04551))_
- They have a near-infinite lifespan, but leave behind very few offspring, and so are doomed to gradual decline and extinction. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04551))_
- These creatures have an intellect close to that of humans, and create societies and live their lives in the same way as humans. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04552))_
- Many of them are unable to reproduce on their own and can only produce offspring with the help of humans. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04552))_
- Many of them are unable to reproduce on their own and can only produce offspring with the help of humans. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04552))_
- To protect themselves or to show off their power, sorcerers of the ancient kingdom period often gave them various offensive abilities and frenetic personalities, and many of these kinds of mythical and magical beasts are dangerous. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04554))_
- However, on the other hand, they can survive on very little food. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04554))_
- Sleep is necessary for the maintenance of normal animals and humans both as individuals and a race, but some mythical beasts and magical beasts are created such that they do not need to sleep. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04554))_
- Magic that causes sleep (such as " _Sleep Cloud_ " or " _Sleep_ ") has no effect on such things. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04554))_
- 170 magic have almost no reproductive ability. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04554))_
- Magic that causes sleep (such as " _Sleep Cloud_ " or " _Sleep_ ") has no effect on such things. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04554))_
- However, this is rather a result of selfish value judgment by humans, and even if they are called mythical beasts, they do not seem to like interacting with humans. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04555))_
- There are also things such as dragons that are called both mythical _and_ magical beasts. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04555))_
- Creatures that have a beautiful appearance and are neutral or friendly towards humans are called _mythical beasts_ , while those that have an ugly or unpleasant appearance and are harmful to humans are called _magical beasts_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04555))_
- There are also things such as dragons that are called both mythical _and_ magical beasts. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04555))_
- Examples of mythical beasts include griffons, unicorns, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04556))_
- and magical beasts include chimeras, manticores etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04556))_

### 《 **Animals** 》

- Of course, they are basically the same animals that live in our world, but they also include those of unusual size and animals that are already extinct in our world. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04558))_
- There are animals in Forcelia too. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04558))_

### 《 **Plants** 》

- Plants are basically the same as those that live in our world, but there are also abnormal creatures that move around under the influence of magic, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04560))_

### 《 **Undead** 》

- Undead are monsters that exist and move around through forces that are the exact opposite of those of us humans and normal animals. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04562))_
- There are many mysteries surrounding the origin of these undead, but it's completely different from how normal animals give birth. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04563))_
- This negative life force is sometimes called the _undeath force_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04563))_
- If you meet them, there is a high probability that you'll fight. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04564))_
- Many spells with mental effects are also meaningless. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04565))_
- Undead are not affected by poison or illness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04565))_
- They do not require rest in the normal sense and can never be magically put to sleep. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04565))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04563))_

> If the source of our life is called _positive_ life force, then the undead's source can be called _negative_ life force.

### 《 **Magical Creatures** 》

- Pseudo-life forms created (or created in the past) using ancient magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04567))_
- During the ancient kingdom period, various magical creatures were created as a challenge to the _gods_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04568))_
- The magical creatures that were created in this way basically continue to live indefinitely without needing to eat or breathe, and many of them are still around today. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04568))_
- The sorcerers sought to gain power as creators. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04568))_
- These are also of the magical creature type. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04569))_
- There were also many failures in the production of magical creatures. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04569))_
- Among the failures that were carelessly discarded, there are some that are still alive due to their strong life force. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04569))_

### 《 **Daemons/Underworld**

### **Creatures** 》

- In the age of the gods, they were known as terrifying slaughterers who followed the dark god Phalaris, and in the age of the ancient kingdom, they were summoned to this material world by sorcerers who were skilled in summoning magic, offering their wicked power to the sorcerers. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04572))_
- Daemons are underworld monsters that live in a material world different from ours. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04572))_
- They have a number of special abilities and wicked personalities, making them extremely dangerous. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04572))_
- Although they rarely make their presence felt in this world, those summoned during the days of the ancient kingdom still dwell in underground labyrinths and remote ruins. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04572))_
- They are said to have a deep connection with the dark gods, and many are also well-versed in dark magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04572))_
- Many of them seem to have a humanoid appearance, but there are many different types, such as those with horns, tails, wings, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04573))_
- Furthermore, in the original world in which daemons live - the underworld - it's said that they form society just like humans. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04573))_
- Many of them seem to have a humanoid appearance, but there are many different types, such as those with horns, tails, wings, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04573))_
- Below them are types called greater daemons and lesser daemons, and even the lowest rank Lesser Daemons have powers that far exceed those of humans. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04574))_
- There are different ranks of daemons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04574))_
- The highest rank ones, called daemon lords and archdaemons, are said to have powers comparable to that of ancient titans or dragons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04574))_
- With the discovery of two similar daemons (lesser daemons and greater daemons) called doubleburg and doppelgänger, it's become an established theory among sages that daemons are born as lower rank daemons and grow to become higher rank. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04575))_
- In the underworld where daemons live, there are also animal-like creatures with low intellect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04576))_

### **13.2 Monster Catalog**

### **13.2.1**

### **How to Read the Monster Catalog**

- Monster data is written in the following format. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04589))_

### **[ Dog ]**

- Monster Level=1 Rarity=3 Agility=15 Movement Speed=20 Number=One to around ten Frequency=Frequent Intellect=Animal Reaction=Neutral to friendly Attack Points=Fangs: 8 (1) Strike Points=4 Evasion Points=9 (2) Defense Points=4 Life Points/Resistance=12/7 (0) Mental Points/Resistance=5/7 (0) Special Abilities=None in particular Habitat=Forests, meadows, human villages Perception=Five senses Languages=None _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04591))_
- Dogs are animals that have a very close relationship with humans. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04592))_
- Dogs have been domesticated and used for a variety of purposes, such as hunting dogs, guard dogs, or simply as pet dogs. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04592))_
- New breeds have been artificially created by the hands of humans, and there are many subspecies, ranging from small dogs that are no taller than a person's shins to large dogs that are taller than a human's waist. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04592))_
- They have fur and fangs in their mouths. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04592))_
- The data presented here is for the most commonly seen medium-sized dog, and its body height is roughly from the knee to the thigh of a human. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04592))_
- Dogs have been domesticated and used for a variety of purposes, such as hunting dogs, guard dogs, or simply as pet dogs. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04592))_
- A few lines of game data are shown first, followed by a detailed explanation. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04594))_
- The meaning of each game detail is as follows. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04594))_

Dogs have been domesticated and used for a variety of purposes, such as hunting dogs, guard dogs, or simply as pet dogs. New breeds have been artificially created by the hands of humans, and there are many subspecies, ranging from small dogs that are no taller than a person's shins to large dogs that are taller than a human's waist. **Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04593))_

> Dogs have a habit of following their leaders, and humans have taken advantage of this habit to use dogs for various purposes. Dogs react to humans in a variety of ways. If they are domesticated, they will not attack humans because they consider humans to be their masters. However, special guard dogs, military dogs, etc. that have been trained to treat all but specific people as enemies will attack even humans larger than themselves. Dogs are especially aggressive towards intruders-they also have a strong sense of territory. If it's a wild dog that's not owned by humans, it will act according to its own instincts. If they're hungry and think their opponent is weak, they may attack.

### 《 **Monster Level** 》

- If it's 10, even a seasoned adventurer will have a hard time crossing swords with it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04596))_
- This is a score that corresponds to a character's adventurer level, and represents the general strength of that monster. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04596))_
- the damage can be reduced by this monster level score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04597))_

### 《 **Rarity** 》

- This is a score that indicates whether the monster is famous or not. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04599))_
- The lower this score, the more well-known the monster is. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04599))_
- 118), this rarity is the target score for a success roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04599))_

### 《 **Agility** 》

- 《 **Movement Speed** 》 Indicates the speed at which the monster moves. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04601))_
- It's used to determine the order of actions in a combat round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04601))_
- If the monster's movement speed is greater than the character's agility, the character will not be able to escape from the monster unless they think of something. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04602))_
- It's also used to determine whether a character can escape from the monster. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04602))_
- If the monster's movement speed is greater than the character's agility, the character will not be able to escape from the monster unless they think of something. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04602))_
- For such monsters, two types of movement speed are written. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04603))_
- In rare cases, there are monsters that do not move on the ground (such as those that are always floating in the air), so in these cases, the movement method is written in parentheses immediately after the movement speed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04604))_
- 172 _after_ the slash is movement speed when performing _special movements_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04604))_
- In rare cases, there are monsters that do not move on the ground (such as those that are always floating in the air), so in these cases, the movement method is written in parentheses immediately after the movement speed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04604))_

### 《 **Number** 》

- Monsters that act in groups are usually encountered in large numbers, while monsters that have a strong sense of territory and really only exist alone in a certain area are unlikely to be encountered in numbers greater than one. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04606))_
- This is the most common number appearing when encountering that monster. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04606))_
- Monsters that act in groups are usually encountered in large numbers, while monsters that have a strong sense of territory and really only exist alone in a certain area are unlikely to be encountered in numbers greater than one. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04606))_
- This data is not exact, and the game master may change the number appearing depending on the circumstances of the scenario. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04607))_
- However, in order to do so, you will need a reason that the players can understand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04607))_

### 《 **Frequency** 》

- The types in descending order of frequency are **frequent** , **moderate** , **rare** , and **very rare** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04609))_
- In such cases, it would be best to provide a reason that the players can understand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04609))_
- It's a bit unnatural to have _rare_ or _very rare_ monsters repeatedly appear in a scenario. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04609))_
- This is the frequency at which the monster appears. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04609))_

### 《 **Intellect** 》

- There are eight types: **none** , **almost none** , **obeys commands** , **animal** , **low** , **human** , **high** , and **very high** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04611))_
- If it says _none_ , that means it doesn’t have intelligence, so to speak. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04612))_
- If you were to convert it to an intelligence score, it would be _0_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04612))_
- It does not think, nor does it even have instincts. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04612))_
- Spells that affect the mind have no effect on these monsters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04612))_
- If it says _none_ , that means it doesn’t have intelligence, so to speak. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04612))_
- Of course, spells that affect the mind cannot be expected to have any effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04613))_
- If it says _almost none_ , then it has almost no intellect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04613))_
- If you were to convert it to intelligence, it would be _1-2_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04613))_
- They have no advanced thinking and have almost no means of communicating their intentions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04613))_
- This is the intellectual standard of lower animals such as insects. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04613))_

- If it says _animal_ , it means literally the same degree as an animal. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04615))_

### 《 **Reaction** 》

- It can be **violent** , **adversarial** , **neutral** , **friendly** , **hunger-based** or **commandbased** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04621))_
- This represents the monster's general reaction when it encounters adventurers. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04621))_
- Examples include undead who have hatred for the living, and plants that act solely on reflex. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04622))_
- Many have no concept of running away. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04622))_
- If it says _violent_ , that means it will attack immediately. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04622))_
- If it says _violent_ , that means it will attack immediately. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04622))_
- Depending on the situation, they can be your enemy or your ally. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04624))_
- If it says _neutral_ , it means the monster holds no special feelings of hostility nor allyship towards the adventurers. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04624))_
- If it says _neutral_ , it means the monster holds no special feelings of hostility nor allyship towards the adventurers. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04624))_
- If it says _friendly_ , it means the monster has friendly feelings towards adventurers (humans and fae). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04626))_
- In addition, out in the world are also monsters that _get carried away_ , who do things that end up causing annoyance even though they mean no harm. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04626))_
- If you don't feel like they're a nuisance to you, they might provide some assistance. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04626))_
- However, if an adventurer takes hostile action, they will naturally consider retaliation. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04626))_
- If it says _friendly_ , it means the monster has friendly feelings towards adventurers (humans and fae). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04626))_
- This is because they may attack adventurers, seeing them as food. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04627))_
- However, for carnivorous (omnivorous) animals, it can be very dangerous when they're hungry. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04627))_
- If it says _hunger-based_ , it means they're basically neutral. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04627))_
- If it says _hunger-based_ , it means they're basically neutral. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04627))_
- This is because they may attack adventurers, seeing them as food. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04627))_
- _Command-based_ is often seen in magical creatures and lower grade undead that move according to the commands of others. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04628))_
- Whether they consider adventurers to be enemies or not depends on the commands given to them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04628))_
- There are several other reactions as well, but you can understand their meaning by looking at the explanation text after the data. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04629))_

> If it says _adversarial_ , it means they consider adventurers (human or fae) to be their enemies. The specific actions they'll take will vary depending on the power dynamic (difference in number) between the adventurers and monsters, as well as the intelligence of the monsters. Even if the monster is adversarial, if the adventurers have the superior numbers, it will not attack immediately and may try to use trickery.

### 《 **Attack Points** 》

- If you fail, you will suffer damage (refer to _Chapter 4: Weapon Combat_ ). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04631))_
- The higher this score, the more likely the monster will be able to hit you with an attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04631))_
- This represents the monster's attack method and its accuracy. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04631))_
- Each targeted character must make a success roll to determine whether they're able to avoid the attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04632))_
- For example, _Horn_ ： _12 (5)/Hoof_ ： _12 (5)_ means that two attacks can be made with horn and hoof. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04632))_
- Some monsters have multiple attack methods. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04632))_
- Multiple attack points are also listed for these cases. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04632))_
- For example, _Horn_ ： _12 (5)/Hoof_ ： _12 (5)_ means that two attacks can be made with horn and hoof. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04632))_
- If there are multiple attack methods, but they are written on _two_ (or more) lines, the monster may have multiple attack methods, but can only use _one_ of them during a round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04633))_
- If there are multiple attack methods, but they are written on _two_ (or more) lines, the monster may have multiple attack methods, but can only use _one_ of them during a round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04633))_

### 《 **Evasion Points** 》

- If you fail, your character's attack will miss. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04640))_
- This represents the degree to which the monster can evade attacks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04640))_
- In order for a character to hit a monster with an attack, they must succeed on a success roll using these evasion points as the target score and their attack power as the baseline score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04640))_

### 《 **Defense Points** 》

- This is exactly the same as a character's damage reduction due to armor (defense rolls) and adventurer level. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04642))_
- This is represented by defense points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04642))_
- to reduce damage (some fae and youma may wear armor just like humans). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04642))_
- When a monster suffers damage from a character's (weapon) attack, they can reduce the damage by the amount of defense points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04642))_
- Whether or not a character can reduce damage through armor is the standard for deciding whether to use defense points or monster level. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04643))_
- Defense points are only effective against attacks such as weapons, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04643))_
- Defense points are only effective against attacks such as weapons, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04643))_
- Therefore, _only_ defense points should be applied when reducing damage from weapon attacks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04644))_
- Monster level may _not_ be further subtracted from it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04644))_
- Therefore, _only_ defense points should be applied when reducing damage from weapon attacks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04644))_

In order for a character to hit a monster with an attack, they must succeed on a success roll using these evasion points as the target score and their attack power as the baseline score. If you fail, your character's attack will miss. Monster level, like adventurer level, prevents all damage, but a monster's defense points _already_ include damage reduction due to monster level. ### 《 **Life Points/Resistance** 》

- There are two numbers written. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04646))_
- The number before the slash (/) is life points, and the number after is life point resistance. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04646))_
- The number before the slash (/) is life points, and the number after is life point resistance. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04646))_
- **Life points** represents the monster's life force. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04647))_
- As with a character, when a monster's life points fall to 0 or less, they become disabled and, in some cases, die. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04647))_
- If the life point resistance is greater, the monster will be safe from the effects of the poison. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04648))_
- For characters, the resistance roll is successful if the final score of the resistance roll is _equal_ to the toxicity score, etc., but for monsters, the life point resistance must be _greater_ than the toxicity score, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04648))_
- When a monster is poisoned, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04648))_
- In the case of monsters, _life point resistance_ is used instead. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04648))_
- **(Life point) resistance** is a substitute for a character's _life force resistance roll_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04648))_
- a life force resistance roll determines whether they can avoid or reduce the effects of the poison. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04648))_
- Where a character would make a life _force_ resistance roll, a monster would use their life _point_ resistance. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04648))_
- When a character is poisoned, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04648))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04647))_

> When a monster suffers damage, it will lose life points.

### 《 **Mental Points/Resistance** 》

- Monsters whose mental points are reduced to 0 (or less) by _Shade_ magic, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04652))_
- **Mental point resistance** is a score that's a substitute for a character's _mental power resistance roll_ , so when a character casts magic on a monster, this _mental point resistance_ becomes the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04652))_
- **Mental points** have the same meaning as a character's mental power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04652))_
- If the final score is equal to or greater than the monster's mental point resistance, the magic will successfully be cast. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04653))_
- This is done purely for consistency, so that from the character's perspective, **tied success rolls are always successful** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04653))_
- Conversely, for a monster to resist magic, its mental point resistance must be _greater_ than the magic's final score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04653))_
- A _tie_ means the magic will successfully be cast. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04653))_
- A _tie_ means the magic will successfully be cast. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04653))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04653))_

> When a character casts a spell on a monster, they must make a success roll using their magic power as the baseline score.

- ( _Darkvision_ ) means the monster can see completely in the dark. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04662))_
- ( _Darkvision_ ) means the monster can see completely in the dark. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04662))_

- ( _Sonar_ ) means the monster can use sound waves or ultrasonic echoes to perceive the outside world. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04665))_

> Those whose sensory abilities are _pseudo_ have five pseudo-senses through magical means. After all, one part may be missing, or they may have special senses. In such cases, there is an organ that captures the sensation in a pseudo manner. If those organs are destroyed, the pseudo-senses will no longer be usable. For example, skeletons can use their empty eye sockets for pseudo vision. Therefore, if they're blindfolded, they won't be able to see, and they won't be able to see anything approaching from behind. Monsters whose perception is _pseudo_ can experience illusions.

## 13.2.2 Fae

### **[ Pixie ]**

### Monster Level=1

- Rarity=12 Agility=14 Movement Speed=4/7 (air) Number=One Frequency=Rare Intellect=Human Reaction=Friendly Attack Points=Weapon: 8 (1) Strike Points=2 Evasion Points=10 (3) Defense Points=3 Life Points/Resistance=7/8 (1) Mental Points/Resistance=15/9 (2) Special Abilities=2nd level spirit magic (Magic Intensity/Magic Power=11/4) Invisibility _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04676))_
- Moreover, they seem to have a very close relationship with the small spirits called sprites, and have as a basic ability the power of the _Invisibility_ spell that the sprites provide to shamans, which makes them all the more difficult to witness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04677))_
- Although they are relatively friendly towards humans, they do not actively interact with humans and rarely appear in public. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04677))_
- They have wings on their backs reminiscent of insects. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04677))_
- Also, for some reason, they seem to be wearing something that looks like clothes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04677))_
- However, in order to remain invisible, they need focus, and if they cannot do this, you will not be able to use _Invisibility_ , and even if they do use it, if they lose their concentration (such as when attacking or using other magic, etc.) they will appear instantly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04677))_
- Most of them have the appearance of women, but there are also individuals that have the appearance of men. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04677))_
- However, in order to remain invisible, they need focus, and if they cannot do this, you will not be able to use _Invisibility_ , and even if they do use it, if they lose their concentration (such as when attacking or using other magic, etc.) they will appear instantly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04677))_
- They’ll whisper in your ear while remaining hidden from view, and thrust a weapon in their hand (it appears to be a needle-thin dagger) into your butt. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04678))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04677))_

### **[ Fairy ]**

- Rarity=11 Agility=18 Movement Speed=9/10 (air) Number=Several Frequency=Rare Intellect=Human Reaction=Friendly to neutral Attack Points=Weapon: 8 (1) Strike Points=3 Evasion Points=10 (3) Defense Points=3 Life Points/Resistance=10/8 (1) Mental Points/Resistance=18/10 (3) Special Abilities=3rd level spirit magic (Magic Intensity/Magic Power=12/5) Habitat=Uncertain Languages=Fairy, silent spirit Perception=Five senses (infravision) Fairies are a representative race of fae, and many of them still live in the fairy world, and rarely come to the material world, the world we live in. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04682))_
- Stories are often told of humans who inadvertently wandered into the fairy world from these places. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04682))_
- However, there are some places in the material world that have contact with the fairy world. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04682))_
- They are about the same height as elves, but the most prominent difference between them and elves is the beautiful wings that grow from their backs. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04683))_
- These wings are very thin but strong, allowing them to fly, albeit at a slow speed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04683))_
- They may carry items such as silver daggers, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04683))_
- If they really get into trouble, they will choose to use their power of spirit magic rather than weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04683))_
- As they are residents of the fairy world, they have a deep connection with spirits, and use spirit magic up to 3rd level. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04683))_
- Fairies take the form of beautiful elf women, often mostly naked or clad in thin silk garments. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04683))_
- Fairies are sometimes seen where these two worlds meet. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04683))_
- They may carry items such as silver daggers, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04683))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04679))_

> Combat is unlikely to occur, but when it does, pixies can use up to 2nd level spirit magic.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-04681))_
