**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06752))_

> Rarity=14

### Enchanter=Unknown

- This staff can be pre-imbued with magic that activates either when a specific password is spoken or after a predetermined time has elapsed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06756))_
- Anyone who knows the password can activate the magic stored in the staff. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06756))_
- If set to activate after a certain time instead of with a password, the spell will be centered on the staff. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06756))_
- Furthermore, the mental power used to store a spell in this staff cannot be recovered by any means until the stored magic is used. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06756))_
- To store a spell in this staff, you must expend the same amount of mental power as casting the spell normally would require. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06756))_
- In this case, if the activating magic is an area-effect damage magic like _Fireball_ , the staff will be destroyed upon activation. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06756))_
- In other words, as a sorcerer reaches higher levels, they become capable of storing more powerful spells. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06756))_
- The spells that can be stored in this staff are limited to ancient magic with a consumed mental power (not the base mental power cost) of 3 or less. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06756))_
- This staff can be pre-imbued with magic that activates either when a specific password is spoken or after a predetermined time has elapsed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06756))_

The spells that can be stored in this staff are limited to ancient magic with a consumed mental power (not the base mental power cost) of 3 or less. In other words, as a sorcerer reaches higher levels, they become capable of storing more powerful spells. To store a spell in this staff, you must expend the same amount of mental power as casting the spell norm

### 14.4 Magical Item List

### **[ Quickness Ring ]**

- If you don't use the ring for one full day, the penalty will disappear. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06791))_
- If you use it _3_ times, it becomes _-2_ , and if you use it _4_ times, it becomes _-3_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06791))_
- This effect lasts for 18 rounds from the round in which you chanted the password. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06791))_
- There’s no limit to the number of times it can be used, but if the _same_ person uses it _more than once a day_ , he or she will suffer a -1 _penalty_ to their agility bonus thereafter. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06791))_
- There’s no limit to the number of times it can be used, but if the _same_ person uses it _more than once a day_ , he or she will suffer a -1 _penalty_ to their agility bonus thereafter. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06791))_

### **[ Weightless Bag ]**

- This is a backpack made of a mysterious material that repels water. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06824))_
- The weight of objects inside can be ignored. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06824))_
- The weight of objects inside can be ignored. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06824))_

### **[ Explosive Bullet ]**

- This bullet explodes on impact, dealing strike power 30 damage regardless of the sling used. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06831))_
- As usual, bonus damage is _fighter/thief/ranger skill level + strength bonus_ , and furthermore, your opponent can _only_ reduce this damage with adventurer level (monster level). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06831))_

### **[ Card of Power ]**

- This item was developed so that even those without the sorcerer skill can use ancient magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06843))_
- It does _not_ consume mental power when used. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06843))_
- Magic intensity is 15, and the effect _cannot_ be expanded at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06844))_
- 252 manual written in low ancient is included in its box, and if you chant a spell name in low ancient while tearing up the card, the magical power previously bestowed on the card will be released. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06844))_
- You can tell the effect of the card by the picture drawn on it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06846))_
- There are many other types of cards besides these (each game master should create them). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06850))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06839))_

> Rarity=13

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06840))_

> Enchanter=Void, "The Immoral Summoning Sorcerer"

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06841))_

> Form=Palm-sized card of unknown material Base Trade Price=Magic’s base mental power cost x 320 gamels

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06842))_

> Powers=User can use various ancient magic

### **[ Guard Glove ]**

- When the wearer grasps tightly while holding nothing in their hand, a force field is generated around his or her arm, which has the same effect as holding a shield. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06855))_
- Also, if you’re holding something in your dominant hand, you _cannot_ use spirit magic either. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06855))_
- You _cannot_ use ancient magic while one hand is clenched. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06855))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06852))_

> Rarity=12

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06853))_

> Form=Left hand (rarely right hand) glove Enchanter=Many

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06854))_

> Base Trade Price=1500 (Small shield)/5000 (Large shield) Powers=Generates a force field equivalent to a shield

### **[Candlestick of Infinite (Un)Luck]**

- If you light a candle, the fire will magically last for one week, but the candle’s fire itself offers no protection and will easily go out with a slight breeze, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06860))_
- Misfortunes that may befall you will be prevented in some way before they happen, and business or stock speculation will definitely yield a profit. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06860))_
- Then, if the candle’s fire in the candlestick goes out, that person will be struck by _every_ conceivable misfortune as a final result. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06860))_
- Even if that person relights the candle, this misfortune _cannot_ be gotten rid of. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06860))_
- If you light a candle in this candlestick (any normal candle on the market is fine), whoever lights it will be blessed with great luck. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06860))_
- Misfortunes that may befall you will be prevented in some way before they happen, and business or stock speculation will definitely yield a profit. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06860))_

### **[ Seal Stone ]**

- No memories will remain while the soul is sealed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06883))_
- Only one person’s soul can be sealed in at a time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06883))_

- which do not have souls. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06883))_
- It has no effect on undead, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06883))_
- Base Trade Price=480,000 gamels (not for sale) This stone’s magical power will appear when you aim it at an opponent and chant a password in low ancient. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06883))_
- Only one person’s soul can be sealed in at a time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06883))_
- If you hold the stone and chant the password of release, _or_ if you destroy the stone, the sealed soul will be released and return to its body. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06884))_

### **[ Scape Doll ]**

### Rarity=12

### Enchanter=Many

- A scape doll will break when its life force reaches 0. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06895))_
- If a character ties his or her hair to it and etches his or her name into it, then when he or she suffers physical damage, the scape doll’s life force will be reduced instead of that character’s life force. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06895))_
- The scape doll’s life force will be the same as the life force of the character who etched his or her name into it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06895))_
- If a character ties his or her hair to it and etches his or her name into it, then when he or she suffers physical damage, the scape doll’s life force will be reduced instead of that character’s life force. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06895))_
- However, if the scape doll itself suffers damage, the character’s own life force will decrease, rather than the scape doll’s life force. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06896))_
- Also, if you use multiple scape dolls, all the scape dolls will break at the same time, so it’s completely pointless. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06897))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06893))_

> Form=A wooden doll about 20 cm long Base Trade Price=90,000 gamels Powers=Suffers damage in place of the user

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06895))_

> At this time, if there is surplus damage, reduce the character's own life force.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06896))_

> Once you start using it, this effect cannot be canceled even if you untie your hair.

### **[ Statue of Changing ]**

### Rarity=14

### Enchanter=Unknown

- A shapeshifted form can return to his or her original state by either touching the snake statue or with _a final score_ 15 _Dispel Magic_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06901))_
- The animals you can shapeshift into by touching each statue are as follows. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06902))_
- Crocodile=Crocodile Bear=Grizzly Ape=Boss Ape immediately if they fail a mental power resistance roll against target score 16. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06902))_
- You’ll usually need to hide it with an eyepatch, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06902))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06902, source-range-870a16bc-06906))_

Crocodile=Crocodile Bear=Grizzly Ape=Boss Ape immediately if they fail a mental power resistance roll against target score 16. You’ll usually need to hide it with an eyepatch, etc. If _spirit magic_ is used around the wearer (used towards the wearer, or the wearer him or herself attempts to use spirit magic), this stone will seal the spirit, and the spirit magic is nullified as a result.

### **[ Zero Gravity Disc ]**

- If _spirit magic_ is used around the wearer (used towards the wearer, or the wearer him or herself attempts to use spirit magic), this stone will seal the spirit, and the spirit magic is nullified as a result. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06906))_

### Rarity=18

- This plate is made of a magical metal that completely negates gravity. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06909))_
- All objects placed on the plate lose their weight, and the plate itself is always floating. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06909))_
- Also, greater spirits _cannot_ be sealed, so spirit magic used _will_ take effect as-is, and at the same time the stone will _break_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06910))_
- However, if you try to block magic that uses a spirit with an average monster level of 5 or greater, the magic _itself_ will be blocked but the spirit will _not_ be sealed and the stone will _break_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06910))_
- If you push it up towards the sky, it’ll fly anywhere as-is. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06911))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06906))_

> If _spirit magic_ is used around the wearer (used towards the wearer, or the wearer him or herself attempts to use spirit magic), this stone will seal the spirit, and the spirit magic is nullified as a result.

### Form=Iron jar

- Anyone who sees this eye (even if it’s an image reflected in a mirror) will fall asleep _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06926))_
- Base Trade Price=1,000,000 gamels (not for sale) This is used by transplanting it into a body in place of a lost eye. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06926))_
- When you chant a password, a daemon will be summoned from the jar. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06928))_
- If the summoner is gone, the daemon, according to its intrinsic nature, will begin to act violently. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-06932))_

## Chapter 15: Experience Points

## 15.1 Four Types of Experience Points

- If you were to become one of the thieves' transport partners without realizing they’re stolen goods, your mission would _not_ be accomplished. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07037))_
- In _Sword World RPG_ , there are four ways to gain experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07038))_
- These are **accomplishing missions** , **fighting monsters** , **success rolls** , and **special bonuses** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07038))_
- If each character achieves the goal (mission) set by the game master, they will each receive **1000** experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07039))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07039))_

> If they _cannot_ achieve it, they will only get **500** points.

### **Accomplishing Missions**

- Most experience points are earned by accomplishing missions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07041))_
- If you're going into ruins to search for treasure, the goal is probably the treasure hidden there, and if you're rescuing a captured dignitary, it’s to bring that person back safely. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07041))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07042))_

> If the game master deems it to be an incomplete success, but not _exactly_ a failure, he or she may decide to award experience points somewhere in between, such as 700 or 800.

### **Fighting Monsters**

- When you achieve a goal set by the game master, you will receive experience points for _accomplishing missions_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07044))_
- You can also earn experience points by defeating monsters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07045))_
- **Monster level x 10 ÷ number of adventurers** experience points will be awarded for each monster. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07045))_
- At this time, the mission will only be accomplished if it's returned to its rightful owner. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07046))_
- Upon closer examination, a request to transport an item may turn out to be stolen goods, and in a change of situation, must be returned to its rightful owner. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07046))_
- This may be different from what it appears to be upon entering the scenario. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07046))_
- At this time, the mission will only be accomplished if it's returned to its rightful owner. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07046))_

### **Success Rolls**

- This depends on how much work the adventurers did during the adventure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07048))_
- How assertively the adventurers acted is reflected by the number of dice rolls made by the players. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07048))_
- Also, experience points should _not_ be awarded for meaningless success rolls. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07049))_
- Double ones when making checks for _damage dealt in combat_ or _armor defense effects_ in combat should _also_ be added to the number of times. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07049))_
- However, _double ones after a critical_ during damage checks are _not_ included. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07049))_
- The adventurers will earn **number of double ones x 10** experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07049))_
- Unlike the previous two types, experience points earned in this way will _vary_ depending on the adventurer. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07049))_
- However, it can be difficult to remember every time the dice were rolled, so only the number of times a player rolled _double ones during a success roll_ should be recorded. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07049))_
- Unlike the previous two types, experience points earned in this way will _vary_ depending on the adventurer. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07049))_
- However, _double ones after a critical_ during damage checks are _not_ included. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07049))_
- However, it can be difficult to remember every time the dice were rolled, so only the number of times a player rolled _double ones during a success roll_ should be recorded. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07049))_

### **Special Bonuses**

- It'd be most appropriate to keep it to about **1/10th** of the experience points earned from accomplishing a mission. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07051))_
- Giving away a large amount of experience points is harmful to the enjoyment of the game. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07051))_
- However, this should be done as _sparingly_ as possible. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07051))_
- The game master is free to award bonus experience for particularly outstanding play. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07051))_

## Part 3: Optional Rules

## Chapter 16: Optional Rules for Combat

## 16.1 Combat in Which Monsters Roll Dice

- In _Sword World RPG_ , the monster side (game master) rarely rolls dice during combat. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07062))_
- This reduces the burden on the game master and allows him or her to focus on other parts. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07062))_
- However, some game masters may also want to roll the dice. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07063))_
- In an emergency, the game master can control the results of his or her own dice, making it easier to weave a story. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07064))_
- This is because these advantages exist. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07065))_
- Here we'll introduce the procedure by which the game master also rolls the dice and performs combat. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07065))_
- This is because these advantages exist. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07065))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07065))_

> This is because these advantages exist. Here we'll introduce the procedure by which the game master also rolls the dice and performs combat.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07063))_

> When both sides roll the dice, the range of numbers will expand and the enjoyment can increase.

### **Attacks From Monsters Against Characters**

- A score equal to a monster’s **attack points - 7** is written next to it in parenthesis. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07067))_
- This is the target score for a character to evade the monster’s attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07067))_
- Also, if the game master rolls double ones or double sixes, that does _not_ automatically result in failure/success (but for a _character_ , it still _does_ ). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07068))_
- Therefore, a tie would result in _character's evasion success = monster's attack failure_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07068))_
- As you can see from the way the dice are rolled, this is the process for a success roll with _monster's attack points -7_ as the _difficulty_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07068))_
- Therefore, a tie would result in _character's evasion success = monster's attack failure_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07068))_
- The monster's _strike points_ are fixed, and the difference is based on protection from the character's armor. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07069))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07068))_

> As you can see from the way the dice are rolled, this is the process for a success roll with _monster's attack points -7_ as the _difficulty_ . Therefore, a tie would result in _character's evasion success = monster's attack failure_ . Also, if the game master rolls double ones or double sixes, that does _not_ automatically result in failure/success (but for a _character_ , it still _does_ ).

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07067))_

> The game master should roll 2D and add it to the number in parenthesis.

### **Attacks From Characters Against Monsters**

- A score equal to a monster’s **evasion points - 7** is written next to it in parenthesis. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07071))_
- This is the target score for a character to hit the monster with an attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07071))_
- Please remember that the game master should be considered _first_ to roll the dice. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07072))_
- Therefore, a tie would result in _character's attack success = monster's evasion failure_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07072))_
- As in the previous section, this is the process for a success roll with _monster's evasion points -7_ as the _difficulty_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07072))_
- Therefore, a tie would result in _character's attack success = monster's evasion failure_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07072))_
- A monster's _defense points_ are fixed, and the difference is based on the character's strike roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07073))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07068, source-range-870a16bc-07072))_

> As you can see from the way the dice are rolled, this is the process for a success roll with _monster's attack points -7_ as the _difficulty_ . Therefore, a tie would result in _character's evasion success = monster's attack failure_ . Also, if the game master rolls double ones or double sixes, that does _not_ automatically result in failure/success (but for a _character_ , it still _does_ ). ## 16.2 Weapon and Armor Modifiers

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07080))_

> In the basic rules, we did not consider the finer differences based on the shape of weapons and armor, but even though the required strength is the same, swords, axes, spears, and hammers differ in ease of use and power. This rule quantifies the finer differences between weapons and armor.

### **Weapon Modifiers**

- The terms used on this table are explained below. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07083))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07080, source-range-870a16bc-07083))_

> In the basic rules, we did not consider the finer differences based on the shape of weapons and armor, but even though the required strength is the same, swords, axes, spears, and hammers differ in ease of use and power. This rule quantifies the finer differences between weapons and armor. The terms used on this table are explained below.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07082))_

> Here is a table of weapons commonly used by adventurers (" _Table 16-1 Weapon Optional Rules_ ").

### **Attack Power Modifier**

- **Attack power modifier** indicates the modifier added to an adventurer's attack power when using that weapon. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07085))_
- Weapons with higher numbers are more likely to hit an opponent, while weapons with smaller numbers are less likely to hit. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07085))_

### **Strike Power Modifier**

- In the basic rules, a weapon's required strength _is_ its strike power, but with this rule, a weapon's strike power equals **weapon's required strength plus this strike power modifier** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07087))_
- **Strike power modifier** is a modifier added to a weapon’s _strike power_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07087))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07085))_

> **Attack power modifier** indicates the modifier added to an adventurer's attack power when using that weapon. Weapons with higher numbers are more likely to hit an opponent, while weapons with smaller numbers are less likely to hit.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07087))_

> In other words, even if the required strength is the _same_ , one can say a weapon with a _higher_ strike power modifier will deal _greater_ damage to an opponent.

- ***The lance itself has a +5 strike power modifier, +10 during a charge, and +2 damage itself _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07094))_

## 16.3 Projectile Bonus Damage

- likely you are to deliver a powerful strike. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07099))_
- Weapons classified as _swords_ , _axes_ , _spears_ , _bows_ , and _crossbows_ suffer +1 to this critical target against _metal armor_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07099))_
- In other words, these sharp weapons are less likely to deliver a powerful strike due to the armor’s metal. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07100))_
- In the basic rules, any weapon’s bonus damage equals _fighter (or thief, ranger) skill level + strength bonus_ , but for _projectiles_ , an adventurer’s strength is not fully utilized as-is. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07126))_

### **Number of Projectile Shots**

- on the ground and use them as bullets, it _must_ be every _other_ round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07103))_
- However, if you leave it set for a long time, the string will lose its elasticity, and the quarrel will easily come off if you tip the crossbow. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07104))_
- A crossbow can _only_ shoot every _other_ round due to the time it takes to wind them up. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07104))_
- But if _not_ , they’ll take _one_ round to prepare and can only be thrown every _other_ round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07105))_
- But if _not_ , they’ll take _one_ round to prepare and can only be thrown every _other_ round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07105))_
- A bow can be fired _every_ round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07106))_
- However, this is _only_ if you have arrows ready in your quiver. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07106))_

### **Evasion Modifier**

### **Shield Modifiers**

- Heavy metal armor slows down your movement and makes it more difficult to dodge your opponent's attacks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07112))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07115))_

> If you ready a shield, it’ll be easier to avoid your opponent's attacks. With this rule, if you ready a _large_ shield, your _evasion speed_ will be +2. However, if you do use a large shield, it’s inevitable that you’ll have to neglect your attacks. Therefore, when using a large shield, you will suffer a -1 penalty to your _attack power_ . However, large shields with a _magical power_ of +1 or greater do _not_ have this penalty.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07113))_

> With this optional rule, there are differences in the way small and large shields are handled ( _Table 16-2: Shield Optional Rules_ ).

### **Defense Modifier**

- With this rule, if you ready a _large_ shield, your _evasion speed_ will be +2. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07115))_
- However, large shields with a _magical power_ of +1 or greater do _not_ have this penalty. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07115))_
- If you ready a shield, it’ll be easier to avoid your opponent's attacks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07115))_
- Therefore, when using a large shield, you will suffer a -1 penalty to your _attack power_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07115))_
- However, if you do use a large shield, it’s inevitable that you’ll have to neglect your attacks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07115))_
- Therefore, when using a large shield, you will suffer a -1 penalty to your _attack power_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07115))_
- Metal armor generally has a higher defensive effect and grants a bonus to defense. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07116))_
- **Defense power modifier** is the **defense power bonus** you receive while wearing that armor. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07116))_

### **Critical Target Variation**

- If this field says _yes_ , the _opponent's_ critical target will be +1 when they attack you with a sword, spear, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07118))_
- **Critical target variation** indicates that the armor _reduces_ the chance of being dealt a strong blow from some weapons while wearing it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07118))_
- **Anti-Strangling Protection** The _anti-strangling protection_ indicates whether the armor protects you when being strangled by a garrote or arms around the neck. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07121))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07119))_

## 16.5 Combat Options

### **16.4**

### **Projectile Range Extension**

- In the basic rules, the range limit is 20 meters for throwing weapons and 100 meters for shooting weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07132))_
- In the basic rules, the range limit is 20 meters for throwing weapons and 100 meters for shooting weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07132))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07132))_

> If you follow these optional rules, you can extend these even further.

### **Long Distance Modifiers**

- Hitting a distant target with a weapon is a nigh-impossible task. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07134))_
- Throwing weapons have a **-1 modifier to attack power for every meter beyond 20 meters** , and shooting weapons have the same for **every 5 meters beyond 100** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07134))_

### **Maximum Range**

- Each throwing or shooting weapon has a **maximum range** , and they will _never_ hit a target farther away than this ( _Table 16-4: Maximum Ranges_ ). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07136))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07137))_

> **Table 16-4 Maximum Rang**

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07138))_

|**Table 16-4 Maximum Ranges**||
|---|---|
|Dagger|30|
|Hand Ax|20 + Character's Strength|
|Javelin|30 + Character's Strength x 3|
|Short Spear|25 + Character's Strength x 2|
|Trident|20 + Character's Strength x 2|
|Boomerang|20 + (Character's Strength - Required Strength) x 2|
|Club|20 + (Character's Strength - Required Strength) x 2|
|Shortbow|100 + Required Strength x 5|
|Longbow|120 + Required Strength x 5|
|Light Crossbow|80 + Required Strength x 5|
|Heavy Crossbow|100 + Required Strength x 5|
|Sling|100|
|Staff Sling|100|

|Net|5|
|Bola|20 + (Character's Strength - Required Strength x 3|
|Light Cranequin Crossbow|80 + Required Strength x 10|
|Heavy Cranequin Crossbow|100 + Required Strength x 10|
|Ballista|150 + Required Strength x 10|
|Prod|100|
|Dart|20|

### **16.5**

### **Combat Options**

- These optional rules allow for a variety of attack methods, rather than just simple attacks _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07141))_

### **Full Defense**

- In return, you’ll gain a +2 bonus to your _evasion speed_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07143))_
- When focusing on defense, an adventurer _cannot_ attack or use any magic during that round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07143))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07143))_

> When an opponent is too strong, and fighting normally is of no use, an adventurer can **focus wholly on defense** .

### **Strong Blow**

- When the opponent is unable to evade, or the adventurer is cornered and there is no point in defending, the adventurer can use all his or her attacks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07146))_
- At this time, you can choose one of the following. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07147))_
- This action can be performed in combination with the _mow down_ , _ram_ , and _area aim_ actions (all described below). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07149))_
- This is because it's automatically assumed to be a strong blow. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07152))_
- This is because it's automatically assumed to be a strong blow. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07152))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07143))_

> When an opponent is too strong, and fighting normally is of no use, an adventurer can **focus wholly on defense** . When focusing on defense, an adventurer _cannot_ attack or use any magic during that round. In return, you’ll gain a +2 bonus to your _evasion speed_ .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07145))_

> When a foe cannot evade, or if they're so cornered they have no choice but to defend, an adventurer can put everything they have into their attack.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07149, source-range-870a16bc-07152))_

> This action can be performed in combination with the _mow down_ , _ram_ , and _area aim_ actions (all described below). When using this optional rule, ignore the _critical target -1 when attacking a sleeping or defenseless target_ rule (see p. 139). This is because it's automatically assumed to be a strong blow. You can choose _critical target -1_ , or you can choose _bonus damage +2_ instead.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07150))_

> You cannot declare _mercy_ (of course!).

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07150, source-range-870a16bc-07152))_

> You cannot declare _mercy_ (of course!). When using this optional rule, ignore the _critical target -1 when attacking a sleeping or defenseless target_ rule (see p. 139). This is because it's automatically assumed to be a strong blow. You can choose _critical target -1_ , or you can choose _bonus damage +2_ instead.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07151))_

> However, if you’re attacked during the round in which you took this action, your _evasion speed_ will be reduced by -4.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07150))_

> You cannot declare _mercy_ (of course!).

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07152))_

> You can choose _critical target -1_ , or you can choose _bonus damage +2_ instead.

### **Charge**

- When an adventurer is armed with a spear, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07154))_
- he or she can **charge** an opponent and use his or her weight and 264 speed to deal great damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07154))_
- Weapons that can perform a _charge_ are limited to spears, pole weapons (which can _thrust_ , see below), and lances. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07155))_
- Weapons that can perform a _charge_ are limited to spears, pole weapons (which can _thrust_ , see below), and lances. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07155))_
- First there must be a _margin_ of _**weapon length (requires strength x 15 cm) x 2**_ **to** _**adventurer’s agility x 3**_ meters between the adventurer and the opponent. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07156))_
- The hit check for the charge is made at the _end_ of the round (at action order 0). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07157))_
- If you’re attacked by a projectile in the middle of a charge, your evasion against it _always_ suffers a -4 penalty. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07157))_
- However, if the one _being_ charged attempts to counterattack the one _performing_ the charge, his or her action will be _delayed_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07157))_
- There are _no_ score modifiers to the _hit check_ of a charge. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07158))_
- If you charged, you’ll suffer a -4 penalty to your evasion when dodging this attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07159))_
- _Regardless_ of whether the charge was a hit or a failure, the opponent _can_ attack at the _end of the round_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07159))_
- Of course, this is _not_ possible if you’re taking a different action, such as casting magic, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07159))_
- Of course, this is _not_ possible if you’re taking a different action, such as casting magic, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07159))_
- A weapon’s length is proportional to its _required strength_ , so make your hit checks based on the weapon with the _greater_ required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07160))_
- In this case, attack order is determined by the weapons’ _length_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07160))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07152))_

> When using this optional rule, ignore the _critical target -1 when attacking a sleeping or defenseless target_ rule (see p. 139). This is because it's automatically assumed to be a strong blow. You can choose _critical target -1_ , or you can choose _bonus damage +2_ instead.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07154))_

> Since you can attack and move at the same time, you’ll also be able to attack from a slightly distant position (you can attack while performing _full movement_ ).

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07154))_

> When an adventurer is armed with a spear, etc. he or she can **charge** an opponent and use his or her weight and 264 speed to deal great damage. Since you can attack and move at the same time, you’ll also be able to attack from a slightly distant position (you can attack while performing _full movement_ ). This is resolved as follows.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07156))_

> If you’re any closer than this, it's not possible to charge, and if you’re any further away, you cannot reach your opponent.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07158))_

> If it hits, add +10 to strike power when determining damage.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07159))_

> An opponent who’s struck by a charge can take a counterattack action in response.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07159))_

> An opponent who’s struck by a charge can take a counterattack action in response. _Regardless_ of whether the charge was a hit or a failure, the opponent _can_ attack at the _end of the round_ . If you charged, you’ll suffer a -4 penalty to your evasion when dodging this attack. Of course, this is _not_ possible if you’re taking a different action, such as casting magic, etc.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07160))_

> If your opponent's weapon is a spear, etc., you may _both_ charge each other - this includes cases where you’re waiting.

### **Snipe**

- You may spend up to **4** rounds aiming and the critical target may go as low as **-4** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07163))_
- In this case, you will _not_ gain a bonus to your hit check using this method (since your opponent is not aware of you, your _opponent_ suffers an automatic -4 penalty to _evasion_ ). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07163))_
- However, this critical target reduction only applies to the _first_ strike roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07164))_
- From the _second roll onward_ after a critical, the critical target will be _as normal_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07164))_
- From the _second roll onward_ after a critical, the critical target will be _as normal_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07164))_
- However, this critical target reduction only applies to the _first_ strike roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07164))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07159))_

> An opponent who’s struck by a charge can take a counterattack action in response. _Regardless_ of whether the charge was a hit or a failure, the opponent _can_ attack at the _end of the round_ . If you charged, you’ll suffer a -4 penalty to your evasion when dodging this attack. Of course, this is _not_ possible if you’re taking a different action, such as casting magic, etc.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07162))_

> Bows and crossbows can be **aimed and fired at a vital point** if the target is completely unaware and not moving around much (they _cannot_ be aimed and fired if the target is aware of the sniper's presence and is alert to their surroundings, or if they're fervently moving around).

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07163))_

> In this case, if you spend one round aiming, you can reduce the _critical target_ by 1 if the shot hits.

### Example:

- There's a guard standing at the entrance, but he doesn't seem to notice Ducard II. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07166))_
- Ducard II makes a strike roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07167))_
- He _can_ continue to roll to increase the damage, but from here on the critical target will be determined as the normal _10_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07167))_
- However, while _aiming_ , there’s a chance that your opponent will sense your presence. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07168))_
- You must make a success roll each round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07168))_
- If you fail, your opponent will notice you, you’ll _no longer_ receive bonuses from aiming, and you’ll also _miss_ the chance for a _surprise attack_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07169))_
- The _difficulty_ is the opponent's _monster level_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07169))_
- If the target’s final score is greater, or if they’re equal, the sniper’s presence will be noticed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07170))_
- The target must make a success roll using _ranger skill level + intelligence bonus_ as the baseline score, and compare his or her final score with that of the sniper’s _hide_ / _camouflage_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07170))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07164))_

> However, this critical target reduction only applies to the _first_ strike roll. From the _second roll onward_ after a critical, the critical target will be _as normal_ .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07166))_

> If it hits, the critical target will be reduced by -3, making it 7.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07164))_

> However, this critical target reduction only applies to the _first_ strike roll. From the _second roll onward_ after a critical, the critical target will be _as normal_ .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07167))_

> If he rolls a 10 or greater, more damage will be added, but if he rolls a _7, 8 or 9_ , the strike roll will end there.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07167))_

> Ducard II makes a strike roll. It's an 8. That's a _critical_ . He _can_ continue to roll to increase the damage, but from here on the critical target will be determined as the normal _10_ . If he rolls a 10 or greater, more damage will be added, but if he rolls a _7, 8 or 9_ , the strike roll will end there.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07168))_

> To prevent this, you can use the thief skill’s _hide_ ability or the ranger skill’s _camouflage_ ability.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07168))_

> However, while _aiming_ , there’s a chance that your opponent will sense your presence. To prevent this, you can use the thief skill’s _hide_ ability or the ranger skill’s _camouflage_ ability. You must make a success roll each round.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07170))_

> When your opponent is a character, he or she can notice you by using the _sense danger_ ability.

### **Mow Down**

- When there are multiple enemies in front of you, you can swing your weapon horizontally and **attack them all at once** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07173))_
- If it hits, damage is determined as normal. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07174))_
- It’ll be _-2_ if you attack _two_ at a time, _-3_ if you attack _three_ at the same time, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07174))_
- When taking on this attack, the _number of enemies you target at once_ will equal a penalty to your attack power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07174))_
- Usually there won’t be more than three. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07175))_
- If you do decide to mow down, you _must_ attack _all_ enemies in front of you. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07175))_
- _Mow down_ can only be performed against enemies in _front_ of you. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07175))_
- For example, if there are _three_ , you _cannot_ attack only _two_ of them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07175))_
- For example, if there are _three_ , you _cannot_ attack only _two_ of them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07175))_
- _Mow down_ can only be performed against enemies in _front_ of you. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07175))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07171))_

> Please note that with other projectiles such as slings, etc. you _cannot_ reduce the critical target by aiming.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07173))_

> A character can only do this if he or she has a two-handed weapon and it’s the type that cuts (sword, ax, pole weapon that can _cut_ ).

### **Grapple**

- This action is like wrestling, in which you attempt to fight by grappling with your opponent. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07177))_
- Then, any attempt to grapple will automatically occur at the _end_ of the round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07178))_
- To attempt a **grapple** , you must first _not_ be holding any large weapons or shields in your hands. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07178))_
- Then, any attempt to grapple will automatically occur at the _end_ of the round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07178))_
- If you succeed (and your 265 opponent fails to evade), you’ll enter a grapple. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07179))_
- When grappling, both sides automatically become _prone_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07180))_
- Large weapons must be kept out of hand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07180))_
- Actions that characters in a grapple can take are limited to the following. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07181))_
- Actions that characters in a grapple can take are limited to the following. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07181))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07179))_

> Anyone who attempts to grapple will suffer a -2 penalty to his or her attack power, then must make a hit check for their attack.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07180))_

> Once you’ve entered a grapple, the _only_ weapons you can use are daggers and garrotes (described below).

### **1. Normal Attack**

- Opponents have a -2 penalty to evasion. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07183))_
- Attack with a dagger, barehanded (claws, talons), cestus, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07183))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07181))_

> Actions that characters in a grapple can take are limited to the following.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07183))_

> If a monster has fangs, it can also bite.

### **2. Strangle/Continue to Strangle/Escape**

- If you’re barehanded, strike power is 0 (critical target 12), and if you’re using a garrote, strike power is 10 (critical target 10). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07185))_
- Opponents have a -2 penalty to evasion. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07185))_
- **Strangle** your opponent barehanded or with a garrote. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07185))_
- Once strangling, the strangler can automatically deal damage every round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07185))_
- If the final score of the one trying to break free is greater, or if there’s a tie, he or she will escape. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07186))_
- While being strangled, you cannot make any sound at all, so you _cannot_ use magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07186))_
- To resolve breaking free, the one being strangled must make a success roll using _adventurer level + strength bonus_ as the baseline score, while the strangler must make one using _fighter/thief skill level + strength bonus_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07186))_
- Also, even if your character doesn’t suffer damage, if or she remains strangled for a _number of rounds equal to his or her life force_ , life force will immediately fall to 0, he or she will fall _unconscious_ , and you’ll need to make a _death check_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07187))_
- You _must_ make a death check _every_ round so long as the strangling continues, even if you do _not_ suffer any damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07188))_
- However, you _cannot_ attack monsters that _don’t breathe_ (undead, etc.), monsters that are _much larger than your character_ (ogres, etc.), or those that wear _metal armor_ , by strangling. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07189))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07185))_

> Only _adventurer level_ can reduce strangling damage.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07185))_

> **Strangle** your opponent barehanded or with a garrote. Opponents have a -2 penalty to evasion. Once strangling, the strangler can automatically deal damage every round. If you’re barehanded, strike power is 0 (critical target 12), and if you’re using a garrote, strike power is 10 (critical target 10). Only _adventurer level_ can reduce strangling damage.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07186))_

> If you’re being strangled, the _only_ thing you can do in the rounds onward is attempt to break free.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07186))_

> If you’re being strangled, the _only_ thing you can do in the rounds onward is attempt to break free. To resolve breaking free, the one being strangled must make a success roll using _adventurer level + strength bonus_ as the baseline score, while the strangler must make one using _fighter/thief skill level + strength bonus_ . If the final score of the one trying to break free is greater, or if there’s a tie, he or she will escape. While being strangled, you cannot make any sound at all, so you 

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07189))_

> You cannot deal damage to these, nor can you suffocate them.

### **3. Pin Down**

- If the pinner’s is greater, the pin is successful. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07191))_
- However, the one being pinned can attempt to break free. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07192))_
- At this time, please make the same check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07192))_
- Once pinning, the pinner will automatically continue in that state and can perform normal attacks (of course, weapons are restricted). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07192))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07191))_

> To resolve this, the one attempting to pin down must make a success roll using _fighter/thief skill level + strength bonus_ as the baseline score, while the one resisting must make one using _adventurer level + strength bonus_ .

### **4. Using Holy Magic or Common Runes**

- It’s _not_ possible while being _strangled_ , but if you’re merely being _pinned down_ , you _can_ use holy magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07194))_
- Common runes require you to not only be able to speak, but to _also_ be able to use a catalyst. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07194))_
- It's usually a ring, so you _can_ use it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07194))_
- Common runes require you to not only be able to speak, but to _also_ be able to use a catalyst. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07194))_
- Ancient magic, spirit magic, and spellsongs _cannot_ be used. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07195))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07191))_

> Use your strength and weight to **pin down** your opponent. To resolve this, the one attempting to pin down must make a success roll using _fighter/thief skill level + strength bonus_ as the baseline score, while the one resisting must make one using _adventurer level + strength bonus_ . If the pinner’s is greater, the pin is successful.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07194))_

> Even while grappling, you can use holy magic so long as you can speak.

### **5. Escape from Grapple**

- Both sides will fall _prone_ , with a short distance between them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07197))_
- To escape from a grapple, make a success roll check using _adventurer level + agility bonus_ as the baseline score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07197))_
- Weapons you dropped when entering the grapple will probably be lying around nearby. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07197))_
- An opponent who is trying to maintain the grapple may counter this by making one using _adventurer level + agility bonus_ _**or** adventure level + strength bonus_ as the baseline score, whichever is greater. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07197))_
- If the one trying to escape’s final score is greater, or if there’s a tie, the grapple will be broken. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07197))_
- Those being strangled or pinned down _cannot_ attempt to escape from a grapple unless they _first_ escape from _those_ conditions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07198))_
- If the strangler or pinner attempts to escape, he or she will automatically succeed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07198))_
- Those being strangled or pinned down _cannot_ attempt to escape from a grapple unless they _first_ escape from _those_ conditions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07198))_
- For adventurers who rely on weapons, this may pose a threat. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07199))_
- It’s fine for the game master to have certain monsters attempt to grapple. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07199))_
- Of course, a monster that does not have hands cannot _strangle_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07199))_

## 16.8 Weapons and Armor That Are Too Heavy

### **16.6**

### **Holding Weapons in Both Hands**

- In the basic rules, characters cannot hold separate weapons in each hand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07237))_
- This section presents extended rules for **holding weapons in both hands** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07237))_
- However, there are many people who admire dual wielding. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07237))_

### **16.6.1 Character's Dominant Hand**

- Characters are generally righthanded or left-handed, but in actuality there are also people who are _ambidextrous_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07239))_
- Left-handed or right-handedness is decided by the player's preference. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07240))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07239))_

> Therefore, when creating an adventurer, let's say that if you roll 2D and get _double sixes_ , they'll be ambidextrous.

### **16.6.2**

### **Two-Handed Attacks**

- If a character has weapons in both hands, he or she has three options for attacking. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07243))_

### **Attack with both hands at the same**

- There’s _no_ need to reduce one to a quarter. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07246))_
- Also, the required strength for _one_ of the weapons is limited to _half your own strength (rounded up)_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07246))_
- Also, the required strength for _one_ of the weapons is limited to _half your own strength (rounded up)_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07246))_
- When a character is ambidextrous, there is _no_ opposite hand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07247))_
- If you attack with only _one_ hand, there is _no_ penalty, and if you attack with _both_ hands, the penalty is only _-2_ for each. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07247))_
- If you attack with only _one_ hand, there is _no_ penalty, and if you attack with _both_ hands, the penalty is only _-2_ for each. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07247))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07246))_

> If you’re fighting with the thief skill, the required strength for _both_ weapons is only _half_ the character's strength.

### **16.7**

### **Throwing Multiple Darts or Daggers**

- All objects thrown at the same time must have the _same weight_ (required strength). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07251))_
- The _number_ thrown at the same time becomes a _penalty_ to attack power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07253))_
- There must _always_ be _one_ target. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07254))_
- This optional rule increases the chance that a high-level character will defeat a low-level character or monster in one blow. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07255))_
- Never forget the limitation of projectiles: they _cannot_ be used while in an _engagement_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07256))_
- Never forget the limitation of projectiles: they _cannot_ be used while in an _engagement_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07256))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07250))_

> It’s possible to throw **multiple** darts or daggers at once. However, the following restrictions occur in this case:

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07252))_

> The _total required strength_ must be up to the _character's strength_ (or _half_ that if it's the thief skill).

### **16.8**

### **Weapons and Armor That Are Too Heavy**

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07259))_

> A Character can also use weapons and armor whose required strength **exceeds** his or her own strength.

### **If a Weapon or Shield’s Required Strength Exceeds Your Strength**

- If one in _one hand_ is too heavy, you will receive a penalty, but there will be _no_ stacking. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07261))_
- For each point of required strength, reduce _attack power_ by -1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07261))_
- For example, if your sword is 3 too heavy in required strength and your shield is 2 too heavy in required strength, your penalty is **-3** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07261))_
- For example, if your sword is 3 too heavy in required strength and your shield is 2 too heavy in required strength, your penalty is **-3** . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07261))_

### **If Armor’s Required Strength Exceeds Your Strength**

- At the game master's decision, any success roll that uses _agility bonus_ as a baseline score may be subject to an _equal_ penalty _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07263))_
- For each point of required strength, reduce _evasion speed_ by 1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07263))_

## 16.9 Special Weapons

- There are halberds, pole axes, lancers, partisans, glaives, pikes...... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07268))_
- **Pole Weapons** A pole weapon is a general term for a two-handed weapon that has a long handle with a metal blade attached to the end. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07268))_
- If he or she fails, they will be swept off their feet and fall _prone_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07269))_
- Players should examine which attack methods they can use with each pole weapon (for example, a halberd can be used in four ways: _thrust_ , _cut_ , _stab_ , and _sweep_ ) and arm themselves accordingly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07270))_
- Players should examine which attack methods they can use with each pole weapon (for example, a halberd can be used in four ways: _thrust_ , _cut_ , _stab_ , and _sweep_ ) and arm themselves accordingly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07270))_
- The ways to use these pole weapons are as follows. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07271))_
- Pole weapons have the same basic idea of attacking the enemy from a distance, but their functions are specialized in various ways. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07271))_
- Below are some typical pole weapons and how to use them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07272))_

### **Thrust**

- Its tip is sharply pointed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07274))_
- You can treat these the same as spears. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07274))_
- Please use _spear/2H_ for attack power modifiers, strike power modifiers, critical target, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07274))_

### **Pole Ax**

- It can be used in two ways: _thrust_ and _cut_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07276))_

### **Halberd**

- It’s the most sophisticated pole weapon, and can be used to _thrust_ , _cut_ , _stab_ , and _sweep_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07279))_
- It has an ax-like blade. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07280))_
- Its scores and handling are based on _warhammer/2H_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07281))_
- It _cannot_ be used to declare _mercy_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07282))_
- Please use the same scores and handling as _flail/2H_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07282))_
- It has a hammer-like tip. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07282))_

### **Lucerne Hammer**

- This weapon can be used to _thrust_ , _cut_ and _beat_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07284))_
- On the other side, there’s a hookshaped protrusion. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07284))_
- The lucerne hammer has a similar shape to a halberd, but the tip of the spear is longer than a halberd, and the striking part is a hammer rather than an ax. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07284))_
- The strike power _itself_ is 0-- this attack method is not suitable for dealing damage), and there are _no_ criticals. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07285))_
- It allows you to sweep your opponent by hooking their foot. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07285))_
- The glaive is a weapon with a knifelike blade attached to the end of a long handle. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07287))_
- This weapon can be used in two ways: _thrust_ and _cut_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07287))_
- Pole weapons cannot reach their full power in tight spaces. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07290))_
- It would not be a good idea to bring them into underground labyrinths, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07290))_
- If you don't have a space with this radius, you _cannot_ use attack methods other than _thrust_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07290))_
- The length of a pole weapon is **required strength x 15** centimeters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07290))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07288))_

> If the attack is successful, the opponent suffers damage and at the same time must make a success roll using _adventurer level + dexterity bonus_ ( _evasion points -7_ for monsters) as the baseline score and the _attacker's final_

### **Main-gauche/Sword Breaker**

- You'll gain a +1 bonus to evasion speed _only_ if your opponent's weapon is a _dagger_ , _rapier_ , or _short sword_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07292))_
- A **main-gauche** is a dagger held in the left hand (right hand for left-handed adventurers) for parrying. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07292))_
- Of course, your opponent's weapons are limited to _daggers_ , _rapiers_ , and _short swords_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07293))_
- It has a similar bonus to evasion speed, and moreover, if used skillfully, can _break_ or _drop_ an opponent's weapon. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07293))_
- A **sword breaker** is a type of maingauche with serrated blades lined up along the back. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07293))_
- If you roll an _11_ , and you _evade_ your opponent's attack, you can make your opponent _drop_ his or her weapon. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07293))_
- Of course, your opponent's weapons are limited to _daggers_ , _rapiers_ , and _short swords_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07293))_
- Main-gauche and sword breaker have the _same_ attack scores as a _dagger_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07294))_
- Also, if you use it for an attack, you’ll _lose_ the evasion bonus. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07294))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07290))_

> Pole weapons cannot reach their full power in tight spaces. The length of a pole weapon is **required strength x 15** centimeters. If you don't have a space with this radius, you _cannot_ use attack methods other than _thrust_ . It would not be a good idea to bring them into underground labyrinths, etc.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07293))_

> When you roll 2D for evasion, if you get _double sixes_ , you can _break_ your opponent's weapon.

### **Whip, Net, Bola**

- If an attack with one of these weapons hits, the evader must make a success roll using _adventurer level + agility bonus_ as the baseline score and _attacker's final score at this time_ as the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07296))_
- If he or she fails, they’ll be _entangled_ and fall _prone_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07297))_
- While entangled, there is _always_ a -4 penalty evading your opponent's attacks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07300))_
- Once entangled, an escape check is required _every_ round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07300))_
- If the entangled target has hands or similar organs, the target score will _decrease_ by 1 each round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07300))_
- To escape, you must first _release_ any armaments you’re holding. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07300))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07294))_

> Main-gauche and sword breaker have the _same_ attack scores as a _dagger_ . When attacking with _both_ hands, you’ll still suffer a -2 ／ -4 penalty. Also, if you use it for an attack, you’ll _lose_ the evasion bonus.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07296))_

> These weapons can perform attacks that **entangle** opponents.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07296))_

> These weapons can perform attacks that **entangle** opponents. If an attack with one of these weapons hits, the evader must make a success roll using _adventurer level + agility bonus_ as the baseline score and _attacker's final score at this time_ as the target score.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07299))_

> If the opponent is of a _larger_ race than him or her, the attacker must _release_ the whip or net.

### **Whip**

- At this time, roll 2D, and it'll _only_ entangle the opponent if you roll _double ones_ . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07302))_
- If the attack hits and the opponent is _not_ entangled, the whip will _return_ to the attacker's hand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07302))_
- However, its range is limited to 5 meters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-870a16bc-07303))_
